reviews.title: Fantastic fit, design and great value
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase These AirPods are my absolute favorite. I love these because unlike some Pro models, they don't come with foam or rubber earpieces which to me, are not very comfortable. These AirPods fit perfectly, fully compatible with all Apple devices, quality and durability is what one expects from Apple. Audio quality and features such as noise cancellation and adaptive audio are second to none. I also love the wireless charging case with speaker, so when I put it on a wireless charger, I get a chime telling me it's charging. Also in the latest released of all OS's, my devices tell me when the AirPods are fully charged. I'm very happy with these AirPods.

reviews.title: Absolutely amazing noise cancellation!
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase My original AirPods were from pre-2020 I think? They were still working technically, but they weren’t really holding a charge anymore and I had replaced the charging unit back in 2021, so it felt like instead of replacing that again maybe it was best to just get a new pair. I can’t even express how IMPRESSED I was with the noise cancellation! It’s honestly amazing coming from someone who has not had noise canceling headphones. The adaptive learning feature is also so cool - there’s several features but I like the one that listen to hear if there’s a conversation going and then it lowers the audio you’re listening to. There’s also a spacial awareness type one to hear what’s going on around you, like you would without noise cancellation. But it’s the noise cancellation that’s the real star of the show. When I was on a plane and I put these in my ears without even listening to anything audio wise, it numbed the sound of the plane so much and made it so quiet it was amazing. They’re small, and light in weight. The quality is just amazing - Apple does it again! They connected with my iPhone Pro 16 so well, instantly. I really can’t say enough positive things they’re worth every dollar.

reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase 16 Months In and Still My Everyday Go-To I use these AirPods for literally everything — phone calls, music, podcasts, working around my Airbnb property, camping trips, running errands, you name it. I bought them in December 2024 and they've been in my ears almost daily since. Sound quality is still excellent. Whether I'm listening to music while turning over my Airbnb between guests or taking calls while setting up camp, the audio is clear and crisp. The Active Noise Cancellation is a game changer — when I need to focus, it blocks everything out. And when I need to hear what's going on around me, Transparency Mode switches seamlessly. The USB-C charging case was a huge upgrade. No more hunting for a special cable — I use the same charger for everything. Battery life has held up great even after 16 months of heavy daily use. Comfort-wise, I can wear these for hours without them bothering my ears. They stay put whether I'm cleaning, walking, or out on a trail. Bottom line: If you're looking for wireless earbuds that do it all and actually last, these are it. I've had mine for well over a year of daily use and the sound quality, noise cancellation, and battery are all still performing like day one. Worth every penny.

reviews.title: Exactly as expected! (A subjective review, duh)
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase I never take the time to write reviews because why spend so much time on something that will ultimately have no effect on my life? Well in this case, these Airpods have met and exceeded every single one of my expectations and I figured that was worth writing about. Important disclaimers: - OF COURSE this is a subjective opinion, because that is all a review is. Take it with a grain of salt. - Different products work better or worse for different people, it's just that simple. - When talking about noise cancelling headphones, it's important to consider them for what they are, not what we think they SHOULD be. THE FIT: OKAY. I am someone who has very small ear cannals. I have spent years trying to find earbuds that fit my ears comfortably. Silicone in-ear tips have always been extremely uncomfortable for me. That being said, I did own the first gen Airpods Pro for a couple of years before buying these Airpods 4. I wanted earbuds that I could exercise with, use for commuting, and use for blocking out sound when I'm at school or on an airplane. The airpods pro fit the bill, but I could only wear them for short periods of time before they would start to seriously hurt. On top of that, they would always slowly come out of my ears. It didn't matter what size of eartips I used. My ears are just NOT made for in-ear silicone tips. BUT THESE AIRPODS 4!!! Holy smokes they are so comfortable. They never fall out. I can wear them for hours at a time and not even notice them. I haven't worn a pair of Airpods this comfortable since the gen 2 Airpods. The 3's didn't work for me either, but somehow apple really cooked up the ideal fit for these Airpods 4. THE NOISE CANCELLATION: Okay. Obviously, these are NOT going to have the same level of noise reduction as in-ear earbuds because there is no seal created by any silicone tips. If Airpods Pros and silicone tips have always worked for you, just buy those. However, considering that the noise cancellation in these Airpods is ALL driven by software, I have been extremely impressed with the results. It reduces the white noise of annoying sounds like public trains, airplanes, etc. very effectively. While voices and other louder sounds aren't cancelled completely, when music is playing through the earbuds these noises are quite enough to hardly notice. For me, that's enough. I don't need complete silence, I have my over-ear noise cancelling headphones for that. THE SOUND QUALITY: When I say that I was blown away the first time I listened to music on these Airpods, I was NOT kidding. The amount of bass that they managed to pack in to such small, open-eared earbuds is frankly impressive. The sound profile allows for an incredible listening experience no matter what genre of music you listen to (I listen to a wide range of pop, folk, indie, and rap primarily). THE OVERALL VERDICT: These Airpods 4 with Active Noise Cancellation are exactly as I expected them to be. They fit extremely well, the ANC is very acceptable for what they are, and they sound amazing. That's not to mention the TINY case that they come in, making them so easy to slip anywhere. I have been looking for a solid pair of earbuds with ANC that work with my ears for the longest time now. I'm happy to say that these are everything I've ever wanted. If you have had issues with silicone ear-tip earbuds fitting you properly, then these are definitely worth a shot!

reviews.title: Higher price but very good quality if you use apple products
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase I’ve been using the AirPods 4 for a while now across pretty much everything—grocery runs, workouts, and travel—and they’ve been super reliable. The noise cancellation is surprisingly good for how compact they are. It makes a noticeable difference in busy stores or while traveling. One feature I didn’t think I’d care about but now really appreciate is how they handle background noise and conversations. They’ll sometimes bump the volume up when there’s more noise around you, and if I start talking to someone, they lower it automatically. It feels very natural once you get used to it. They’re also exactly what you’d expect if you’re already in the Apple ecosystem—everything just connects instantly and switches between devices without any friction. That alone is a big reason I stick with them. And from a design standpoint, they look and feel great. Only downside for me is that occasionally the noise cancellation can give me a slight headache if I wear them for too long, but it’s not a dealbreaker. They are on the pricier side, but for how seamless they are to use and the overall experience, they’ve been worth it for me.

reviews.title: Effortless Sound, Smart Silence, and Apple Magic Done Right
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase Apple rarely tries to reinvent the wheel — instead, it refines it until it feels invisible. The Apple AirPods 4 with Active Noise Cancellation follow that exact philosophy. These earbuds don’t scream for attention, but once you use them daily, you realize how well everything just works. From music and calls to workouts and travel, they deliver a smooth, reliable, and genuinely enjoyable listening experience — especially for people already in the Apple ecosystem. ⸻ ✅ Pros 1) Active Noise Cancellation that feels natural The ANC here is very well balanced. It reduces background noise effectively without creating that “pressure” feeling some earbuds have. Perfect for: • commuting • flights • working in cafés • focused listening It’s not aggressive — it’s smart and comfortable. 2) Seamless Apple ecosystem integration This is where AirPods shine: • instant pairing • automatic device switching • rock-solid Bluetooth stability You put them in your ears and they’re ready. No apps to configure, no friction. 3) Clear, well-balanced sound The sound profile is clean and pleasant: • clear vocals • controlled bass (not overpowering) • crisp highs without harshness They’re tuned for long listening sessions, not just quick demos. 4) Excellent call quality Voice clarity is strong, even in noisy environments. Calls sound natural and consistent, which makes these great not just for music, but also for work and daily communication. 5) Lightweight and comfortable for all-day use They’re easy to forget you’re wearing. No ear fatigue, no pressure points, and they stay secure for walking, light workouts, and daily movement. ⸻ ⚠️ Cons 1) Not customizable like some competitors If you love deep EQ tweaking or tons of manual sound adjustments, these may feel a bit limited. Apple focuses more on consistency than personalization. 2) ANC is strong, but not the most aggressive on the market It’s excellent for everyday use, but people looking for maximum isolation in extremely loud environments might find stronger options elsewhere. 3) Premium price You’re paying for polish, reliability, and ecosystem integration — not just raw specs. ⸻ Wrap Up: Are the AirPods 4 with ANC Worth It? If you’re an Apple user who wants effortless wireless earbuds with reliable Active Noise Cancellation, the AirPods 4 are an easy recommendation. They sound good, feel great, handle calls well, and integrate seamlessly into daily life without any learning curve. They may not be the most customizable earbuds on the market, but they excel where it matters most: simplicity, comfort, and consistency. Overall: a refined, no-drama pair of earbuds that quietly gets everything right.

reviews.title: Excellent sound quality and noise cancellation: comfortable and easy to use
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase Click to play video I bought these Apple AirPods (with active noise cancellation) and the truth is that they have exceeded my expectations in several aspects. From the first use, the connection with my iPhone was fast and uncomplicated, something that I value a lot on a daily basis. Active noise cancellation works quite well for its size. It does not completely eliminate all sounds, but it does significantly reduce ambient noise, making them ideal for work, traveling or simply concentrating. They also have an environment mode that allows you to listen to what is happening around you without taking them off, which is very practical. As for the sound, it is clear and balanced. The bass is present without being exaggerated, and the voices are heard clear. For calls, they also fulfill well: the other person hears me clearly even in environments with some noise. Comfort is another positive point. They are light and fit well to the ear, so I can use them for several hours without discomfort. The case is compact and easy to carry, in addition to charging quickly.

reviews.title: Excellent add-on's - Pricey
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase The ANR feature on this model, is fabulous, but energy hungry. Excellent add-on's in a moving vehicle for taking calls, listening to tunes, and enjoyment of communications over a long period of time. Good fit. Durable. Easy to hook up to your mobile device. These are not a good value for the money, as they are Apply pricey and expensive, however, if you are going to spoil yourself or others who appreciate excellent fidelity, then go for it. I love the case on these as they have contactless charging. Nice features. Pretty good add-on. Works for AppleTV in the living room too....

reviews.title: Apple AirPods 4 inalámbricos
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase I got the Apple AirPods 4 and honestly… I’m in love 😍🔥 From the very first moment, you notice the difference in sound quality. They sound super clear, immersive, with really nice bass, perfect for music, videos, or calls. Plus, the spatial audio makes it feel like the sound is all around you 🎶✨ What impressed me the most is the active noise cancellation 😮‍🔥 It blocks out a lot of outside noise, so you can fully focus on what you’re listening to. And if you need to stay aware of your surroundings, the transparency mode is perfect. 💯 Another strong point: battery life Each earbud lasts around 5 hours of continuous use, and with the charging case you can get up to about 30 hours total, which is perfect for all-day use 🔋✨ They’re also super comfortable. They fit really well in your ears, so you can wear them for long periods without discomfort. 💬 My honest opinion: They’re perfect if you want comfort, great sound quality, and smart technology all in one. Ideal for everyday use, the gym, work, or traveling. ✨ Bonus: They connect super fast with iPhone and other Apple devices, and the USB-C charging case makes everything more convenient.

reviews.title: Perfect for outdoor walks
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase These are absolutely perfect for outdoor walks. I love my AirPods Pro 2, but when I go for walks with them I can hear a light "thumping" or something every time I talk a step. It's something about having sealed earbuds in. But with the AirPods 4, walks are great. They cancel out *some* car noise, dog barks, construction sounds, etc. Basically, just enough to be able to hear music better, but not enough to make it dangerous. With AirPods Pro 2, I was always a little worried about not hearing a car approach when crossing the street. But with AirPods 4 + ANC, the noice cancellation level is perfect for me.

reviews.title: Excellent noise cancellation (but not of screaming babies), sometimes hard to take out of the case
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase These AirPods come in a case with proportions somewhat different from earlier models. The two buds are arranged inside the case in a clever way, and the case recharges the pods even if the case is not plugged in. My only complaint is that given the size of my fingers and the arrangement of the pods, I sometimes have a lot of trouble getting a good enough grip to pull the first pod out of the case. Once one pod is out, the other is easy for me to grasp. I wore these on a recent 100% full airplane and -- aaaaaaah -- all the background noise floated away as soon as I put them in my ears. Aaaaaaaaah! The noise cancellation was excellent... ...until the baby across the aisle decided to start explaining, loudly, to the world that she needed food, or a new diaper, or a nicer hug from her parent, or whatever. I've looked at reviews of other noise-cancelling ear pods, and it seems that almost NONE of them are any good at filtering out baby cries. NONE. Supposedly, only over-the-ear headphones can do this, and they don't do it really by cancelling noise but by blocking it. Sigh. The parents were frantically doing their best to get their baby to quiet down, but -- you know -- it was a baby. Sometimes this can't be done.

reviews.title: Buzz & loose fit
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase I've been an Apple guy since the iPod days, so l grabbed these hoping for that classic clean sound-and yeah, the audio quality delivers. Bass is tight, vocals pop, and Personalized Spatial Audio actually makes me feel like I'm inside my playlist. Took them on a morning run last week, and the Transparency mode was clutch for hearing traffic. But here's the thing: they just won't stay put. I've tried all the angles, even the "twist and lock" trick from my old EarPods, but after 10 minutes of chewing gum or a quick head turn, one side's dangling off my ear. Worse though-the static. Every now and then, usually when I'm on a call or halfway through a podcast, the right bud starts buzzing like a bao radio signal. Happened twice yesterday while I was making coffee. No water or sweat involved. It's not deafening, but it's that annoying electrical hiss that makes you pull them out and put them back in.

reviews.title: Beginners Guide to Purchasing AirPods:
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase Here’s my take on what AirPods you should buy if you’ve never bought a pair: AirPods 4: a great low-cost option and low risk if you’ve never had AirPods before. As for noise cancelling (ANC) versus non noise cancelling, the ANC here isn’t great and I wouldn’t buy these solely for that. Rather, look to the ANC here if you’ve tried the Pro versions and know that for whatever reason they do not work for you but you still want Apple noise cancelling AirPods. Though if you need ANC and the Pro series don’t fit in your ears I would look to Sony or elsewhere because you may be disappointed with these due to the lack of ear-tips sealing your ears for the best ANC performance, like the Pro series does. AirPods Pro 2/3: I’ve only owned AirPods Pro 1 and 2. The first generation pods never stayed in my ears, they’d slowly fall out no matter what. I ended up giving them to a family member. The Pro 2 introduced new XXS ear tips which solved the fit option for me and now they work great. Though, I use both the Pro 2 and 4 intermittently for different reasons. The Pro 2 are great for movies, content, music, though they still start to get uncomfortable or fall out if I’m eating or talking. The AirPods 4 work well for those specific scenarios, when I’m eating or talking on the phone for brief moments. If you have the money to spare, try the Pro AirPods and if they don’t fit or work, circle back to these. AirPods Max: Avoid at all cost, I’ve never owned these to be completely transparent, but they are extremely outdated and due for a massive update really any day now. They have worse performance than the AirPods Pro 2/3 at 2x the price. I’ll also note, these are frequently on sale, so grab them when they’re 20-30% off and it’s a steal.

reviews.title: Least favorite pair of AirPods but better than any of my MANY non-apple earbuds.
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase Least favorite pair of AirPods but better than any of my MANY non-apple earbuds. I don’t find the AirPods Pro I or II comfortable (silicone in ear canal). The standard AirPods 2nd generation fit my ears the best and I assumed these would fit similarly but they do not. I do like the squeeze controls on the stems. The ANC (automatic noise cancellation) is OK but not as good as the AirPods Pro II that I use on airplanes (the ear canal silicone seal improves ANC although as noted not as comfortable for me). Despite the negatives above they are still preferred to all of my non-apple earbuds. I have probably bought 10 pair of earbuds from other manufacturers over past 5 years trying to find a more affordable option and none are as reliable/consistent as these. Pairing and switching between apple devices (iPhone, multiple iPads, Mac) is all pretty seamless with the AirPods and much more difficult with the other brands.

reviews.title: The best investment in electronics
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase Lighter than my AirPods Pro 1st generation, but the sound quality and noise cancellation quality is even better, I am impressed. This is the most used Apple product for me besides the iPhone. It lasts a whole week easily with phone calls and music . And I can leave me phone inside the house, while still listen to podcast while working in the garden. I bought it during holiday sales event, thus well worth the price paid (a bit over $100 I remember). Will gift it to wife once I got my new AirPods Pro 3, she will enjoy it I believe.

reviews.title: NO On-ear volume control! Uncomfortable!
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase Update: I bought these used in Like New/mint condition. They are still paired to the previous owner even though they looked brand new when I bought them. I’m having a lot of issues with them becoming garbled while listening and they chirp every time I open the case and at other random times. I would not recommend buying these used. I have no way to unpair them from the previous owner and they are not performing the way I would expect an Apple product to function. I also hate that Find My won’t make them chirp reliably but they will chirp randomly all the time ( both the case and actual earbuds). Previous Review: (still applies!) I've had these for two weeks. I almost returned these b/c they can be quite uncomfortable and I still haven't found a way to keep them in my ears as securely as I'd like, and also have them in comfortably. It's still a relief to remove them. But, I do really like the active noise cancelling feature for some things (treadmill and elliptical machine especially, and I noticed quite a difference in the grocery store). I've never had ANC headphones before. I like that they turn on right away without having to push a button to wait for them to turn on, and I can monitor their battery life on my phone, and and pair between my two Macbooks fairly easily (the 2014 Macbook still needs me to manually pair it), as well as my iPhone and Apple watch. I would prefer the rubber seal things for comfort and security, but after messing with them for a couple of weeks, I realize that I don't need to wedge them into my ears in the same way that I used to with my cheapo Amazon rubber earpiece earbuds and I now find them tolerable. I also like that they are white so if they fall off while I'm outside, they're easy to find, unlike my cheapo black ones. I like that they pause automatically if one falls out (although it's annoying if one is just loose and they pause for no reason. Sometimes they have to be loose to be comfortable). They will also auto-pause if you fall asleep (although I find that they pause too early while I am still awake). I like that you can wear just one when you are sleeping on your side, although Active Noise Canceling only works when you are wearing both. I bought them used so I am sort of annoyed that they still seem to be paired with someone else's iPhone and a different email address was showing up at first. It keeps notifying me that they are found moving with me but I am able to use them and I was able to change the name to my own name and I can use Find Me with them as well, but they will often make ringing noises at odd times. I'm not sure I'd buy used again even though they said they were in Mint condition and looked like they had never been used - original packaging and everything. My biggest annoyance other than their lack of comfort, is that there is NO WAY TO ADJUST VOLUME ON THE EARBUDS! I kept looking for videos to figure this out and it's only available on some earlier models. I keep trying to swipe the stem and nothing happens. I'm so used to over-ear headphones where I can adjust the volume, so I often find myself going back to my old headphones while I'm exercising because I don't want to have to pause my workouts just to change the volume on my phone. It means turning off my treadmill so I don't fall off while I'm adjusting the volume and it's really annoying! I also find that switching between modes is annoying so I have to take out my phone b/c it keeps wanting to turn off the ANC and go to a different mode automatically. I can never trust which mode it's in so I have to pull out my phone to look. I do like how easy it is to pause on the stems. I don't really like how you miss out on part of your podcast or book when you talk b/c usually I'm just talking to my dog on a walk and I don't need it to lower the volume automatically. This is probably a mode issue and I just need to figure out which mode to be in instead of always defaulting to ANC. I got my first pair of Gen 1 airpods free with my 2020 Macbook and they broke right away, so I'm really hoping these last a lot longer than those did! So overall, I'm keeping them, mostly b/c my other ones stopped pairing with my apple watch suddenly, but I don't love them. Some things are good, and just as many things are not. Voice clarity is very good. I can talk to my sister while on my treadmill and she can hear me just fine, and never mentions that she can tell that I'm walking (but then again, she does 90% of the talking).

reviews.title: Baptized and Still Working
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase I accidentally sent my AirPods through a full wash cycle… spin, rinse, the whole revival service. I just knew it was over when I found them at the bottom of the washer! But let me tell you… I pulled them out, let them dry, and they still worked! Not only do they work, they charge like nothing ever happened. Praise the Lord for waterproof mercy and unexpected blessings 🙌”

reviews.title: The buds are too freaking small and the charge is much slower.
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase ***I came back to edit my review because the charge time is really THAT bad. These take an entire HOUR to charge the buds. My 2nd generation pods took less than 15 minutes and would last the same amount of time. Long time Apple fan here, but I have some thoughts on these. Firstly, the earbuds are super small and I've already dropped each one a few times in the one week I've owned them. I think it's Apple's mission to want you to lose them so you have to buy more... I'm a short girl with normal sized hands, and I can barely get them in and out of the case without fumbling them. It's really annoying. I wouldn't even dare try putting them in or taking them out somewhere that if I dropped them they'd be lost forever, because that would likely happen at this rate. They don't even slide in and out of the case very easily, it's awkward. And I'm not new to Airpods, I had the 2nd generation ones for years before these as well so I'm familiar. At least the older buds were a bit bigger and not as easy to fumble. Not sure why Apple thought "the smaller the better" here. Second, the sound is weird honestly. Yes, you can change things in settings and turn certain things off and on and blah blah blah. But that's where the "overcomplicated" part comes in. It took me like ten minutes to figure out how to keep the volume from lowering every time I started quietly singing along with my music (Note: It's the conversation awareness setting). There are all types of other settings too that I don't really understand to be honest, it will take some getting used to and tweaking. And I guess it really depends on the setting as to what I would want on and off as opposed to my last set that I just popped in my ears and went about my day. It would be cool if you could at least set up like "work mode" or things like that instead of having to go in all the time and change everything manually. Also, sometimes it feels like you're under water or like your ears need to pop, and sometimes you can't really tell if they are connected or not, like you will hear the sound but can't always tell if it's coming from your phone speaker or the Airpods. And in true Airpod fashion, sometimes they just disconnect for absolutely no reason. I spent ten minutes trying to reconnect them when all I was doing was sitting at my desk and they disconnected on their own out of the blue. My bluetooth was saying they were connected, yet my music started playing for the whole office. I ended up having to manually disconnect them and then reconnect for them to start working again. Thirdly, these take much longer to charge than older versions did. My last set could charge the buds completely in about 10-15 minutes tops. This set took about 20-30 minutes to charge to 100% after they were at about 10%. The case holds a charge okay and that charges about the same as previous versions, but the buds themselves take longer than other sets I've owned. Let's see... I'm trying to think of good things to say... It's cool that it dings when you put the case on the charger.

reviews.title: tt la
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase it was difficult to pair with phone, case did not want to engage bluetooth function per their steps to press on front of case few times...I did press and many attempts than finally on a final retry it worked. case makes a beeping sound when I pick up and put down somewhere else, as if connecting to my Find My app. sound and noise canceling works well for my need. not sure if need the Pro version for more $$$. I do recommend and refurbish $ model is like new - no damages, no battery issues, in original casing as new - except it had prior user id and able to cancel .

reviews.title: Comfortable for long use with effective noise cancellation
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase I’ve been using the AirPods 4 for a few weeks now. I previously owned an older generation of AirPods (which I lost) and I also have AirPods Pro, so I have a decent point of comparison. The first thing you notice is how comfortable they are. The open-fit design doesn’t press into your ear canal like silicone tips do, which means I can wear them for long listening sessions without any soreness at all. The active noise canceling is better than I expected for this style of earbud. To be clear, it definitely doesn’t isolate as completely as AirPods Pro or AirPods Max — that’s not what these are trying to do. But it does a good job of reducing steady background noise, like office chatter or city hum, without making my ears feel “sealed off.” For my use cases, mainly wearing them in the lab and at the gym, they block out most of the gym’s background music and a lot of the constant equipment noise in the lab, while still staying comfortable. For me, that alone sets them apart from many other earbuds. As for sound quality, I’d describe it as fine. I’ve never really considered Apple earbuds to be about top-tier sound anyway. The tuning feels balanced for everyday listening, bass isn’t overly boosted, and vocals and highs come through clearly. It’s not going to compete with high-end wired headphones or true audiophile gear, but that’s not a surprise. One downside is battery life. Because I use them frequently and almost always with ANC turned on, the battery life isn’t great, honestly a bit worse than I expected. In short, these are comfortable, easy-to-use ANC earbuds that strike a good balance between performance and daily usability. If, like me, you value long-term comfort and Apple ecosystem integration, and you want decent noise canceling and sound without needing the absolute best in either category (which is also how several friends who bought them feel), they’re definitely worth considering. Translate all reviews to English

reviews.title: Great Sounding Earbuds on Android⭐
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase I'm a lifelong Android user. I've never had an Apple product because I didn't think they were that innovative compared to Samsung. Samsung has the fold and flip phones. Apple has the ugly bar on their iPhones they call "Dynamic Island." Not my thing. I've even had Samsung buds live. They were truly great sounding with nice bass and a smaller case. I decided to get these based on bunch of written and video reviews. I was disappointed that it didn't have an app for Android. I was a little disappointed, but these were on sale for like $120. The case is surprisingly smaller than the buds live case. And I like how these don't go all the way inside the ear, just like the buds live; it just sits on it. That's why I chose to get these instead of the pro models. The sound is what won me over. One word to describe Apples sound signature: fun. These sound fun and explosive. The bass is always present and the highs have an extra high. All the notes sound smooth. Great instrumental separation also. My favorite band is Linkin Park and they sound awesome in these. I've had Samsung, Sony's and Shures and although I love them all, these come out on top. The ANC is also surprisingly effective. It blocks out around 50-80%. The transparency mode is super. It's almost like wearing nothing in my ears. Though the fit takes some getting used to. I do have to constantly adjust it when it moves out of place. I also can't sleep on my side with the stems sticking out. I know why they're there. It's for the mic and the mic is superb. Next version should have it a bit more ergonomic and have the stem retract a bit. Love the white color on it. Seems to be made well. Durability factor is quite good. I can wear there for hours and not have issues with sound. Sucks though that I can't view battery info on my android though. Might have to use third party apps for that. I was sad about not having the option to use the main equalizer for it but the sound straight out of the box is top notch; I don't think it needs any sound equalizing, at least for me. All in all, these are great sounding, light earbuds.

reviews.title: A Comprehensive Review of the Apple AirPods 4: Pros, Cons, and What to Know
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase Apple AirPods 4 Wireless Earbuds – Review: Benefits & Important Considerations 🌟 Benefits 1. Improved Sound Quality AirPods 4 offer enhanced audio performance with better clarity, deeper bass, and more balanced mids. Music, calls, and videos sound noticeably richer than previous generations. 2. Stronger Active Noise Cancellation (ANC) The upgraded ANC reduces background noise effectively, making them great for commuting, studying, or working in noisy environments. Transparency Mode also feels more natural and comfortable. 3. More Comfortable and Secure Fit The redesigned shape fits a wider range of ear sizes. It feels lightweight and secure, even during long sessions or workouts. 4. Longer Battery Life AirPods 4 deliver improved battery performance, giving more listening time on a single charge. The wireless charging case also boosts convenience. 5. Better Find My Integration Each earbud supports Precision Finding, making it easier to locate them individually if misplaced. 6. Seamless Apple Ecosystem Integration Instant pairing, automatic device switching, spatial audio support, and Siri integration make daily use smooth and effortless for Apple users. ⸻ ⚠️ Important Considerations 1. Best Performance Only with Apple Devices Full features—such as automatic switching, spatial audio, and Find My tracking—are only available within the Apple ecosystem. Android users will miss many smart features. 2. No Customizable EQ (Without Apple Music) While sound quality is good, customization is limited unless you rely on Apple’s built-in options. Users who prefer detailed EQ controls may find this restrictive. 3. Price Is Higher Than Many Competitors AirPods 4 are positioned at a premium price. There are cheaper earbuds with strong ANC and similar features. 4. Fit May Still Vary by Ear Shape Although improved, not everyone may find the open-fit design comfortable. For people who prefer silicone ear tips or a more sealed in-ear experience, fit may be an issue. 5. Battery Degrades Over Time Like all true wireless earbuds, the built-in batteries are not replaceable. Heavy users may notice reduced battery life after 2–3 years. ⸻ ⭐ Overall Impression Apple AirPods 4 offer a strong combination of improved sound, better noise cancellation, comfort, and excellent integration with the Apple ecosystem. They are a solid upgrade for existing AirPods users and a great everyday choice—especially for iPhone owners—though the price and limited customization may be drawbacks for some.

reviews.title: These Do Not Fall Out!
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase Where do I begin? Six months ago i purchased the more expensive Apple AirPod Pro 4 after using Bose ear buds since they first made them, early 2000s. I liked the AirPod Pro 4, but i could not keep the darn things in my ears, especially in my left ear. They come with the tips in several sizes. It took me a week to find which tip was the most effective at keeping in my ear. That was a headache. Still they occasionally fell out when i was in the grocery store, etc. Light duty stuff. Okay, with some behavior modification, never bending over, never sweating, or never really moving, they would stay in my ears. Maybe. So hectic 4th of July weekend i jump in the car, as i reach to put the car in drive the right one fell out of my ear. Hit my shoulder and tumbled, landing on the little rubber part of the emergency brake where it attaches to the car. I held my breath as i reached for it as it sat right in the middle of rubber slit. If i touched too hard it might separate the slit & fall into the car. Softly i tried to lift it off the rubber. It slipped between the slit and i heard it hit the metal bottom of the car. I grabbed my flashlight, separated the slit and saw it sitting about 8-9” lower on the bottom of the car. I tried for several hours to snag that thing but i could not get it. So, i ordered these because they were less expensive. Period. Like my AP4 the sound was exceptional. It was much easier to set up than the AP4. I live in my AirPods. I wear them gardening, shopping, in the car, mowing the lawn, cleaning the house. I do not leave my house until they’re in my ears. I love the noise canceling feature, but also wear them without music if I’m in public & I don't want to chat with people. But the thing i love the most…they fit right in my ears and stay there. They don’t feel like they’re about to fall out. These do not have the rubber tips, but they fit, i can bend over, i can move. Now I have only had them a couple days so I gave them a 4 because i never write a review for anything electronic until i’ve used it quite a bit, so after a few months i will update and if everything works out i will upgrade it. A few more things about these that i like when you tap them to turn on the music, it comes on instantly, i think Apple refers to that as the buttons. Response time is much quicker to turn on and off. There is not a pause like the 4. It has a crisper sound than the 4. My 4s would occasionally lose connectivity, but these do not. Sometimes it pays to buy the less expensive item, especially if you get it on Amazon Prime Day. Also, this set comes in a smaller case than 4 so it’s prone to slide right out of your hand. I have a small hand & this thing is hard to hang onto. I ordered a strong case to slip this case into in a bright color so its easier to find in my handbag and to make it more durable. Glad i found these.

reviews.title: Great sound, but takes time to get used to
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase I had older AirPods and thought I had lost them, so I bought these for under $100 USD as a replacement. (Good price) The sound quality and noise cancellation are very good, but at first they felt a bit strange and uncomfortable. I even felt slightly dizzy, which caught me off guard. After using them more, I realized they automatically lower the volume or adjust when someone talks to you, which took some time for my body to adapt to. Shortly after, I found my old AirPods (happier with them), and my brother couldn’t use his anymore, so I ended up giving these to him (I had used them for about two weeks). Interestingly, he told me the exact same thing: at first he felt dizzy because of not hearing outside sounds, but after a few days, he absolutely loved them. We’re not sure if that initial dizziness is just part of getting used to the noise cancellation or maybe something genetic in our case 😅, but once adapted, the experience is great. Overall, very good value for the price, especially if you’re looking for strong noise isolation, just expect a short adjustment period. One person found this helpful Translate all reviews to English

reviews.title: Battery could be MUCH better
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase Look, I’m giving four stars because it’s not the sellers fault this Apple product wouldn’t stay charged. I loved the sound quality, I loved the notification options and noise cancellation but after the first few days, I found I was needing to charge the case twice a day and then after the first week, the case would be dead within a couple hours. The AirPods themselves would also die quickly. I was charging them 3-4 times a day. That’s not Apple quality. The right bud gave me roughly two hours play and the left about 3.5 hours. I eventually turned off all AirPod notifications to the pods so I could simply listen to music, but that didn’t help the charge life. I think this particular one item is simply faulty. I’m afraid to buy another set for fear they’ll resend me this same pair though :( I really did love the quality of the sound, there’s just something wrong with the battery life.

reviews.title: It Just Works
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase The only reason these don't get 5 stars is the fit. I'm one of those with weird ears that earbuds in general are pretty hard to get correct fitment for. These stayed in for a short amount of time before I would have to keep readjusting them. I also had to wear them with a forward cant to get them to stay in place for that short time. I couldn't wear them straight up and down. I purchased ear hooks to alleviate this issue and has been a game changer. The sound is amazing. With them fitting up and down like they should I could really hear the spatial sound effect. I could go on and on about these and all the features that go along with your iphone and it would take paragraphs. The sound is great, as mentioned before. Not super bassy. I did notice though if you cranked them up to the highest level the base is removed for some reason. So you have to kick it back a notch to get the richer sound again. Which isn't horrible I suppose if you're looking to save your hearing in the long run. Are there better sounding earbuds out there...Yeah probably. But these sound great and the ease of use along with the live translation, compatibility with your iphone and all apple products in general, it's worth sticking with something that you know will work. My only complaint was the fitment and since I bought the earhooks, the case won't allow me to keep the earhooks on the airpods to charge. So I have to remove the hooks each time and reinstall once charged. A bit of a pain in the ass. But not earth shattering. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: An altogether different sound
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase As a professional film editor I've spent literally thousands of hours under some pretty good headphones. So I get these out of the box tonight and had a very strange experience .. at first I thought these things sound like crap. Then I worked out a couple of settings and my opinion reversed immediately. You know when people say "I'm hearing stuff in my music I never heard before" .. yeah. I'm attributing it to insane imaging .. every instrument, vocal, effect and mixing trick stands in it's own space. You can intentionally isolate and focus on any one thing. The only place I'm finding them a bit deficient is in the low end .. it's crisp and punchy and the sub is clean but they just don't have it down there like my Sonys but they're the king of bass. The noise cancellation works well and I think contributes to the clear space needed for this amazing imaging. Haven't tried the voice translation yet.

reviews.title: Really Good Sound, Plays Good Dolby Atmos
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase The sound to me playing Apple Music on my iPhone 12 is crystal clear and rich. I use these to listen to Dolby Atmos songs- With these earbuds I can hear the instruments playing above, below, and all-around as the sound engineer has placed them. I used the ANC while travelling today. On the train and busses, the ANC did great, I thought. On the airplane - to be honest, I've never had a pair of ANC earbuds that I've been satisfied with on an airplane, so I'm not going to ding the AirPods on account of the ANC not being perfect in the difficult environment of a commercial jet. One aspect I haven't figured out yet: if I try linking these AirPods to my Google Pixel 7 Pro phone by Bluetooth, the phone doesn't recognize that the AirPods are there. I'll keep trying. Anyhow, of the many pair of earbuds I've personally had over the years, I think these AirPods are the best.

reviews.title: Great Volume & Noise Cancellation!
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase Just got mine! I used to have the Airpods 2nd Generation with spatial audio and they honestly weren’t as loud for what I paid for so I ended up not using them much also lost them lol. However, the volume on these are great and very loud! These airpods fit perfectly in my ears and don’t fall off. The noise cancellation also works great! I mainly wanted airpods to use for school and just overall for emergencies when I forget my gym headphones but these are definitely going to become my everyday earbuds. I’m honestly impressed, definitely worth the money in my opinion.

reviews.title: Not impressed, but cool features
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase These sound weird. Sort of muffled like the highs, lows, and mids are muddled together. Apparent most in actual music but also applies to videos where people are talking. The headphone accommodation option for balanced tone set to “moderate” seems to stop bass-heavy mixes from sounding like both I and the music are underwater, but other albums sound tinny so I have to set it to “slight.” I would prefer not to have to do this back and forth since the menu is buried deep in settings, and I didn’t have to do this at all with my 3rd gens. However, I lost the 3s and was using 1st gens before this so I’m keeping the 4s because they sound better than my 1s by a long shot. Fit: These fall out of my ears when I’m lying on my back sometimes, which has never happened with any other AirPods/EarPods models I’ve owned. Upright, they’re pretty in there, unless my ears are wet with either sweat or tears (happens), then they’ll slip right on out. ANC: ANC is a welcome addition, but only if the background noise is steady (AC/heater/rain/fridge). The more inconsistent the background noise, the more inconsistent the ANC is, which makes my head feel weird in places with sudden loud sounds like when I’m around roadwork or a busy restaurant. I was experiencing 15-20% battery drain per hour when I got these because “Find My Network” was toggled on by default for these AirPods. Toggling it off stopped the drain completely, now Find My doesn’t even show in my battery stats, so that’s worth checking if you have the same issue. USB-C, magsafe charging via apple watch puck, and the speakers on the case for find my are all a plus, no complaints here. I like how I can now use find my to find the individual airpods when they’re out of the case, or the case with no airpods in it, or the case WITH airpods in it. Overall, the jump from the 1st gens to the 3rd gens was like a completely different world for me, and I wasn’t expecting the 4th gens to sound worse sonically. If you want all the cool features I guess they’re worth it

reviews.title: Hey blue collar mfs read this review.
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase I wanna start by saying I’m a fabricator and I have tested these to the max in every way you can. I absolutely love the higher end version of these for the features that they provide. I hated the pros cause the rubber tips always felt like crap in my ears I love they went back to just durable plastic in the ear. They work fantastic for noisy environments where you just gotta take a call from the wife but your coworkers won’t stfu or truck are idling or someone banging a hammer on steel or aluminum. One issue I have is that the microphone for noise cancellation and transparency mode only can go to a certain decibel before they crack in your ear. I’ve seen this with grinders, welders and even my weed eater at the house it just can’t handle it BUT you have the ability to turn off both modes and with music or an audiobook goin it works for cancelling out a good amount of noise. The conversation awareness is pretty awesome when I have a driver coming in and I don’t wanna pause my music cause I gotta talk real quick, the adaptability mode is okay but I don’t see a point in having it they attach easy to my lunch box and are extremely portable come in the same size as gen 1s. Having Siri read off my wife’s honey do list is a nice feature I wish I could shut off sometimes but overall these are best pair of headphones I’ve owned by far. Working out with em, rocking my son, or ignoring people at work you won’t find a better pair.

reviews.title: I love my AirPods 4
reviews.body: Style: With Active Noise CancellationSet: Without AppleCare+Verified Purchase I'm alli-in on the Apple ecosystem, and have previously had some gen. 2 AirPods as well as Airpods Pro with noise cancellation. I always loved the AirPods 2, until the battery got weak. And I *wanted* to love the AirPods Pro, but my ears just don't work with in-ear tips. They just work their way loose, then fall out, especially if I'm eating or talking. You know, the thing you want to do with AirPods. Enter the AirPods 4 with ANC. I'm in love. It's the best of both worlds: A form factor like my old AirPods, but with the sound quality and noise cancellation of my Pros. They don't cancel like my Max's, but it's still a noticable improvement when the background noise is knocked out. The Bluetooth connectivity works flawlessly, and it's an easy matter of pressing the button on the case while near my device -- iPhone, iPad, MacBook -- to make a quick connection. And they hand off really nicely from my phone to my iPad to my MacBook. As an added bonus, newer generations of Macs and iPhones and iPads can connect to two sets of Airpods at once, something my wife and I now do often while traveling. They seem a little pricey, but, if you catch a good sale, these are a terrific value for media consumption, phone calls, meetings, etc.

reviews.title: 6 June, 2025
reviews.body: Please do not buy So disappointed in these. For reference I had the airpod 2nd gen and I got them in 2019. I have worn them EVERY SINGLE DAY more often than not on full volume for almost 6 years now. Finally, the battery life has died and the right main speaker fades in and out so I decided to finally treat myself to some new ones. These are not at all the same quality. First of all - the shape. These are not the same shape as the original AirPods. The originals were smaller and sat further into your ear comfortably, allowing less noise to seep through. These ones are bigger (despite the many reviews saying otherwise - I have attached an image so you can see) and actually sit further out in the main portion of your ear meaning that more noise can make it through because they are not deep seated. This goes to show you why they have a $50 upsell for noise cancellation. To fix a problem they created by changing shapes. Also on comfortability, the speaker divot on the top is actually painful to have in your ear because it’s a sharp plastic edge so having them in longer than a few minutes hurts. These ARE NOT small-ear friendly, coming from someone who does not have small ears. These do not fit. Second of all - sound The sound on these is SIGNIFICANTLY worse than older AirPods. I had both of my parents test out one of each AirPod and listed to the same so my and they both agreed the older was better. I saw one other review refer to the sound as “tin-y” and I agree with that. It sounds like every artist is singing with a lisp, symbols (or similar sounding instruments) also sound raspy, and overall everything has this static-like undertone going on. If I hadn’t had the better ones for so long and become used to them I probably wouldn’t have had an issues with these but now I cannot use them because I can hear how wrong all of my favorite songs sound. Not as important but still, these can only be worn in the designated ear in one single position. The older AirPods could be worn in just about any position and even upside down in the opposite ears. I would like to offer some food for thought - Apple got rid of the old design, changed to the new shape that didn’t work well, is selling an upsell to fix the problem they made, and then when you finally get tired of that you they hope you will just splurge and buy the AirPod pros which are nearly double the price even on sale. I took it inside to have it scanned, and sure enough, they had given me the regular AirPods 4 instead of the ANC model I paid for. Thankfully, the team checked my app receipt and swapped it out for the correct one right away. I’m happy with my purchase, but definitely check the box before leaving, especially with electronics. reviews.body: downgrade from the second gens As a music connoisseur and someone who literally used my gen 2’s for probably a good 6 years till they stopped working imma give my thoughts (i got the ones without noise canceling btw). in terms of actual music quality it was definitely an adjustment since it’s more base heavy. originally i didn’t like it but since ive been using these new ones so much i literally don’t notice a difference anymore, so id say not a negative in my opinion. in terms of shape/size these definitely suck way worse. the gen 2’s were a really really perfect size for me cause my ears are super small. the gen 4’s are shorter and fatter (lol) which means i definitely deal with some more ear pain/discomfort while wearing them. whereas the gen 2’s were much thinner and i couldn’t even feel them. they don’t fall out for me i know some people mentioned that (maybe it’s cause i cram them super deep in my ears tbh), but definitely not comfortable. my main issue is the battery life on these if ur gonna take anything from this review as to why you shouldn’t buy these it’s simply because these things die so incredibly fast to the point where i feel insulted that i spent money on them. i have to fully recharge these things once every two days, even though im not using them super crazy. my gen 2’s could last me through an apocalyptic disaster and by the time we’d resume society they’d still have some battery life left. so really really massive downgrade, steve jobs would throw on his tightest turtleneck sweater and cry if he knew how bad the battery life on these things are. so again my professional opinion here definitely not worth getting. with quality seemingly getting worse yet more expensive honestly i’m just considering getting non-apple wireless earbuds (even tho i love how easy these connect). oh i almost forgot one last thing that also sucks in comparison to my gen 2’s is you know when you take ur airpods out, either to put them somewhere or back in the case. my gen 2’s would stop playing music, with these it’s a super fun game where sometimes ill take both airpods out and it’ll decide to disconnect and start playing my music full volume out loud on my phone. so if ur looking to randomly blast waka flaka from ur iphone’s speaker while ur at work look no further than the gen 4’s. Now I have been charged for an item that I did not receive. Is that fair? I have spent more than 2 hours today trying to get a resolution for this but no help from Target. reviews.body: Horrible - huge downgrade in Airpods function/quality Do not buy the Airpod 4's. They are a downgrade in quality and in ergonomics/functionality. reviews.title: 9 July, 2025Verified purchase
reviews.body: Pretty good I have been using the gen 2 AirPods for those times when I need to be able to hear what's going on around me. They're decent for listening to music, but not great. After the price for the AirPods 4 was recently lowered, I jumped on a deal and purchased them (with ANC) hoping that they would offer a better listening experience while still being transparent enough for me to remain aware of my surroundings. They definitely produce better base and have a fuller sound overall. They're not on par with a good pair of in-ear earbuds, but that’s due to their open design. The ANC is effective, depending on the type of noise you are dealing with. Not sure ANC is necessary though since the reason many people buy open-ear earbuds is to be able to hear what's taking place around them. Some ambient noise should be expected, but to each their own. My biggest concern was how well they would fit, having tried the AirPods 3 which turned out to be too bulky for my ears. Happily, the 4's do fit my ears quite well. I haven’t had any problems with them falling out. There are many other good open-ear earbuds out there to choose from, but at the reduced price and given how well they work within the Apple ecosystem, I had to go with the Airpods 4. They are a nice upgrade to the AirPods 2. Rodgman value

I was a little bit hesitant before deciding to try it because I had AirPods Pro and I had also tried my husband’s AirPod 1 and AirPods 3. none of them would fit my ears. They kept falling out, so I wasn’t sure if this new version would work for me, but I’m glad I bought it and tried. it actually worked great. The sound quality is good especially with the spatial audio, noise cancellation works much better than my original AirPods Pro, and it’s surprisingly comfortable. I’ve been wearing it for about four hours tonight with video calls, listening to music and watching TV, and the AirPods stayed in my ears even when I’m walking. I’m really happy with the purchase. reviews.body: Nice improvement over older gens Make this short. I’ve owned every generation of AirPods. I know total waste of money. The pros (both generations) although noise cancellation is better. For me at least I can’t stand the rubber tips. These obviously do not have those and the noise cancellation for noises like an airplane engine or maybe a constant hum work really well. Only area they fall short in that department is high pitch things. But that’s as expected. The sound quality in my opinion is noticeably better than the Gen 3’s. Personally if you don’t like the rubber tips on the pros. These are a no brainer and are the best AirPods Apple has offered yet. Spend the extra get the active noise cancellation. reviews.title: 22 August, 2025
reviews.body: Package missing and stolen I've never ordered online from them before and after this experience, I won’t again. They marked my package as "delivered," but I never received it. Instead of an actual delivery photo, they uploaded random images from a parking lot—nowhere near my address. When I contacted customer service for help, they were completely unhelpful. They insisted that because a photo was marked as "proof of delivery," there was nothing they could do. This is incredibly frustrating and feels dishonest. Poor service, unreliable delivery, and no accountability. Avoid ordering online from them if you actually want to receive your package. reviews.title: Blocks most of the external sounds
reviews.body: I was not sure how the ANC on the 4th gen AirPods would work. I wanted something that would block the music at the gym. I am pleased with the results. I have been using this at the gym many days of the week and must say that it is very effective to block most external sounds except the very loud sounds, which it does help reduce. Once you have music playing through the AirPods, you won't even notice much of the loud external sounds. So overall, I give it an 8/10 for ANC. It is not a true noise cancelling AirPods but it gets the job done. I like that it has no rubber tip and easy to slide in and out of the ear. Amazing sound as always.

I’m not trying to give you all a hard time but I’ve been a Walmart member for a minute, and I’ve never had any complaints up until now. Properly packaged or not; it was empty. I was trying to cancel the package before it shipped, but it did, and now I don’t have it and cannot get my money back is crazy.

reviews.title: Apple AirPods
reviews.body: I’m very happy with the product, this is my 5th pair this year, my ears are the size of a newborn baby and they keep falling off my ears.

reviews.title: Apple AirPod 4
reviews.body: So I have had the AirPod Pro two and I have absolutely love them. I got this for Christmas. I’ve had them for two years and so I heard great things about the four and decided to get them. They’re not quite as comfortable as I thought they would be I like them better than the first generation which I originally had in the very, very beginning. I do like them. They are just not up to the standards that I thought they would be great noise, cancellation, small, and petite just not as comfortable.

reviews.title: Woah…
reviews.body: Tbh, I needed new AirPods bc my older ones were acting weird, buuuuut these AirPods I love em sooooo much. The noise cancellation is chef’s kiss!! Also the other features like it adapting to the noise going on around you or turning off music when it realizes your talking to someone. My only issue is that I believe they were made for a certain ear type. They tend to fall out of my ear a lot even if I do the turn/twist method. Also if you sweat a lot, they WILL fall out. I’m trying to find a work around. Overall, I love em they are AirPods but with ANC…which is rlly cool.

reviews.title: AirPod 4s with active noise cancellation
reviews.body: These AirPods 4 with active noise cancellation are awesome they really work and hope others buy it too

reviews.title: Best decision I've made.
reviews.body: I absolutely love these ear buds. I had the original AirPods and the volume started going in them. I chose these because I wanted them to be noise canceling. The absolute best decision I made. I love the noise cancellation feature. Best decision for actually drowning out the background noises.

reviews.title: AirPods connected to someone else’s account.
reviews.body: Just bought these in store from store 100 as what I thought was brand new. I went to connect them to my phone, and received an alert once I connected them telling me the owner of this item (the AirPods) would have visibility to my location and had an email I did not recognize. According to Apple, there is no way to fix this unless the “owner” deactivates the item from their account. If Walmart is selling previously used products at the price of brand new, maybe they should let their customers know before buying.

They track way better than previous generations. However, they quite often have this weird electric sound that randomly takes place as well as distortion on calls.

reviews.title: Perfect for certain ear sizes

I feel like these have a more comfortable fit for my ear shape and size and you don't have to deal with the replaceable tips, that rip after a while. reviews.title: Top notch, especially for the price
reviews.body: These are the most comfortable earbuds I've ever worn. On the very first time I wore them, I did not believe they were fitted properly because of how seamless the fit in my ear is. I'm used to shoving eartips into my ear canal, you know? I can barely tell that they are in my ears every time I wear them. That alone is just supreme, and then there's ANC which is really the icing on the cake. It sounds like wearing in-ears, but in a whole other realm of comfort. This is perhaps the best value Apple product for the price!

reviews.title: box came empty/product was stolen pre shipping
reviews.body: i would love to give 0 stars but that’s not an option. i ordered these for my husbands bday and received an empty box, no product in it. i have been fighting with customer service for over a week now as its obvious my product was stolen before shipping and i have gotten nowhere. customer service reps have been less than helpful and at times rude. i have asked to speak to customer service in the US and have been told that is not an option. i am now currently out the cost of the product and had to buy an alternate gift for the bday that has passed. my last call i was told that i should receive a new product on 9/9 (original order was placed 8/25 and empty box was received 8/26). if i do not receive the product by 9/9 i will have to dispute as fraud with my CC.

reviews.title: I bought these because I own AirPod pros (gen 1 and 2) but the gen 1s occasionally make a buzzing noise or sounds distorted so I don’t know how much time I’ll have left with them. They are great so far. Comfy and light. I almost forget they are in my ears. Noise cancellation is just as good as AirPod pros.

reviews.title: Good for small ears
reviews.body: I needed new headphones as my previous AirPods were starting to die. I have smaller ears and have found these to be pretty good at staying in my ears through my work day, running and weight workouts and all. The noise cancelling is great and I really like that I got them for a great deal through Walmart's app.

reviews.title: AirPod purchase
reviews.body: The noise cancellation is better than I expected…10/10. Sound quality is very good. The only issue for me is that the buds are a bit large for my ears and I have a constant fear of them falling out so I wouldn’t wear them when exercising but that’s just a personal preference. All in all I would recommend and would purchase again.

reviews.title: Love these! ANC works great!!
reviews.body: I love these AirPods! The ANC is awesome and really cuts out a lot of the outside noise. You can also make it to where it changes whenever you speak or if it hears voices.. I turned that off because it would hear me singing and lower the music haha! They are also pretty comfortable to wear and fit well in my ears. Before I purchased these, I was unsure if the noise cancellation would be worth the extra money, but it most definitely is in my opinion! These are much better than my old AirPods, which were the first generation I think.. I’ve had them for almost 5 years, and they still work great, but it was time for an upgrade. OH, I also like that these have a little speaker on the case so you can make them chime if you can’t find them. I love AirPods.. they really last and have great quality sound which makes it worth the price! So if you’re on the fence about these, I say go for it! You won’t be disappointed.

reviews.title: Don't order anything small from Walmart!!!
reviews.body: The box was shipped with nothing but packing paper inside NO air pods. When i contacted Walmart I was given the reassurance that this would be corrected by a refund, but received an email instead stating sorry no refund. The package was delivered so therefor no refund will be given. So I contacted Walmart again asking what changed from the conversation before and was assured that it was processed incorrectly the first time and that it would be resent correctly and I would receive a full refund. But yet again i received the same email stating sorry the package was delivered and no refund would be given that I would need to dispute the charges with my bank. Walmart has thieves in their warehouses and instead of investigating it they push it off onto the customer. Shame on you Walmart!

reviews.title: Just okay. Bad fit.
reviews.body: They’re okay. The noise canceling is good but certain loud noises like weight’s clinking at the gym sound loud and weird. The conversation awareness is great and worked well for me. These fit worse in my ear than the originals. If I’m moving my face muscles, like smiling or talking, they start to fall out. This is a deal breaker for me.

The case was flimsy and what's worse the MagSafe charger did not work ; as in the magnet did not work so the case just fell off the charger which I use with my current airpod 3's charging case. reviews.title: Poor battery life. Weird noise cancellation
reviews.body: Sound is decent but certainly nothing special at this price point. Battery life is terrible. Get about 6 total hours from a fully charged case. Noice cancellation is strange dips in and out, lets in a lot of sound in. All in all a huge disappointment.

reviews.title: Good product at a fair price
reviews.body: I got these to walk in because someone recommend them to me because my old generic ones broke. I didn't realize how nice the noise cancelling part was. Maybe it's normal in the higher priced ones but these raise and lower the volume based on the surrounding noise. I've only had them for a few weeks so I'm not sure how well they'll hold up but, for now, they are the nicest ones I've ever owned.

reviews.title: This is my 4th pair, i started with the 3’s and loved them but the volume stopped working so i had to get a new pair. I tried the pro’s first and the first pair was damaged, so I exchanged them for the same ones, they were terrible so I returned them. I decided the 4’s were similar to mine, so I got them, they were defective ( so I thought) and only held a charge for 1.5 hrs 2 hrs at best, and the charging case also died super quick too, I had to charge it every 6-8 hrs. I was stunned and thought that it had to be a defective pair, there’s no way they could sell them for that much and the charge lasts only 2 hours, and anytime someone called me and I attempted to answer on my airpod, it was extremely distorted and had loud buzzing in the background. I would have to hang up and call them to fix the issue. Missed an important call that couldn’t be returned too. So I exchanged them hoping for a pair that wasn’t damaged and/or defective, and the next pair ( the ones I’m reviewing now) weren’t much better. The charge has never lasted longer than 3 hours, the case does hold a charge a bit longer, about 12-16 hrs, but still cannot receive any calls without the buzzing and distorted voices. It was the worst purchase of my life. I also got a couple pair of Beats too, in between the AirPod purchases and both of them were defective as well. I’m getting them from Walmart, because that’s the only option in my city. Maybe if you got them from Apple or anywhere else you may have better luck. As for me, I’ve bought 7 very expensive sets of ear buds from Walmart and I’m 1/7! 🥲

reviews.body: This is my fourth pair in the past 4 years. Only because I’m dumb and leave them in hotels or drop them in the ocean. I keep buying a new pair because nothing else compares. I have tried Beats and other brands but nothing compares to Air pods. I recently lost the pros and replaced it with these. These are great for noise canceling and all the standard features. I just feel a little disappointed because the pros were a bit louder and had the ability to see how charged the case and each individual air pod is. I don’t see that as an option with these. Regardless can’t go wrong with Apple!

reviews.title: Absolutely Disappointing – Not Worth the Hype or t
reviews.body: I purchased the AirPods 4 ANC with high expectations, especially given the premium price tag. Unfortunately, my experience has been nothing short of frustrating. The main reason I got these was to use them during meetings on platforms like Zoom, but they’ve been a complete disaster. Every time I speak, I get the same complaints from colleagues: “Your audio is cutting out,” or “Your voice is breaking,” or even worse, “Can you switch to a different mic?” It’s beyond embarrassing. Imagine being in a professional setting and having to apologize repeatedly because your expensive earbuds can’t handle basic voice transmission. Totally unacceptable. Apple advertises these as top-tier ANC earbuds, but when it comes to call quality—which is a core feature—these fall flat. If I wanted to sound like I was talking from the bottom of a well, I would’ve saved my money and used some cheap knockoff instead. At this price point, it’s ridiculous to be delivering subpar performance. I expected clarity, consistency, and seamless integration. What I got was choppy audio, annoyed coworkers, and a headache. Apple needs to seriously fix this issue or stop marketing these as a reliable option for professionals. These might be fine for casual music listening, but if you’re planning to use them for meetings or work calls—don’t. You’ll regret it.

reviews.title: Disappointed
reviews.body: I was wondering are these brand new or are they refurbished because they have been giving me problems since I purchased them. Constant calling dropping, music stops playing mid song. I’m not too happy with this purchase!

reviews.title: They suck fall out your ears and do not work good
reviews.body: The regular two works better. Fits better. When ever I get a call my AirPods will be working fine for everything else but my regular phone calls sound robotic and sound as if my phone was to far away even when I’m right next to it.
