reviews.title: DISAPPOINTED
reviews.body: THE VACUUM WORKS AS EXPECTED FPR A MINUTE OR SO AND THEN BEGINS SURGING. i CAN STOP AND CONNECT THE CHARGER AND LET IT CHARGE UNTIL ALL THE BLUE LIGHTS ARE STEADY. iT IS NOT PERFORMING AS EXPECTED.

reviews.title: Just can’t give that fifth star
reviews.body: I was surprised that this wonderfully performing cordless vac exhausts (filtered, not smelly) air close to the users face, especially if using it in shortened, hand mode. It’s a great little vacuum. It works perfectly… Except I don’t like that air coming back in my face. I had my engineer son-in-law evaluate all the cordless vacs and this one was the best. I don’t have a pet, but it does a good job on pet hair according to others.

reviews.title: Works great but poor battery life
reviews.body: I've had the vacuum for several months and right out the box I was a little disappointed in the battery life. At first I was getting about 20mins max battery life. Now, only a few months later, the battery life is about 10 mins. Not even enough to vacuum my den properly, much less the rest of the hardwoods in the house. The charge time is relatively long so it's usually the next day before I am able to continue where I left off. I will say that the performance is quite good. It easily sucks crumbs and pet hair out of corners without the vacuum being directly over it. When trying to vacuum couch cushions, the extreme suction almost locks the head to the cushion and bogs down the beater bar and almost instantly trips the beater bar motor. Then you have to let off the trigger and depress it again for the beater bar to resume. On hard surfaces, it works amazing. The only two complaints as mentioned would be the short battery life and the inability to vacuum upholstery.

reviews.title: Great power and flexibility in a small size. Makes vacuuming fun!
reviews.body: I bought this Dyson V8 as an addition to my upright Hoover that I've had for years. I've always wanted to try one but they're so expensive. This was a great prime deal. I wanted a handheld unit for some of the more difficult areas to get into like the cat tower and nooks and crannies. I tried it on the cat tower and was super impressed. The suction was really great and the accessories included were great. They are so easy to swap in and out. I let it charge overnight and then went to use it on the rest of the house. I have some shag carpet, a rug, standard carpet and tiles. It work great on all of them. I found the max mode later and used it on my area rug to deep clean it. I was impressed. Then I realized I can use it with my car! I can't wait to clean my car. I have a vacuum for my car but it's terrible and I know this will be better. Cleaning the vacuum itself is also very easy. One of my biggest issues with my upright is it's such a pain to open the canister to empty it. This one is so easy. Also, my upright is pretty loud and this one is so much quieter. I was very happily surprised. It's been so great so far and it's going to replace two vacuum cleaners. I'm not using my upright anymore. I bought four more accessories as well to make it even more flexible. Charging it is very easy and the wall charging mount is very useful. I haven't mounted it yet but I will later. I'm extremely pleased, especially with the prime deal that I got and couldn't be happier with my purchase.

reviews.title: Battery life is short!
reviews.body: Suction is terrific!! But the battery life is bad. I can’t even vacuum my whole house on 1 charge, and my house is only 1600 sq ft!!!

reviews.title: Battery life
reviews.body: Battery does not last very long

reviews.title: I like the way it stows away.In charge
reviews.body: The first time i'm I used, they pulled stuff out of my carpet and I use good vacuum cleaner plane

reviews.title: Superior machine, just like mama said.
reviews.body: This Dyson is a game-changer if you have trouble using an upright like I did, because of joint and muscle problems. My husband installed this one on the wall above an outlet, so it is always ready to go, and it does a heck of a good job picking ip the dog hair from the precious rescue he brought home one stormy night. Four stars instead of 5 because of having to hold down the trigger for it to run. My trigger finger is all twisted and crooked from arthritis. My mother has the same complaint.

reviews.title: Great upgrade vacuum
reviews.body: I’ve been using a clunky plug in vacuum for about 5 years now, I’d always wanted a cordless vacuum and now I have one! Switching to the Dyson V8 was a spectacular decision for me! While the charge time does take a while and the actually battery life isn’t too great, it lasts long enough for me to vacuum the areas I need (every room besides bathrooms and kitchen have carpet) which is enough for me. I had previously vacuumed about 3 days prior to getting the Dyson, as soon as I put it together even on a low charge I went over the same already cleaned area just to try it out and WOW I was amazed at everything extra it had picked up. We are frequent carpet powder users and I thought my last vacuum was doing a fine job getting up all the remains, but boy was I wrong! After going over all the carpet I had put carpet powder on months ago and vacuumed up the Dyson picked up about another cup of powder which I’m assuming was deeply bedded in the carpet. It’s a great vacuum for myself and my needs, but if you need longer battery I’d look somewhere else, or I guess put on a dewalt battery pack I guess

reviews.title: Great vacuum!
reviews.body: This is my fourth vacuum in almost 2 years. I have had a Bissell, Hoover, the Zokerlife (for a short time), and now this one. This one is amazing! It is quite, lightweight, and has great suction. It’s the first vacuum I have ever owned that I could listen to music while cleaning and actually hear it. Furthermore, I have a German Shepard/Border Collie mix and she is shedding a lot right now and I have been trying to figure out a vacuum for a couple of weeks. I have a robot vacuum to supplement, but they can only do so much. This vacuum arrived yesterday and handled the dog hair like a pro (the picture is real. It’s her favorite spot in the house). The trigger for the power is a little annoying, but not bad. The fan is very strong and does blow at you while you are vacuuming, but it’s cool air, which feels somewhat nice these days. I also like the easy power setting toggle to switch from slow to max. I was easily able to switch from my hardwood floors to my carpet without skipping a beat. The only true downfall for me is the battery life. It charges fairly quickly, but I didn’t even get through my first floor. I am going to invest in a second battery because the pros definitely outweigh the cons with this vacuum.

reviews.title: Not worth the price
reviews.body: When I first purchased this item, the suction and run-time were both great. However, after only a few uses, the power and “pull” from the vacuum abated, and now the life of the battery is less than five minutes. It’s still picking up dirt, but I have to constantly plug it in to charge. Not worth the price, unfortunately. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: This vacuum is basically a member of the family
reviews.body: I LOVE this vacuum. After decades of throwing away other vacuums that would only last a few months before they started losing suction and blowing out smelly air (despite replacing filters and cleaning regularly), I decided to purchase a Dyson. I do not regret my decision one bit! The V8 is so lightweight and easy to maneuver. While the dust collection cup is small, it’s easy to empty and works fine in my small home. So versatile and goes from carpet to hardwood to tile seamlessly with no adjustments necessary. Super durable. I have long hair, a toddler, and a 75 lb dog and a cat and this vacuum handles it all with ease. It picks up dust and dirt, hair, food crumbs, cat litter, leaves… the list goes on. I love the charging rack that holds all the accessories and vacuum. Looks nice in the laundry room. Easy to change out accessories and I’ve used it to vacuum furniture and remove cobwebs from the ceiling and crumbs from my daughter’s car seat and high chair. It’s never clogged or lost suction. The max power option is nice to get dirt from deep in the carpet. Charge lasts a long time. I love that parts can be replaced if they ever stop working, rather than replacing the whole vacuum.

reviews.title: Revolutionized cleaning
reviews.body: You’ll never want to go back to a regular vacuum

reviews.title: great seller, will buy from again
reviews.body: Wow, 1 day delivery! used it right after delivery, worked great.

reviews.title: Good Product
reviews.body: Love it, we did have a shark cordless and it died. I ordered this one because it was on sale big time, it is amazing. It picks up so much more than our old one.

reviews.title: Be prepared to feel disgusted LOL
reviews.body: I live in a townhome that is all carpet with 3 cats… for the last year i’ve been using a Corded Shark Animal version vacuum. I thought it did pretty good picking up Hair and dust. From the pictures in this review, you can tell i was wrong omg. I’ve had this dyson for less than 24hr. I could not wait to use it. These pictures are after vacuuming the living room, stairs and two bedrooms. The suction power on the dyson is insanely incredible. Which leads me to why I only rated 4 stars.. The battery. On MAX mode, it dies so fast. I wish i could’ve afforded the more expensive dyson for a longer battery but it’s a trade off i guess. We typically never vacuum the whole house at once so in the long run this will work out for us as we periodically vacuum a few days out the week. In my opinion, could the battery be better? Yes but was it still worth it? absolutely. I have a small allergy to cats but i love my babies. This dyson will give me peace at home lol

reviews.title: Where have you been all my life?
reviews.body: I was torn as whether or not to spend $ on a Dyson, after using a corded shark for 4 years. The Shark needed a replacement part that was $120, so I figured why not just buy a whole new vacuum. We live in NYC, so we were lucky enough to go to the Dyson testing store to see what we liked. I initially assumed we would just buy the latest model. But our apartment is under 1500 sq feet. I was still torn after the store. My partner said he was morally opposed to spending 1K on a vacuum. Luckily, a gal pal had an extra v7 at her pad and I asked her if we could use it. One time hands on at our apartment, and I was sold! The trigger button that I thought would annoy me didn't bother me at all. I immediately ordered the v8 and it's the best decision I've ever made. We really didn't need all the extra bells and whistles of the newest model. Now, my floors are regularly clean, I use the upholstery attachment on our dark couch once a week, and it's made my home feel like I'm living in paradise. Like legit upgrade for my mental health!! When there's a mess of crumbs or a broken glass, it's so easy to just grab it off the wall and clean straight away. Soooo lightweight, and so relieved not to have a clunky heavy corded vac. We also have a spiral staircase, and it does wonders there, as well as making it easy to clean our upstairs area. My only regret is that I didn't get a Dyson sooner.

reviews.title: Very low quality for this money, very bad and useless support team, worst experience in my life :)
reviews.body: Nightmare with Dyson V8 Cordless Vacuum Cleaner I want to share my frustrating experience with Dyson here—maybe someone from their team will see it. I bought a Dyson V8 Cordless Vacuum Cleaner on Amazon in early July. One month in, it stopped charging, and the dust container jammed. I contacted Dyson support, sent it in for repair, and was told it was fixed within two days. They asked me to pick it up from their service center, which is very far from me. I asked if they could send it back to me, and they said they'd check with the repair center and get back to me within a couple of days—no one ever did. After two days, I followed up, and they gave me the same response. By the third time, they said it had been sent out four days ago (without notifying me), but I missed the delivery because I was at work. It was returned to the service center. I called Dyson again, explained my situation, and they sent it again (so they said), providing a tracking number. After a week, there were still no updates. I contacted the service center, and they told me it wasn't their responsibility anymore; I needed to contact UPS. UPS informed me that the package was lost in transit. I reached out to Dyson support again, even speaking to a supervisor, who promised it would be resolved within a week—and if not, they'd send me a new machine (another false promise). A week later, Dyson said UPS had found the machine and would return it to the service center, after which they'd send it back to me. Two weeks passed with no updates. When I followed up, Dyson told me UPS still hadn’t delivered the machine to their service center. After asking for a supervisor again, I was suddenly told I needed to pick it up from the service center in Plainview, NY—where it had apparently been sitting all this time! When I asked them to ship it to me, they claimed they don’t ship cordless machines anymore, even though they did so just a month ago. Despite escalating to a supervisor once again, I’m still waiting for my vacuum.

reviews.title: Love it but the battery is HORRIBLE
reviews.body: I recently got my Dyson and I am in LOVE with how well it cleans my house. The only down side of it is it literally only lasted me 10 minutes. I timed it because the first time I used it, I let it fully charge for 5 hours and then I started in my living room. My living room is rather small. As I finished the living room and was headed towards the hallway, it died. Battery done. I was disappointed because I was in a cleaning mood and now I had to wait another 5 hours for it fully charge. Once it fully charged I said this time I’ll time myself to see how long it last. I was hoping I could at least get the hallway and my two bedroom rooms done before it died again. Sad to say it died just as I was about to start on the second bedroom. Smh. So now I have to wait another 5 hours for it charge just so I can finish where I left off. With that being said, I’m not sure I’d recommend this vacuum and I plan on returning mine. For $400 I think the battery should last longer than 10 minutes. I’m so disappointed because I genuinely LOVE this vacuum and the way it cleans my carpet but I just can’t justify $400 and I can’t even vaccum my small house on one fully charged battery….sigh.

reviews.title: Love these Dyson Cordless Vacuum Cleaners
reviews.body: I've been buying Dyson vacuum cleaners because I have allergies and pets. I haven't found another brand that works as well to clean the carpets and furniture. Once I found out that Dyson added HEPA filters to their cordless vacuums, I immediately bought one. I really like the new design for emptying the dust bin on this one. I never thought about having to pull hair and fur out of the older model, but I certainly think about it every time I don't have to now! Wow! Everything falls out easily with this model! This cordless vacuum does a great job getting my LVP and tile floors really clean before I mop them. It also does a great job on my thick carpeting, but I do have to pull out the stand up Dyson periodically to fluff the carpeting and get deep into the cotton fibers because the carpeting tends to lay down when walked on. No complaints though, I knew that would be the price of buying the carpet I bought. The attachments are still easy-on/easy-off with this model. The furniture/pet attachment works better than earlier models for some reason.

reviews.title: Nice vacuum but the battery could last longer
reviews.body: Very nice vacuum. My only complaint is that the battery does not last that long. We own 2 so that when my wife cleans she can finish the house at once without waiting for the battery to charge. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Perfect for intended use
reviews.body: I never thought I would spend the money on a cordless because they are usually so expensive. I didn't really think it was worth the money just to avoid plugging and unplugging and plugging and unplugging. This one was pretty affordable, though, and seemed to have decent reviews. Here's mine: It's not supposed to replace a regular vacuum, it's just supposed to be for a room or two or a quick house sweep. The push-button feature (which is no big deal--I have wrist/hand arthritis and it doesn't bother me) enables it to stay charged longer. You're not supposed to hold it down the whole time, no wonder your hand hurts. I have done two and a half big rooms pretty thoroughly. I love this thing! I can do my ceiling vent covers easily. I can grab it and spot clean all over the place. It's great. I just plug it in when I'm done to make sure it's charged for the next little run. It's not a suction beast but it picks up most stuff. My big vacuum is there for the deeply embedded stuff if I really need it. It's not meant as a pet hair vacuum, there are vacuums designed for that purpose. It's a lightweight spot vac. What are people thinking? I don't know.

reviews.title: Lightweight, great suction, portable
reviews.body: There’s not much negative to say about this vacuum. It has great suction and it’s very lightweight. Small enough that it can be stored just about anywhere, but unless it is kept plugged in, it will lose its charge - something to keep in mind. The bin is not that large and has to be emptied frequently if vacuuming large spaces, especially in a house with a lot of girls and a lot of hair like ours, but I guess it’s a trade off for the lightweight and portability. If there was one thing I would change, and probably would not have purchased had I known about it beforehand, is the on switch. It would be great if it could lock into place while using, but with this model, you have to hold it down the entirety of the vacuum cycle and my finger is numb and painful by the time I’m finished. The switch is also on the carry handle and very easy to trigger it on by accident.

reviews.title: Best vacuum
reviews.body: The perfect vacuum for our 2400 square foot home with a dog. We had one before that lasted 7 1/2 years before the battery ran out. We’re ordered it again because it is durable and dependable.

reviews.title: Terrific as advertized!
reviews.body: It does everything advertised! Nice addition to our cleaning arsenal! One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Great vacuum!
reviews.body: Amazing suction and power! The only drawback is the short battery life. cleaned half the rooms and it was done and needed to recharge.

reviews.title: Great vacuum- terrible battery!
reviews.body: I love the vacuum, but the battery only lasts 10 minutes tops.

reviews.title: Life Changing and so satisfying
reviews.body: One of the best investments I’ve purchased recently. It’s super light weight, suction is out of this world, the air feels so much cleaner after one use and it’s extremely easy to dump. Also super satisfying to see how much it gathers in a short amount of time. At first I wished the battery life lasted longer, but that was because I had it on turbo the whole time. But I’ve learned when I need to use turbo vs not and I’ll switch h back in forth (which is super easy) and it will last for the full upstairs and the steps and some of downstairs before it needs charged again. I have a 2,500 square foot home. I also have 2 motorized vacuums in my house, but there is nothing like a Dyson! It just does the job so much better, especially on carpet. I also have a golden retriever that sheds a ton, and a cat! So I love all the different attachments to clean everything, but just the floor. Totally worth the money! And the fact that it’s cordless and light weight makes it so easy to clean!

reviews.title: Battery won’t charge
reviews.body: I’ve only this for about a week and I’m disappointed. Will likely end up returning it. Battery lasts about 10 min on full charge. Why did I pay over $300 for something that can’t hold a charge?

reviews.title: hold the button down whole time
reviews.body: you have to hold the button the entire time you’re vacuuming! also, no light & battery is short lived. the suction is too strong for my area rugs & won’t vacuum them :(

reviews.title: Will not stay charged long enough to vacuum 4 rooms with hard floors in low
reviews.body: I always used to swear by Dyson. Not anymore. Having bought more than one of these, I would give this zero stars but the vacuum actually worked for a few months before the battery started to die. On low setting, after initial purchase, the unit would clean 4-5 rooms hard floor before losing battery. After 2 months the battery life was reduced to half, I change filters, empty regularly and replace filters when needed. After 6 months, the unit would only vacuum 2 rooms before dying. I ended up using a knockoff cheap vacuum for a while and when contacted Dyson. They told me everything would be covered under warranty. After doing a short video call with them showing them the issue, they confirmed my replacement. At the end of the call they told me I wasn’t covered and the replacement would be almost 200$. I paid for an extra warranty and have never been treated as terrible as I was with support. I 100% recommend not using or buying this product as the quality of this machine had declined terribly over the last few years as well as the company. From an (now) ex-Dyson user, I hope this helps someone out going forward.

reviews.title: Terrible battery
reviews.body: I absolutely LOVE the vacuum. What I don’t love is that my battery goes from 3 bars to 1 bar after using it 8 minutes. I kid you not. I had read about that in other reviews, but ignored it. My father has an older version and the battery lasts forever. I may try and contact Dyson, but if you need to use it any length of time you will be in trouble One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Keep it handy. It’s dandy.
reviews.body: Handy and lightweight and easy to use. Be a little more powerful.

reviews.title: What a JOKE of a vacuum!😂🤣😂
reviews.body: What a JOKE of a vacuum. It's like having a TOY model. It barely has any suction for the publicity it gets for being a "GREAT" product. One would have to think they have a genius marketing department as the product itself it a total disappointment. It barely cleaned the dust off of my overhead fan. I ended up having to get up on a ladder and wiping it down by hand to get the job done. I've heard everyone speak to how "AMAZING" this product is, I guess their standards are far less than mine. I have a 20 year old Meile vacuum that I had hoped to replace this with for ease, but at this point I'm keeping the Meile as it actually has suction power to get the job done. I have a house with tile and hardwood floors, I just wanted it to be an easy thing to do quick cleanups. Personally, I have more success with a broom and a dustpan than this overpriced TOY. If this is the quality of DYSON, it's a major disappointment. The only real benefit I found is that it's quiet when it operates and that's about it. Guarantee you that I'm sending this one back. I paid 299.95 for this on Black Friday, and now see online that one USED at Amazon is the same price. So guess I'm the SUCKER for making the purchase.

reviews.title: Dyson V8 Cordless vacuum cleaner
reviews.body: Really cleans good. Highly recommend!

reviews.title: Light and versatile
reviews.body: This is my 3rd purchase of this vacuum. I get about 2-3 years use out each one. I have 2 dogs and 3 sons, so it gets used every day. It is lightweight and easy to use. It has the sucking power of a full size vacuum. I use it on my wood floors, carpet, and perfect for stairs. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Great for vacuuming
reviews.body: This is a great cordless vacuum. I like how it’s light weight and I’m able to move in tiny spaces. Charging wise so far hasn’t been an issue since I usually will charge it the day before or hours before I need to use it. My place is 1400 square foot and this works well. The only thing I do not like is the hand bottom that turns on and off the vacuum. You have to use your index finger to push it and hold it in place to keep the vacuum on, which my finger rubs against the handle. I bought a used one and it works like new. I’m glad that I bought it.

reviews.title: Vacuum perfect
reviews.body: The Dyson V8 was a perfect purchase for me. It is perfect for my wood floors. The suction power is great, the noise level is not to loud. I like the functionality of the cordless vacuum. The vacuum was a great price and fits my needs. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Short runtime, poor quality battery, not worth it.
reviews.body: The vacuum is powerful and does a good job as long as the battery lasts. I have owned the vacuum for nine months and the battery life has been my only complaint. The runtime has been short from the beginning but I was willing to deal with it because of how powerful the suction was. I found myself wishing I could buy another battery but the battery is not removable. It would be a huge improvement if you could have multiple batteries and swap them out when they died. In conjunction with an already short runtime, after nine months of use the runtime has dramatically diminished, indicating the battery is beginning to fail. I had a cheap stick vacuum before the Dyson and I honestly think it was a better value. I would not recommend wasting money on this unit.

reviews.title: Where have you been all my life?
reviews.body: The primary reason I bought the Dyson V8 was for the hair screw attachment which works really well on upholstery and other cloth based furniture. Being a cat owner, this hair screw attachment is a godsend! My house is primarily wood floor with a few large area rugs in the living room/dining room. The Dyson V8 cleans medium/low pile rugs very well. Occasionally you might have to go over an area a few times but honestly nothing to really complain about in terms of suction. As for the battery, as long as you have it set to the normal setting (left side) you should get around 25-30 minutes of run time using the main brush head and hair screw tool. The crevice tool/combination tool should get you up the 40 minutes advertised, maybe slightly less depending on how worn your battery is. Do NOT use MAX setting all the time. The MAX setting is intended for more stubborn particles and is only meant to be used for a very short duration. Using MAX will limit your overall battery usage to around 7 minutes or less.

reviews.title: Great Vacuum, trash battery
reviews.body: It takes 5 hours to fully charge, and lasts like, 10 minutes. Yeah, I should have thought about how much power vacuums eat before I bought it, but I just wanted a cordless vacuum that didn't eat beans. Don't get me wrong, it's a great cordless vacuum. Eats up cat hair like nobody's business. Its lightweight and east to carry around, and it's much more quiet that a standard plug-in vacuum. I just wanted to warn people of the battery life thing. If 10 minutes of good vacuuming is worth 5 hours of down time, get this. I personally don't mind, because I have a roomba that doesn't reach my room, and this fixes that, but, to each their own.

reviews.title: Heavy unit, horrendous screw-style motorhead
reviews.body: I've owned two other Dyson vacuums in this same style, this one is quieter, but this V8 is much heavier (strange, it's a newer model), the canister and handle parts together are longer, and the screw-style small motorhead is a piece of junk! It rattles very loudly and bangs against the housing, it's not anchored on one end and flexes around, it has a pivoting flat extension that gets in the way while you vacuum, it's horrendous. I could have paid about $90 less for the model without the small motorhead but I have a pet and need it for cleaning the cat tree, bedding, and upholstered furnitures. Unfortunately I've discarded the packaging and not returning. The old model of the small motorhead was great. This V8 also takes much longer to charge at 5 hours vs 3.5 for the older vacuum of the same style. I regret purchasing this and do not recommend.

reviews.title: Battery doesn’t last
reviews.body: I bought this to vacuum my area rugs and couch. Unfortunately the charge didn’t last longer than 15 minutes. I plugged it in again the put the attachment on to vacuum my couch and it didn’t last more than 5 minutes. Very disappointed 😔 returning it

reviews.title: Love it but
reviews.body: Love it but it did not come with a crevice tool

reviews.title: This should be able to use on rugs
reviews.body: For this to be a Dyson it could be much better. I have several of Dyson equipment that I perfectly happy with. This however is a bit of a disappointment. I can’t use it to clean my very thin rug.

reviews.title: Easy to use and great suction
reviews.body: Love love this vacuum

reviews.title: Dyson
reviews.body: Without a doubt, the best vacuum cleaner I've ever had, nothing escapes it, I highly recommend it

reviews.title: Game Changer
reviews.body: I am really happy with this vacuum. This has changed the way that I live day to day. Vacuuming is a breeze, legit takes 10 minutes or less for my whole house (basic rancher). It has two modes, one is quieter which is great for when the baby is sleeping. You can use the low mode on hardwood floors, or high on carpets or hard to pick up pieces off the floor. I zoom through cleaning now, which helps as I have five cats and a dog in the house- the hair collects quickly. I am now able to reach underneath my couches and the bed easily. No more hair tumbleweeds going across my floor! Excellent to pick up kitty litter without spraying it around, and does not scare my dog surprisingly. The attachments are great for vacuuming my couches, and I also run them across my coffee table/surfaces in between dusting. The head of the vacuum also turns, so it’s easy to get hard to reach spaces without having to move furniture. It’s changed the way that I live. Highly recommend. The best vacuum I’ve ever owned, worth the spend.

reviews.title: Dyson for live
reviews.body: Click to play video This is my absolute favorite Dyson. It is light, agile and easy to maneuver. Their new furniture head is ingenious - it vacuums all the hairs but doesn’t collect any hair on itself that you have to cut off the head. Absolute pleasure to use. I wish they never stop selling V8s. I am afraid something would happen to mine and wouldn’t be able to get a new one.

reviews.title: My favorite vacuum
reviews.body: I'm only five feet tall. An upright vacuum is a lot of vacuum for me. I also have asthma and migraines, so between vacuums kicking up dust and the noise, it's a challenge to vacuum. I love this one because it doesn't make as much noise as an upright, and I'm done in about half the time. Dyson's are also the absolute best vacuums for asthma/allergies because they don't kick up dust. I have cats and I have a lot of hair, and this one gets their hair up and gets mine up without it tangling around the brush, which has always been an issue with other vacuums. Love that I can convert this to a handheld and vacuum up my car. Charging does take a while, so I usually charge it, move onto something else, and then come back to vacuuming. Overall though, super happy with this purchase; this is now my primary vacuum.

reviews.title: I regret this purchase.
reviews.body: This equipment is awkward and clumsy to handle. I have 3 main issues with this unit. The weight is the first issue. The specs say that it is light-weight. Just the unit head is 5 pounds with the brush attachment. This may not sound like much, but when you consider this amount of weight is in your hand to support while you are doing what you need to do, it gets heavy fast! It takes strong upper body and arm strength to support this amount of weight. And when you attach the floor attachment, it adds another 3 pounds of weight. This weight is not on the floor, it is in your hands. The second issue is the battery heating up. When using it as a dust buster or a regular floor attachment, the blower is blowing cold air into your face and when it heats up after just a minute or two, it is blowing hot air into your face, or upper torso. On the 'max' setting, it just takes a minute for the battery to heat up and start blowing hot air. The third issue is how clumsy the floor unit is. It takes patience to get the floor attachment to level out to the floor, and each time you move it to another position you have to get the attachment to level out to the floor. Most aggravating. Another issue is the hair trigger the 'on' button has. This could be a great thing, but the design of it is that when you are picking up the unit, the on button is triggered. It is a constant struggle to handle the unit without pressing the 'on' button. I regret this purchase. I could not recommend it in any way, shape or form.

reviews.title: Can humans be in love with machines?
reviews.body: I think yes! Firstly, I love that it’s cordless, and the battery stays charged long enough to do my whole house! It zips around super easy and picks up everything I need it to. Hard wood, linoleum, carpeting, throw rugs… it handles it all. When vacuuming throw rugs, just switch to the lower setting. If you’re debating on getting this one, I doubt you’ll be disappointed. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: I have 2 of these
reviews.body: Like: good suction, assortment of attachments, wall mount. Small enough to use in the car. The battery runs as long as I want to vacuum, it is not big enough to vacuum a whole house at once. Dislike: small bin (lots of emptying), not enough wall mounts for all attachments.. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Make sure everything is in box
reviews.body: I like it, but missing a critical part.

reviews.title: Review?
reviews.body: The older I get the more I appreciate a quality vacuum and this is hands down the best vacuum I have ever had.

reviews.title: Sturdy, well constructed
reviews.body: This purchase was to replace a five month old Shark Cordless Pro which proved too flimsy for regular use. Thus far the Dyson has held up better and seems sturdy. The brushless roller is helpful in this long-haired household-no more weekly cleaning long hair tangled around the brush. For me the biggest drawback has been the trigger on switch which must be held throughout use. It's tough on arthritic fingers, and I also accidentally pull the trigger when handling the unit. The battery lasts well on normal suction. It's a bit overpriced, but what isn't these days?

reviews.title: Pretty good. Died prematurely. Has a warranty though.
reviews.body: This vacuum was SO great! Then it broke after being used nearly daily for 9 months. The battery stored functioning. Dyson is processing a warranty and I am hopeful my awesome vacuum will be good as new in no time. We have multiple dogs, multiple kids, and live on a little farm. The vacuum worked hard. It was great to use because of how light weight it is. I like it best on hard floors, but also use it on thick carpet. I have a shag rug it cannot clean at all. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Love It, but wish the battery was easier to replace
reviews.body: I absolutely love my Dyson and would definitely recommend it! However, I do wish the battery replacement process was more straightforward. I purchased a spare battery, only to discover that swapping them out isn’t as easy as I had hoped. This has led to some frustrating waiting times for charging. While the battery lasts around 35-40 minutes, it’s not quite enough to clean my entire house in one go. Overall, it’s a great product, but a more user-friendly battery replacement would make it even better!

reviews.title: Dyson stick vacume
reviews.body: Had to send it back it was defective

reviews.title: Light and easy to use
reviews.body: I rented a condo in Florida this year they had this Dyson in the unit... I loved it! I came home and bought one for myself and am not disappointed. The only issue I have with the vacuum itself is that it says that it has a 40 minute charge. I've discovered that I can only vacuum one floor of my house at a time before it runs out of battery life (about 15 minutes). Mine was obviously built in Mexico because ALL of the directions came in Spanish so I had to figure out how to put it together and hang it up without instructions. I ended up calling Dyson directly and they sent me online videos which were perfect. I love the vacuum, I just wish it held the charge as long as it's advertised to do.

reviews.title: Awesome Suction
reviews.body: I have went through a few vacuums trying to find the perfect one for hardwood floors because every other vacuum i have bought always either shoots the crumbs back out onto my feet or the crumbs just completely falls right back out as soon as i think I've vacuumed it all up. So i decided to splurge on a vacuum so that maybe i would actually find a good vacuum for once, and here it is. I'm in love. You can actually feel it suction to the floor when you switch from low to high. Now i will say the battery doesn't last too long but thats the only downside for me. But it's still worth it! I was able to vacuum my living room, hallway and one bathroom before it died, so that's still not too bad. I give it 5 stars!

reviews.title: Love it!!
reviews.body: I hate to admit that I love a vacuum but I do… because of the price I kept putting off buying this and trying to find a cheaper substitute… a real waste of time and money! My dog sheds heavily year round and I am amazed at what a great job this does on all the hair on my short piled rugs and hardwood floors.Amazingly, I hardly ever need to use the “high” setting and therefore am always able to clean my whole, admittedly small house with battery life to spare. Those who are fanatical about “deep cleaning” may need to haul out their big vacuum occasionally but I honestly haven’t felt the need to yet…. Buy it! You won’t regret it One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Amazing except for 1 thing
reviews.body: After buying several cheap, knock offs I finally decided to purchase a Dyson. I love the vacuum and it does a good job however, I don’t like that you have to push the button down to vacuum the entire time. It is kind of a weird feature, considering you’re spending so much on the vacuum. It should just have a standard on and off button without having a hold down a button the entire time you vacuum.

reviews.title: Not worth it…
reviews.body: Great suction, but this is overshadowed by the HORRIBLE battery life. I’ve only had this vacuum 2 years and it started out with 20 minutes of battery life after being charged, which was frustrating enough, but now dies after 3 minutes. I just can’t justify the price and would never purchase again.

reviews.title: My 1st Dyson vacuum and I am happy it works Great!😊
reviews.body: Great price and vacuums Great✨💫💫💫💫😁 Translate all reviews to English

reviews.title: Powerful and lightweight
reviews.body: Light weight and portable especially for townhomes. Powerful for something this size. Wish it can hold more charge though. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Nahhhh- just say no
reviews.body: I used to love my Dyson cordless vacuums BUT the battery does not last long enough and the replacement is too expensive to keep purchasing. It is not a recommended product due to the short lifespan of the battery. Do not waste your money on this (and I have bought 3 of these).

reviews.title: substandard battery life
reviews.body: A monumental disappointment. This vacuum is advertised to run for 40 minutes, and has yet to do so. After charging for several days, it stops after only 20 minutes. Emptying the sup does not help. I do not recommend. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Es una gran ayuda de limpieza
reviews.body: Este año mi familia ha adquirido 4 equipos Dyson v8 ; todos estamos contentos con nuestras adquisiciones pues tenemos mascotas y nos está facilitando la limpieza de nuestros hogares ; me encanta que recoje todo tipo de pelo , le quite 1 estrella porque en el encendido full o más rápido se descarga rápido ; pero en general está haciendo bien sus funciones … repito somo nuevos usuarios y estamos muy emocionados con este artículo. One person found this helpful Translate review to English

reviews.title: Very impressive little device. Exactly what i wanted!
reviews.body: I was a little worried about the small motor and if it'd be capable of cleaning my little apartment. But man it really works great, even all the little attachments which I tested out thoroughly. The mounting system and cleaning out the device is super easy and well thought out. The one draw back I'd see to this is, you do have to constantly hold the trigger down to vacuum. There's no lock for it. But I just got this for my little apartment and it's perfect for that. Battery lasts about 10 minutes on max power, probably 15 ish on normal power, so not a lot of time, but more than enough for my needs. Charges pretty quick too. Overall, I was a bit worried, but totally satisfied when i finally got to try this baby out. :)

reviews.title: I’m convinced…
reviews.body: That people are saying this vacuum is great just n because of the name. I had to make sure I wasn’t crazy or temporarily losing my mind. You charge this vacuum for 5.5 hours to be able to use for 40 minutes. If it’s in max mode, you have 7 minutes. Guess which mode works the best? To use the vacuum, you must continue to hold the trigger. Being an ex-violinist, this caused my hand to cramp. The vacuum head is not as wide as a normal vacuum do you are spending extra time covering areas. Someone mentioned that there is a cord to use in case you do not want to be wireless. There is no additional cord for that. The only cord is the one you use to charge it for 5.5 hours. You cannot use it while it is charging as it will damage the product. The suction is great but I cannot believe the run and charge time. Dyson should really partner with a brand that can meet their suction with a battery that matches their quality. I like the sleekness, it’s not lightweight to the point of it feeling “airy” but it’s not heavy, I love the neatness of the station design to mount it. My carpet even felt differently after using it in the area I was able to vacuum for 7 mins. I’m extremely disappointed. My vacuum search continues….

reviews.title: Really nice cordless vacuum
reviews.body: Bought one for myself and one for each if my kids excellent product

reviews.title: Awesome vacuum!!!
reviews.body: This is a great vacuum! I waited to post acreview because I wanted to really get some use out of it to make an opinion & this is the best vacuum I ever bought. Very light weight. The battery last very long and it is very powerful. Also super simple to empty. I have recommended this vacuum to several people since I bought it. Great purchase!

reviews.title: Dyson stick is household essential!
reviews.body: The quality of Dyson products is always outstanding and this handy vacuum stick is no exception! I have 2 dogs with one that sheds tumbleweeds of hair and I keep my Dyson busy with that alone. No need to empty often since the dirt container holds a fair amount. However, this model is easy to empty neatly without mess before putting it back on the convenient charging rack after use. I love the way the stick can maneuver around furniture to catch all those dust bunnies and the multiple attachments fit into almost any space. Two speeds allows a longer battery run. Dyson is not cheap but the saying “you get what you pay for” is certainly true!

reviews.title: Not worth it!
reviews.body: I bought this model Dyson since it was on sale and I needed to replace my eureka stick vac I’ve had for a few years. Never had a Dyson before and figured why not see what the hype is about…so far I’m not impressed! I primarily use a vac like this (without having to pull out our house central vac system) to clean up after my toddlers messes, to suck up dog hair and a general quick clean up. The battery life is awful and I don’t keep it plugged in 24/7. I can maybe get a few, quick, runs around the house with this before it needs to be charged again. Also I’m so annoyed that it’s a trigger on/off switch instead of a constant on switch (just my personal pet peeve). It’s heavy for my hand/arm and rubs against my thumb when using. It doesn’t stand up straight on its own. Using it on my thin, washable rugs where the kids play, it stops spinning like there’s something caught in it, when there isn’t, so I have to stop it so it can almost reset, then use it again.

reviews.title: 1 December, 2024
reviews.body: V8 vs V15 I bought the Dyson V8 After my Dyson V 15 kept giving me airflow errors. The V8 was less expensive than the V15 because of fewer features (particle size indicator, battery level indicator, bin size and longer battery life) but the V8 still does a great job. I like the fact that you don’t have to take off the nozzle in order to empty the bin, which was the case for the V15. I just wish that the bin on the V8 was bigger. I was concerned that the battery life of the V8 would not last long enough for me to vacuum my whole house, but so far it has been fine.

reviews.title: 23 September, 2024
reviews.body: Gone down hill We've had a number of these over the years and this new model feels a lot more "plasticy" and flimsy than previously. The motor also seems weaker and makes a wheezy whining noise. We have 7 Dyson products in the house so have always been loyal to the brand as although expensive we've always been happy - until now. For the cost we're a bit disappointed with this one

reviews.title: 25 November, 2024
reviews.body: Terrible - don't buy it! The battery life is really short - on max, mine lasts about 10 minutes only and then takes over 1.5 hours to recharge! So I only get through about a quarter of my house! YOu need to squeeze the button with your fore finger which gets tiresome. It does not really suck up eveyrthing so the surface of my wood floor often has dust left. I find it quite noisy too, and as the length of the handle/stick part is not long enough - obviously made for short people (I am tall). I would send it back if it were not such a faff to do so. I wish I had not bought it. Why does Dyson have such a good reputation when this product is so average? I feel like I was duped by all the marketing!

reviews.title: 2 March, 2024
reviews.body: Great Purchase-Love it I bought this about 5 weeks ago and so far it has performed well. What I absolutely love is the lightweight and portability of the unit. (I'm 67 yrs old and ease of use played a big role in my selection) Also the cordless feature was a big reason for the purchase and it is just so much easier that having to work around a cord constantly. I am able to finish the entire house (2100 Sq Ft) without a recharge on the regular setting. (Upstairs is rugs and vinyl plank-downstairs is all carpet) Haven't had to use the Max setting yet as the regular setting seems to do the job. We have a 90 lb Siberian Husky who is a shedding machine....so I use the vacuum about every other day and am able to better keep up with the dog hair....with much less effort than my canister....which I love but needed something easier to work with on a more frequent basis.

reviews.title: 29 December, 2024
reviews.body: Easy and love all the features!! I have back issues. My husband had seen this while browsing on the internet and called me over to look at it and ask me to check it out and see if I thought it might be helpful to me. I read up on it on several sites and read a ton of reviews. It sounded good!! So he ordered it for me. I have someone to come in every 2 weeks and do my floors, dusting and bathrooms. I maintain everything in between. Which living on a farm and my husband refusing to remove his shoes at the door..lol..I had to grab a broom and dust pan to sweep- up quite often. Then there is always the two ( outside) dogs wanting to come in and visit. Well it came.. it was so easy to snap everything in place. ( In anticipation of it coming I had downloaded the Dyson app and it shows videos on everything). He easily installed the charging station in my laundry room. Works wonderful!!! I just cannot tell you how much I love it and how much it has helped me. Everything about it is easy. No bags to deal with, easy to clip off and empty is a big plus to this old back of mine. All the tools are just so easy to snap on and off.. YEA!! I get excited telling people about it. And it… Stays charged and ready!! Picks up wonderfully. Just grab it and go and in a couple minutes my kitchen floor is clean. And I love walking barefoot on a clean floor!! Oh my!! No cord to drag around and I don’t even have to bend over to plug it in!!! I almost forgot that and it’s one of the main reasons I wanted it!!! Bottom line..You are going to love it. Yep!! You are going to love it!

reviews.body: Good, but not as good as expected Bought the Dyson V8 Absolute a couple of weeks ago. I owned a V8 for almost 3 years and I thought that maybe the new V8 would be better. This V8 is different than my previous V8. My old V8 has very good suction power and the battery lasts a decent amount of time. The new V8 has less suction power. If you increase to the MAX position, the battery drains fairly fast. The cleaning head ball joint is very tight and it does not flex as it should. Many times I have to manually orient it to the desired angle. The HEPA filter is a good addition, but it makes me wonder if it is causing the loss of power. The new V8 came with plenty of accessories, and that is a good thing. The old one only had one. We are keeping it as it is doing a decent job, but again, we expected at least the same or better suction power, and longer battery life. 1 reply from Customer Service team - 1 year ago Hello, thank you for sharing your detailed feedback with us. We appreciate your loyalty to Dyson and are sorry to hear that the new V8 Absolute hasn't fully met your expectations, especially in terms of suction power and battery life. Your observations about the cleaning head ball joint and the HEPA filter are valuable, and we will certainly take them into account for future improvements. We're glad to hear that you found the additional accessories useful.We would also like to learn more so we can learn ways to improve future services. Please feel free to email askdysonUS@dyson.com in the Subject Line please include: "Dyson Review" for prompt attention to this matter. Warm Wishes, Manpreet K

reviews.title: 31 December, 2024
reviews.body: Dyson V8 Cordless Vacuum This is my first cordless stick vacuum and also my first Dyson. I’ve been a loyal long term user of a competitive brand with a name that rhymes with park but the reviews for their cordless vacuums weren’t that great so I opted for this in Black Friday. I’ve been using it regularly, pretty much daily or every other day for about a month now and am very happy with my decision to purchase this vacuum. My main level is about 2000 sq ft with only 3 bedrooms having carpet. The rest is LVP. I have 2 cats and a 3 year old toddler so this machine is put to the test often. Pros - 1. Lightweight and super easy to use and maneuver. 2. Suction exceeds my expectations. 3. Comes with accessories including a crevice tool, dust brush, motorized mini brush head and two interchangeable roller heads for different flooring types. Also comes with a wall mounted docking station for charging & storage with hardware included. 4. On regular mode it’s a lot more quiet than any corded vacuum I’ve ever owned. 5. Filtration and dust cup are impressive. They work so well and are easy to use. I love that any hair or dirt built up around the filter inside the dust cup is removed when opening the top cover to release the bottom cover of the dust cup. This is brilliant in my opinion. No more sticking my hands inside a dirty dust cup to remove wads of built up hair and dirt. Areas for improvement - 1. No LED light on the roller head or motor for use with attachments. This is one of my biggest complaints and disappointments with this vacuum. 2. Large debris doesn’t get sucked up due to how far down the front part of the roller head cover comes down. This causes larger items to be pushed around on the floor but never sucked up. This is another point of frustration because it doesn’t suck up pieces of cat food that are smaller than the size of a pea. 3. Carpet roller head doesn’t work on most of my rugs, even those that aren’t high pile. For some reason the brush roll stops spinning and the suction cuts off. Maybe this is a user error but I watched all the instructional videos in the app and didn’t see anything addressing this. This is frustrating since it requires getting out my larger corded vacuum to do the rugs. It does work well on my low pile area rug though. 4. Maybe this is normal with cordless vacuums and my lack of experience with them is showing but I was really surprised that there isn’t an on/off power button. Instead there’s a trigger that you have to hold down continuously during use. I’m not a huge fan of this and have also accidentally bumped it and had the vacuum start which scared the crap out of one of my skiddish cats. Not a huge dealbreaker or anything but just something to note. 5. The handheld motor part gets warm really fast when using the vacuum in max suction mode. It doesn’t get hot but does get noticeably warmer very quickly. Overall I’m very happy with my decision to give this Dyson cordless stick vacuum a try. It charges quickly and holds its charge for the stated amount of time and the suction is great.

reviews.title: 1 March, 2025
reviews.body: Dyson V8 Advanced, an amazing vacuum cleaner. Absolutely love this hoover Dyson! every time I have to hoover, I so look forward to picking this hoover up and hoovering my floors. It is expensive yes, I appreciate that, but it is such to me real well worth it money spent well, however you save up or have the cash buy one of these Dyson V8 advanced hoovers, you will not regret it. So much time you get on low suction with the rechargeable battery, and been completely honest you really don't need the max setting, it's good to know it's there to use and in case, but I don't use it much, can get all my rooms done good with low setting. Then there is the wall mount this is very good if you can set it up, I have not used it, just plug the cord in it and charge it, fine for me, the tools are all great and fit on very easily and comfortably and have good use and suction power and ease of use. Great hoover the best I have ever bought, will probably buy another one in future, they are that good, trust me!, buy one now, you will not regret it in fact you might fall in love with it and you will definitely love your hoovering so very much. Thanks, Jamie.

The machine itself is not easy to push around on carpet at all. It sucks up the dirt just fine but with the battery not lasting even half an hour and having to use so much strength, it’s not worth it. I would not suggest this to anyone. There are cheaper ones out there that work just as well, have a swivel head and with batteries that last much longer. We had a cheaper ones that was broken by accident and we decided to replace it with a Dyson. Ha! We’re not impressed. 1 reply from Rubaid K team - 9 months ago Thank you for sharing your experience. It’s frustrating to face issues like limited battery life and maneuverability on carpeted surfaces, especially after investing in a premium product. Your feedback regarding the swivel head, battery performance, and handling is invaluable, and we have passed it along to our product team for consideration in future improvements. Although your initial experience wasn’t ideal, we would appreciate the opportunity to support you further. If you are open to exploring usage guidance or accessory recommendations, please contact our customer support team at askdysonus@dyson.com. Thanks again for your feedback. Best regards, Dyson Marketplace Support

reviews.title: 20 December, 2024
reviews.body: Very happy with the vacuum. We’ve only had it about two weeks so I can’t speak for the longevity. We have vinyl plank flooring throughout the entire house with a couple of area rugs. I’m able to do the whole house on regular power without having to recharge. Some of the reviews I’ve seen have shown that people are unhappy with the battery life, but it seems to neither doing the entire vacuuming job on high power, which I have not found to be necessary. It picks up really well- admittedly, you have to empty the canister more often than a Dyson upright. We have three cats and it gets all their cat hair. It recharge quickly and the wall mount is very convenient. It easily gets underneath couches, chairs, and foot stools. I like the fact that it’s very lightweight and maneuverable. I haven’t touched our corded vacuum since we got the Dyson. When this dies, as all appliances eventually do, I have no doubt that I’ll replace it with another Randy G.

reviews.title: 4 December, 2024
reviews.body: Loud screeching sound from the cleaner head I bought this a couple of weeks ago and the first time I used it the brush head made a loud screeching sound. I contacted Dyson and they said that it was not normal and I should return to the store. I returned the Dyson and ordered an exact replacement. The replacement had exactly the same noise so I return that one and asked for a full refund. I have had Dyson cleaners for over 25 years. Sadly no longer as the quality of the machines is no longer acceptable and especially for the price. I have now moved onto another brand of cordless vacuum cleaners.

reviews.title: 26 December, 2024
reviews.body: Great job This is my second vacuum. My original one works but wanted an upgrade. Not disappointed, great product and works super well on my hardwood, ceramic and carpet. The reason I gave four stars I found it somewhat tricky to empty. The original older model was easy to empty when full. As I become more familiar with this model, I’m sure that emptying it will become easier and routine. Overall am pleased with the Dyson V8 performance.

reviews.title: 4 July, 2023
reviews.body: Makes life more enjoyable Not sure how, but this vacuum makes my life more enjoyable because of how easy it is to use, and how clean it gets my surroundings. It has become more of a daily meditation than a chore. The Dyson V8 Absolute is perfect for my studio apartment and can handle any task, from cat litter to dusting around the keyboard with the included detailing brush, this is the greatest vacuum I have ever used. And no cord to get in the way. Perfect!

reviews.body: Disappointed I am very disappointed . The vac just pushes bits along the floor and doesn’t pick them up. The dust sticks around the brush and foes not go into the bin. Have a got a faulty one?

reviews.title: 22 December, 2024
reviews.body: Superb This is my 4th Dyson and I love it. Excellent pick up, including pet hair, very versatile.

reviews.title: 25 November, 2024
reviews.body: Expensive and outclassed by other products This is my second of these, bought years apart. I had assumed that it would have ‘grown up’ or been ‘enhanced’ in the intervening years and apart from a gimmicky and fiddly mechanism to empty it, it appears to be the same. It doesn’t stand up on its brush head by itself as other do. You can’t turn the brushes off like other do. It has two settings ‘max’ and ‘anemic’. It only picks up acceptably on max Charging can be via the wall bracket (good if you have an electrical outlet and place to hang it close together, but I doubt you do) or awkwardly leaning in corner. Comes with the brush head and two tools, competitors have more. The V8 is now outshined by the competition and not worth the money

reviews.title: 2 October, 2024
reviews.body: Best light weight vacuum with good suction Bought it a month back and am super pleased with it. It fits my most important requirement - light weight. It's is light yet powerful . Easy to use and does a good job on regular suction and fantastic job on power suction . Battery lasts for my use as I tend to use it for daily quick runs rather than long / deep cleaning . Great on hard floor and carpets and stair carpets are now so easy to clean . The suction on regular mode can be better hence I gave a 4 star.

reviews.title: 20 September, 2024
reviews.body: On the whole, happy A good product and it is excellent for getting under beds and wardrobes etc. There is a great range of movement in the tools which mean it is easy to manoeuvre around the furniture. reviews.title: 5 October, 2024
reviews.body: I love all my Dyson Vacuums! I bought my Dyson V8 Origin a little over a month ago and am overall very happy with it. This is my third cordless Dyson vac. The first one I owned for over ten years and it served me well considering the battery charge on the older models were not as great as the current models. I mainly used it for quick floor clean ups and not for large vacuuming jobs. I also own a fairly new Dyson SlimV12 that I love, especially for the on/off switch. My only complaint on my V8 Origin is the trigger switch. I would've liked for Dyson to make this model with an on/off switch like the Slim model because I have arthritis that flares up occasionally which makes it difficult for me to use for an extended length of time. This is the only reason for the four stars and not five. Overall, I'm very happy with V8 Origin's cleaning ability and long battery life if used on the lower suction setting. I would highly recommend it to anyone on the fence about buying a Dyson cordless stick vacuum.

reviews.title: Seller Verified Purchase
reviews.body: Small vacuum that does quick cleanups We have owned a V11 outsize for years and am very happy with it. We bought the V8 specifically for the room where we keep our cat's litter boxes for quick vacuum cleanups of the tile floor. This vacuum has a small head and is not ideal for vacuuming 2,000 sq ft but for a small apartment or small rooms in a big house the compactness is an asset. It comes with the usual attachments and has decent enough battery life. My one recommendation is buy another set of filters if you are going to use this every day. Once washed they take a couple of days to dry out but they really do trap the dust which is important when vacuuming around litter boxes. Review from Dyson reply from Dyson Customer Service Hi, thank you so much for sharing your experience with us. We’re really glad to hear that your V8 has been a helpful addition for quick cleanups and that it’s working well alongside your V11 Outsize. We truly appreciate your thoughtful insight about using extra filters—daily use around litter areas definitely makes that a smart recommendation, and feedback like yours helps other owners as well. reviews.title: Seller Verified Purchase
reviews.body: Excellent For Daily Use I purchased this Dyson V8 Absolute as a replacement for an older model Dyson stick vacuum that lasted eight years. The design, weight, and maneuverability of this model is excellent and functions well as a secondary vacuum used for daily clean-up. While it is new, I am impressed by the battery life and HEPA filtration. While the suction is not as powerful as our larger tank vacuum, it performs well and efficiently on both area rugs and hardwood floors. I appreciate the various attachments that make going from floors to the sofa to hard-to-reach crevices a snap. Overall, I am very pleased with this product. For allergy sufferers, I don't think there is a substitute for larger tank vacuums with more power and enhanced HEPA filtration, but this model is a great choice for smaller spaces and daily maintenance of larger spaces. reviews.title: Seller Verified Purchase
reviews.body: Loving the Dyson! We’ve had this vacuum cleaner almost a month, and so far it is everything I hoped it would be. The suction is terrific! We have both carpet and vinyl flooring (yuk), and this thing goes from one to the other quite well. The couple of times I’ve bumped it to full power, the fully charged battery has lasted only a few minutes. Thank goodness the slower speed meets our needs most of the time. One of my primary uses of this vacuum is upholstery cleaning. It does a fine job. One of us is blessed with piles of long, long hair, which she sheds constantly all over the house. The Dyson manages that hair beautifully. I have far fewer roller clogs and tangles with the Dyson than with other vacuums. The swivel head is a little awkward to use, partly because the machine is so heavy. I am old, arthritic, and feel the weight and the swivel difficulties keenly. Thank goodness I only need to use this machine a few minutes at a time. The easy release dust bin works better than any I have owned. The filters seem to be doing a good job. We have so far used only the upholstery/pet brush and the hard surface roller. As I learn more about the machine and use it for a longer period of time, I will update my review, if that is possible. For now, we are quite pleased. Review from Dyson

reviews.title: Seller Verified Purchase
reviews.body: Handy quick cleanup device with nice attachments Cleaning has finally become so easy I finally want to do it as often as the situation requires! The vacuum is great, came with useful attachments, converts from long to short in no time, and the attachments are interchangeable with my other ball Dyson. The be wall mount could use improvement. Review from Dyson reply from Dyson Customer Service Thank you for sharing your feedback. We’re delighted to hear that your Dyson has made quick cleanups so easy and enjoyable, and that the useful attachments and ability to convert from long to short have added to the convenience. It’s great to know the attachments are interchangeable with your other Dyson as well. We’re sorry to hear the wall mount could use improvement, and we appreciate you pointing that out. Your satisfaction matters to us, and we remain committed to making Dyson solutions even more practical and user‑friendly. Thank you again for your input.

reviews.title: Dyson, Inc.
reviews.body: Issues? Be aware that the vac will not stand on its own, but has to be propped against a wall or into a corner if you pause your task to perhaps move something out of the way. The "trigger" is very sensitive and there is no way to lock it out while moving the machine or preventing curious little fingers from turning the thing on. The trigger is active even if the dust bin bottom flap is open, but is deactivated while the machine battery is being charged. The battery is not removable and probably could not be replaced if need be. The wall mount is handy and keeps the machine from resting on the floor. The vac performs as expected for a Dyson product.

reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 11 November, 2020
reviews.body: No problems here after 5 days of use Picked this up on Target's month-long Black Friday deal for November. I used a big one at work before the lockdown and we were sent home and really got tired of daily coffee brew prep/clean. So once I saw this for $49.99 I jumped on it even though many reviewers report failures and problems. So far after about 20 brews (5 of which were repeat 16oz water test and break in to clear any manufacturing residue before first brew), it is working fine. I am using cold filtered water from my refrigerator's front dispenser to fill it (8oz) and when it brews, the coffee (and thick ceramic mug) are hot. It is still very warm 20 minutes later. Some also complained about a long brew time. That has not been my case either. For an 8oz cup of cold filtered water, I have a hot cup of coffee in my mug ready to go in under two minutes from reservoir fill to done. Also so far I have noticed no messy leaks. I have to wonder if these reviewers reporting serious problems are using hard tap water that isn't filtered or doing something else wrong/not recommended. If it breaks I will be the the first to report about it here with an updated review. Bottom line: I find it perfect for a single person and the only reason I didn't get one sooner having been working from home the past eight months was that I could not justify the $80 price tag over my traditional daily use 4-cup brewer. I think the $50 price point is totally fair for a 6-12oz single dispenser Keurig. Time will tell if it was a wise choice.

reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 23 December, 2021
reviews.body: Not the product, but the user's error. This coffee maker isn’t perfect, but it does the job (you get what you pay for). It brews K-cups well - strong, hot and fairly quick. Just be sure to keep the machine clean!! Clean the coffee maker to get rid of the coffee grounds and the hard water that builds up inside, or else it won’t work. When it fails to brew a cup, CLEAN it. The machine is telling you it’s time to do so. You can use distilled white vinegar or purchase a coffee maker cleaner/cleaning solution. Run it through a few times and then it will start working again just fine. A lot of these reviews aren’t fair to the product; they are because of human error. What machine do you have that works perfectly if you fail to maintain it properly?! Heck, even our own bodies fail/break down if we don’t maintain them. Not rocket science. reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 22 January, 2024Verified purchase
reviews.body: Stopped working in 4 months! I can’t figure out what the deal is with Keurig single serve coffee makers. Here’s why: my mom has had the same model working just kosher, no issues, since—my gosh I can barely remember—AT LEAST 2008?! It’s a pretty big size but like I said, this was one of the originals, before they started trying to make them smaller & smaller. I, on the other hand, have gone through SIX Keurig models (including this one here) in only 7 years! This K-Mini was bought in early September and by January 16th, it was taking upwards of 15 minutes to make one cup of poor tasting coffee because it was barely capable of literally just one drip at a time! And I always made sure to only use water from my Pür pitcher since we have hard water where I’m from. I even used the keurig branded rinse pods every month, and did a vinegar/water cleansing mixture TWICE each week! But it still crapped out in less than 4 months?! These are NOT cheap, and neither are the pods obviously, so I think Keurig has some explaining to do and REALLY needs to go investigating into the many complaints similar to mine to figure out what they’re doing wrong when building these. Clearly it’s far more than just me. It’s a disgrace! 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 2 years ago This is definitely not what we like to hear. reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 7 November, 2022
reviews.body: Anazing product We’ve had our Keurig Mini since 2020. I have been reading the most recent reviews and I don’t know where people are getting the negativity from. If you’re buying this coffee machine you need to know it’s small and compact so the water reservoir isn’t huge like your normal sized Keurig, hence the name “mini”. It also takes a second to warm up and pour your coffee. We’ve never had a cold cup of coffee come from this machine. We use 20oz mugs most mornings and run it twice, it isn’t super speedy fast but it’s faster than brewing a pot of coffee for us to each fill our 20oz mugs. When I use a regular mug, it’s never splattered or sprayed. These are issues that you should contact Keurig about instead of whining about it on a review. These little coffee makers are small but mighty and we love ours even two years after purchasing. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 3 years ago We just love hearing this! Thank you for taking the time to leave such a detailed review. We appreciate your feedback. Thanks for brewing the love! reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 14 August, 2021Verified purchase
reviews.body: Stopped working in less than one year I bought this product about 9 months ago. The day before yesterday the start button started acting fussy, and today it won't even light up at all. I have always used this item gently, one cup of coffee per day, and I deep clean & descale it every other month. I can't think of any reason it should stop working so quickly, and I can't find any information to help me troubleshoot. The company just tells me I need to clean it, but that's not the problem. It's clean. I tried cleaning it again. It seems like an electrical problem. I don't have my packaging anymore, and it's outside the returns window so I don't know if I can return it for being defective. Be aware that if you buy this machine and it dies there is no online manual or troubleshooting guide that can help you, and customer service, well they aren't product engineers, they don't have any more information than the internet has. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 5 years ago We're sorry to hear about this. reviews.title: 10 November, 2022Verified purchase
reviews.body: LOVE! I downgraded from the larger Keurig with all of the bells and whistles to this amazing little gem since I am the only coffee drinker in my home. It is perfect for my home office. I have been loving the mini. No issues at all. No splattering, no cold cups of coffee. One thing to note is that you must do a cleansing brew before you brew your first cup of coffee per the instruction sheet, so that's possibly why others were having problems. Reading is fundamental people!!! I also love the cord storage in the back. To top it off I purchased it 50% off as a black Friday deal at Target. Great purchase! For those of you that are complaining go spend $200 on a Keurig with the bells and whistles if you can't handle the simplicity of this one. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 3 years ago We just love hearing this! reviews.body: (no review title) I’m giving this item 4 stars imply because it takes longer than most keurigs to brew. That is probably why some of the other reviews are saying their machines didn’t work or they didn’t pull water from the back. You have to wait for the correct lights to press the buttons, lift the handle a certain height then wait to put in cup, etc. even then it takes a while for the coffees to come out. Patient is key with this machine. Now for the pros. It is super cute. I have a small cubicle at work with not much space. This machine fits perfectly and because the cord retracts there is no cord clutter. After you get over the wait time it works just like any other keurigs. Haven’t had it for long so I don’t know how long it will last but I’m assuming as long as the others. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 8 years ago Stephanie, thank you so much for taking the time to review your K-Mini coffee maker. reviews.title: 29 March, 2021
reviews.body: Not the best I have owned precious KEURIG’S in the past, and they were amazing. This particular KEURIG... not so much. To start off, it has a beautiful design, the slim fit makes it ideal for placing on kitchen countertops. The dusty pink color is gorgeous and has a very nice glossy finish. That’s why I’ve given it 2 stars. Now functionality this is awful. It is indeed very noisy and splatters coffee all over the machine no matter what size mug you use. It also lags. For example when you place water in the reservoir normally the coffee comes right out. For this one it takes a moment to register and the coffee takes forever to come out and then the machine shuts off as if it’s made the coffee. A bit of a pain when you’re in a time crunch, trust me. I’ve attached photos of the coffee that splatters everywhere. I’d suggest getting a different KEURIG model, for the price it is NOT worth it. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 5 years ago That's not what we would hope to hear. reviews.title: 15 January, 2019
reviews.body: K-Mini Plus I can’t figure out what is wrong with my coffee maker. If I use bottle or water from water cooler it draws down around one ounce and shuts off. I have to lift the K-cup lever 4-5 more times before all the water is drawn down and it starts heating and brewing, the result is Luke warm half brewed coffee, but if I draw water from the tap ( well water temperature doesn’t seem to matter) it will draw all the water down heat like it should and make a good cup. Why won’t it work with purified drinking water? 1 reply from Consumer Care team - 7 years ago Thank you for your review. Coffee and tea are over 98% water so quality drinking water is necessary for a good coffee, tea or iced beverage. Never use softened water because sodium ions in softened water form a gelatinous mass around the coffee grounds and tea leaves, slowing down the extraction process and causing over-extraction. This can also clog your coffee maker. If you need assistance, please call our Consumer Care team at 866-901-2739, we will be more than happy to help.

reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 17 September, 2020
reviews.body: Best Single Serve! We have three and are all going on 2 years old. Never an issue. Brews perfect K-cup and generic reusable cup. Two are devoted to water only for teas and hot chocolate to eliminate coffee taste. They do have a trick to them. If you repeatedly fill past the 12oz line, they machine will start and then stop. You have to keep lifting up and down the latch to get it to force the water down and signal the brewing light to stay on and start blinking rather than shut off immediately. Once I troubleshooted this little trick, it works just fine (albeit annoying). So, don’t return it until you’ve tried this. They are just a little picky on how much or too little water you put in. We love ours at home and work. Would buy this single size over large any day. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 6 years ago Thank you for taking the time to leave such a detailed review. reviews.title: 14 December, 2019Verified purchase
reviews.body: Great Single Serve Keurig that takes any K-Cup I got this coffee maker on sale just before Black Friday, and I had heard Keurigs are good but hadn't bought one because they usually require specific K-Cups to work. I bought it anyway because it's a good price for a Keurig. This Single Serve can take ANY K-Cup, which is awesome! I only have good things to say about this. It is thin/narrow and takes up a small footprint. It can make cups up to 12 oz. However much water you put in is the amount of coffee you get back. I've read others' reviews where they thought it was loud. I guess that is subjective. I found it rather quiet, with a low hum, but I don't have it in a quiet office environment. I think it takes about 2 mins to get a hot cup. It doesn't drip after it turns off. With my previous coffee maker, I always had drips in the removable tray. Another good thing, there are no grounds in the bottom of my coffee cup. I got the Oasis color, and it's pretty. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 6 years ago Thank you for taking the time to leave such a detailed review. We appreciate your feedback!
