reviews.title: 9 December, 2019Verified purchase
reviews.body: Trust the negative reviews, do not buy! My old Keurig recently broke after 3 years and I was ready for something that took up less counter space. When I saw this as a Black Friday deal, I decided to ignore the negative reviews and buy it anyway. Huge mistake... During the initial cleaning, it kept dripping water lukewarm for 5 minutes after it had "finished" brewing. It was also really loud and sounded like brewing was a struggle. That should have been a red flag, but I decided to brew a cup of coffee anyway. That coffee turned out cold and almost translucent. I tried again with 4 more cups, wasting K-cups, and all had the same result. It was then that I realized the negative reviews were correct and I received a defective unit as well. I returned it the next day and will probably never buy another coffee maker from Keurig again. The quality that used to come with their name is no longer there and now they're just making junk products. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 6 years ago We understand your frustrations and are sorry to hear you returned your K-Mini coffee maker. reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 12 November, 2020
reviews.body: Small Footprint Has It's Trade-offs I've had this for two years, since the 2018 Black Friday deals and paid $47.50 for it with Red Card discount and some additional promotion discount. It has certainly had it's issues, however as the keurig with the smallest footprint, I'd still probably pick it in the end since my counter space is at a premium. As others have noted, it is very loud when it releases water into the heating chamber. I'd say about every 25 cups of coffee it has a freak-out moment and only allows 3oz into the heating chamber, which means unplugging it and starting all over. It is a very annoying malfunction. I use the reusable K-cups with this model with no issues. I wish it made the coffee a tad hotter. I also wish it didn't exhale in a splatter of drips at the end of every cup. It commonly has an audible "moan" for some time after a cup of coffee is brewed. I attribute this to some strange internal pressure thing happening inside the machine. It sounds very sad. I also wish I had just gone with the black model instead of the fancy blue because light colors show all the coffee splatter and require lots of wiping. In summary...it's a cheap coffee maker with some strange issues, but until I find something better at the same size, this will do. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 5 years ago Hello and thank you for your review. The behavior you are describing certainly doesn't sound normal and we would love to help resolve this issue for you. At your convenience, please contact our consumer care department at 866-901-2739 to speak with a representative. We look forward to helping you!

reviews.body: Have to make 10 oz or won't work The size ia amazing, I live in a studio and have a small kitchen. The color fits my cookware. But I cannot make a cup less than 10 oz. If I attempt to make anything under 10 oz of coffee, I have to keep putting in water but it won't dispense. I have to unplug and plug in again. I have wasted multiple k cups doing this. Now I have to make a forced 10 oz of coffee everyday. Had this for less than 1 yr when it started doing this. Can't wait to move and upgrade. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 3 years ago Have you tried reaching out to our Consumer Care team regarding your K-Mini coffee maker? reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 9 January, 2020Verified purchase
reviews.body: Disappointed I have been a fan of Keurig since they first came out, so much so that I regularly give them as gifts. I bought this little guy to replace the K50 model in my office based on its compact size and fun color but it has been a big disappointment! From the beginning brews I practically cross my fingers when I hit the button in the hopes that the machine will brew properly. If it happened on the first try I practically did a happy dance. After about a month of once a day brews it quit working and I called Keurig's awesome customer service and the associate walked me through the steps to get it to work again. For the past two months I have had to repeat the procedure every 3rd or 4th brew to get it to work. Today even that isn't working. NO ONE SHOULD HAVE TO SPEND 20 MINUTES MONKEYING AROUND TO BREW A SIMPLE CUP OF COFFEE! I nearly spit when our custodian said the years-old machine I gave her when I bought this one is working flawlessly and did I want it back? Keurig needs to go back to the drawing board on this model. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 6 years ago We understand your frustration and apologize for your inconvenience. Please call our Consumer Care team at 866-901-2739. We will be more than happy to help resolve your issue. reviews.body: Love it!! I just received mine yesterday evening and I have zero complaints. I’ve used it several times and it works perfectly and I’m surprised it makes such a strong cup of coffee without the strong button option. I was a little worried about that, but it’s fine. I have lots of Keurigs at home, at work and in our RV. This little thing is great. I also used my k-cup universal adapter (make sure you take off the 2.0 adapter) and it locks into place in the machine, once you pop out the k-cup holder from the bottom. It come out very easily. Full pours, nice and very hot, I have nothing negative to say about this little machine. reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 20 November, 2018Verified purchase
reviews.body: Not my cup of (tea) coffee I got this August 19th 2018... By October 2 2018 it stopped working! I have had many Keurig's in the past... so I kept the box for about a week. Worked fine, I live in a small condo, can't keep boxes forever no room for that, so I threw it out. So no box, I can't return it to Target and I have now sent two request to Keurig since October 7th... with no response. That may seem like I am not reviewing the brewer, but reviewing the customer service you get with a brand when something does go wrong is just as important as the item itself. I believe anything can go wrong with any item no matter how expensive, but it is how the company handles it that is important. I got the special Target Oasis, to match my kitchen since my other appliances are retro in aqua, it fit right in. The brewer itself, worked great for about a month and half. Then one day the pump started making horrible noises and it took forever for it to get a cup out... and then it only pumped out about half the cup... I think also the heating element is part of the problem, because it is barely warm, my bath water is hotter than the coffee. Just to add to it, it isn't a scaling problem... I use only filtered water and I tried to descale it... even though I thought a month and half shouldn't have been enough time to cause build up, I tried it, because if Keurig would get back to me, that is always their first fix for things. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 7 years ago Thank you for reviewing your coffee maker. reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 5 March, 2020
reviews.body: Great Features!!!! We bought the new coffee maker to replace our existing one which we have used since 2013 and loved. This new one is so much better. It makes the coffee hotter, is slimmer, and finally we can fit our paper cup under it without removing the bottom tray. The main feature is how you add the water. The way you added the water in the old one caused us a lot of problems. My husband would put water in and open the cover to drain the water down. He would walk away and forget that he put the water in and he would pour more water in and then it would overflow and water came out the bottom. This cannot happen in the new one. Thank God!!. The spout does not splash anymore. Being able to have a darker brew is a plus. The press and and brew is another great plus. Just about the greatest revision for this coffee maker that was ever made. Far superior to the old one. We love this one even more. Going back to the original, my husband says that this was the best invention and even better with the new one. Your designers get A pluses from us. Great job in the new design. 1 reply from Consumer Care team - 6 years ago Thank you so much for reviewing your K-Mini Plus and for your kind words. Happy Brewing!

reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 20 January, 2024
reviews.body: I did the set up with just water and no pod. Worked perfectly. Then I tried it with a pod and coffee spewed everywhere. I had a big mess to clean up. I contacted Keurig. The representative went through the troubleshooting process with me, (which I had already done). Several days later I received a different K cup holder. The coffeemaker works perfectly now. The original pod holder has a solid center where it meets the funnel and has 5 openings along the side. The spare holder that I was sent, has about a half inch opening directly in the center where the pod sits on and only one opening along the side. This is the correct one. I have purchased Keurig products for years for myself and as gifts. This was my first bad experience. Keurig makes great coffee, and I will continue to buy this brand. Hopefully, no one else will have this problem. 1 reply from Keurig Consumer Care team - 2 years ago We appreciate your review and apologize for the issue you experienced with your K-Mini coffee maker. Thank you for allowing us the opportunity to assist! If you ever need any further assistance, please do not hesitate to reach out to our Consumer Care team at 866-901-2739.

reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 21 August, 2019Verified purchase
reviews.body: garbage Please heed all the bad reviews! I bought this after my larger Keurig, K-Classic, died after a respectable 5 years of service. I liked the small size and attractive form factor. It made ONE cup of coffee before deciding it was going to act up. Like so many other reviews stated, it makes brewing noises but does not brew, it sucks in water which goes... who knows where, because it doesn't come out (until it does). After fiddling with it for 5+ minutes, and constantly opening and closing the cup pod, you'll be rewarded with a seemingly endless stream of watered down coffee. Since it does not have a setting for coffee size, it will spit out all the water you just dumped down -- I hope you have several cups ready because it won't stop! I am just so disappointed and flabbergasted that this product exists! I went ahead and bought the K-Elite which is a massively better machine. Just avoid this one! 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 7 years ago We understand your frustrations and apologize for any negative experience. reviews.body: Working wonders, but a bit loud. Absolutely LOVE this Keurig. I had a larger model for years and enjoyed it, but was a lot to clean. This one is great, especially with the removable water reservoir and the k-pod storage included. I also really love the color. We needed something to take up less space, and have used it like crazy since we got it. My husband is sick & tested positive with Covid-19 this week, and has used this a ton for tea (something he wouldn't even drink before). Only drawback is that the machine is quite noisy when drawing in the water to heat up, but if you can get over a 30-second noise complaint then it's terrific.

reviews.title: 10 September, 2020
reviews.body: New Keurig user, good so far Bought this last week. Well made nice looking. Bought the Evening Teal color and love it. I do find the instructions rather misleading despite having both pictures and wording to follow. Probably should call the help line. It does make the coffee but not in the expected sequence. I like that there's a container that slides out where you can keep pods. I chose to use a stainless steel re-usable pod and use unbleached paper liners that make clean up easy. I keep those in the container.The machine operates quite quickly- couple of minutes. The reservoir gives options to fill up to 12oz. The front drip-catcher base slides out for taller cups. After my Mr Coffee unit this takes minimal counter space and works for me making just a single cup. 1 reply from Consumer Care team - 6 years ago Welcome to the Keurig family. reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 5 March, 2023
reviews.body: Good - Until it Quits This was a decent single-cup machine for the time I could use it. I used in for 2 years in my classroom, and it received pretty light use in that time too for making tea (less than 7 cups per week). All of a sudden it just stopped brewing. The power comes on but it won't actually start brewing. I come to find out that this is an extremely common issue with Keurig machines. I have more than 6 ounces of water, there's minimal build up. I'm not about to take it apart to adjust the heat sensor or anything either. Do I want to pay a hundred dollars to replace it? No, I do not. For making a cup of tea, heating water in a microwave is just as effective. In addition, I find the pods expensive, not that great quality coffee, and they're an environmental nightmare to boot. Save your money. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 3 years ago Have you tried reaching out to our Consumer Care team regarding your K-Mini coffee maker? reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: 4 October, 2020
reviews.body: A Mini with potential! I purchased my first Mini in August 2018. It lasted until December of that year before it started having issues with the brewing; as many other reviewers have noted, it would only drain a few ounces of the water, then stop. Sometimes, I could unplug it for a minute or two, lift and press down the lever and get it to work, but it was happening with alarming regularity and that’s annoying every time I wanted a cup of coffee. In January of 2019, I purchased a new Mini (at what I consider to be a great price of just sixty dollars) and while it doesn’t happen nearly as often as it did with my first Mini, I still occasionally have issues with the water not draining for the brewing cycle. Because we have well water (with a softener), I use filtered water from my refrigerator in the Keurig. I have found that running a vinegar & water solution through it to descale once every 3-4 months does seem to help. Another odd thing that seems to help is that I unplug my Mini after every use. As of now, I might have an issue with the water draining maybe once a month, but because our kitchen is small and the Mini takes up so little space, I’m willing to deal with the problem to get a fresh, hot cup of coffee (almost) whenever I want it. So, if you can find it on sale and you’re willing to work with it a little, it can be a great little single serve coffee maker! 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 6 years ago Thank you for your review and feedback. If you ever need assistance, please call our Consumer Care team at 866-901-2739, we will be more than happy to help.

reviews.body: AGREE with it being DEFECTIVE Everything that the last reviewer (Michez) is sadly true. It sure as heck isn't at all worth spending my Target earnings towards and I can't imagine one even spending the reg price of $89.99 for it. If I wouldn't have had gotten such ( what I had thought) was a "great deal" on it I would've went forward with returning it. You do have to constantly turn it on turn it off hit the button. Or u do hit the button wait for it to dispense come back later thinking must have filled up my cup just to come back and see nothing has happened and it turned itself off again..And again. And of course once it decides that it finally does want to dispense it does in fact SPLASH EVERYWHERE. My 6 yr old nephew even noticed and said something when we made our first hot chocolate pod in it. "ITS SPLASHING EVERYWHERE!" So ya definite disappointment in this Kerig machine. Quality is horrible. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 2 years ago This is definitely not the experience we want you to have with your K-Mini coffee maker. reviews.body: Not a good product for me The machine makes a lot of noise, even when it’s off it makes a clicking noise. You have to unplug it to make it stop the clicking noise. The quality of the product is not what I expected from a keurig product. I would not buy this product again. 1 reply from Keurig Care team - 5 years ago Thanks for your review. This is considered expected behavior for the K-Mini coffee maker. Even after the brewer has been turned off or unplugged, heat will still be present inside the hot water tank. This heat causes the air to expand and forces it out of the brewer. The noise you are hear is likely air flowing through the hole that regulates brew pressure. Hope this helps! reviews.title: Seller Verified Purchase
reviews.body: Not worth all the problems! I bought this coffee maker because my old Keurig I had for over 8 years eventually got bad. I figured there was no way a more compact and easier to use machine would be a huge upgrade from my old maker but that wasn't the case. This coffee maker splashes coffee outside of the cup when it pours the coffee into the cup during the making process. Not only that but the coffee maker takes FOREVER to make the coffee which is very inconvenient because I usually take one to work every morning and it takes way too long compared to my old machine. Not only this but I work over night shifts and my family sleeps when I get ready for work, so to make things even worse the coffee maker is EXTREMELY LOUD and could possibly wake up the neighbors. All together I think this maker is a huge waste of money and definitely not worth it. reviews.product_name: Keurig K-Mini Single Serve Coffee Maker
reviews.title: Seller Verified Purchase
reviews.body: Would have given it a 5, but had wrong pod holder. I did the set up with just water and no pod. Worked perfectly. Then I tried it with a pod and coffee spewed everywhere. I had a big mess to clean up. I contacted Keurig. The representative went through the troubleshooting process with me, (which I had already done). Several days later I received a different K cup holder. The coffeemaker works perfectly now. The original pod holder has a solid center where it meets the funnel and has 5 openings along the side. The spare holder that I was sent, has about a half inch opening directly in the center where the pod sits on and only one opening along the side. This is the correct one. I have purchased Keurig products for years for myself and as gifts. This was my first bad experience. Keurig makes great coffee, and I will continue to buy this brand. Hopefully, no one else will have this problem. Review from keurig.com reply from Keurig Consumer Care We appreciate your review and apologize for the issue you experienced with your K-Mini coffee maker. Thank you for allowing us the opportunity to assist! If you ever need any further assistance, please do not hesitate to reach out to our Consumer Care team at 866-901-2739.

reviews.title: Works Overnight—A Must‑Have for Surprise Breakouts
reviews.body: I’ve tried a lot of pimple patches over the years, and the Mighty Patch Original truly lives up to the hype. These patches are incredibly easy to use and work best overnight—just apply to clean, dry skin and let them do their thing. By morning, whiteheads are noticeably smaller (sometimes completely gone), redness is reduced, and the gunk is visibly absorbed into the patch. I love that they’re thin, clear, and stay put all night without irritation. They also prevent me from touching or picking at my skin, which is a huge bonus. The 36-count pack is a great value, and I always keep these on hand for surprise breakouts before an important day. Simple, effective, and gentle—this is one skincare product I consistently repurchase. Highly recommend!

reviews.title: Truly Mighty! Best patches we’ve found.
reviews.body: These patches live up to the name! They are hands-down the best pimple patches I’ve used. The versatility and fit are perfect, and they actually stay put. I have very sensitive skin and have had zero issues with irritation, which is a huge win. My daughter uses them too and absolutely loves how effective they are. A must-have in our house! reviews.title: Amazing Pimple Patches - Actually Work!
reviews.body: Effectiveness: These Mighty Patch Original hydrocolloid patches have been absolutely incredible for treating breakouts quickly and effectively without harsh chemicals or painful extraction. The patches work remarkably well overnight, drawing out impurities and reducing the size and inflammation of pimples by morning. I love how they protect blemishes from picking and touching, which prevents further irritation and scarring. The clear design makes them virtually invisible during wear, so I can use them during the day without feeling self-conscious. The patches stick securely without being painful to remove, and they genuinely speed up the healing process compared to leaving breakouts untreated. Quality: Hero Cosmetics has created an exceptional product with these hydrocolloid patches that clearly demonstrates superior manufacturing quality. The adhesive is perfectly balanced - strong enough to stay in place through sleep and daily activities but gentle enough to remove without skin irritation. The hydrocolloid material is high-grade and effectively draws out fluid and impurities without over-drying the surrounding skin. The patches maintain their integrity throughout wear and don't fall apart or lose effectiveness. The sterile packaging ensures product safety and hygiene, which is essential for treating open or inflamed skin. Value for Money: These pimple patches provide outstanding value when you consider how effectively they treat breakouts while preventing scarring and further damage from picking. The cost per patch is reasonable considering the dramatic results they deliver, often clearing blemishes faster than expensive acne treatments. The prevention of scarring alone makes these worth far more than their price, as treating acne scars is much more expensive and time-consuming than preventing them. The convenience and effectiveness make these far more cost-effective than multiple skincare products or dermatologist visits for individual breakouts. These patches have become essential in my skincare routine and consistently deliver impressive results. Highly recommend for anyone dealing with occasional or persistent breakouts who wants fast, effective treatment!

reviews.title: Fast results no irritation
reviews.body: These patches work way better than I expected. I’ve used them on a few breakouts overnight and they noticeably reduce redness and size by morning. They stay on well, even while sleeping, and don’t irritate my skin at all. Super easy to use and honestly a game changer for quick spot treatment. Definitely worth having on hand.

reviews.title: Love this product but not the best for stubborn pimples
reviews.body: The Mighty Patch Original Patch from Hero Cosmetics is hands-down one of the best acne solutions I’ve used. These hydrocolloid stickers actually shrink pimples and whiteheads overnight — I wake up and see a noticeable difference, even on stubborn spots that usually take days to calm down. They’re super easy to use — just stick on before bed and let them work while you sleep. They stay put, even if you accidentally touch your face, and don’t irritate my skin. I also like that they’re practically invisible enough to wear during the day if needed. Great for emergency breakouts or everyday maintenance. For anyone who wants fast, gentle, mess-free results, these Mighty Patches deliver!

reviews.title: A total game changer (where were these when I was a teen?!)
reviews.body: I honestly wish these had been around when I was younger! The Mighty Patch Original is hands-down the most effective way I’ve found to deal with stubborn whiteheads. Instead of picking at my skin and making things worse, I just pop one of these on at night and let it do the work while I sleep. By the morning, you can actually see the gunk it pulled out right on the patch (gross, but strangely satisfying). It significantly reduces the size and redness of the zit in just one use, and because it covers the spot, it stops me from touching it and causing more irritation. The 72-count pack is a great value and lasts quite a while. They stay put all night, even if you toss and turn, and they’re clear enough that you almost forget you’re wearing them. If you’re looking for a stress-free way to clear up your skin overnight, these are a must-have!

reviews.title: A Skincare Staple That Actually Works (For Both of Us!)
reviews.body: Both my husband and I use them, and we’ve consistently seen great results. They also help keep me from picking at my skin, which is a huge bonus. What I really appreciate is that they’re gentle and don’t irritate our skin at all. We both have different skin types, and they’ve worked well for each of us. If you’re on the fence, I’d definitely recommend giving them a try—they’ve become a staple in our household.

reviews.title: A Reliable Overnight Fix for Surprise Breakouts
reviews.body: I’ve tried quite a few pimple patches over the years, and the Mighty Patch™ Original from Hero Cosmetics easily ranks among the more dependable ones. The first thing I appreciated was how simple and mess-free they are to use. You just cleanse your skin, place the patch on the blemish, and forget about it overnight. No creams rubbing off on pillows, no complicated routine. In terms of performance, these patches genuinely do what they promise most of the time. When used on whiteheads or pimples that are close to the surface, I’ve consistently woken up to noticeably reduced redness and a flatter spot. Seeing the patch turn opaque in the morning is oddly satisfying because it clearly shows it has absorbed fluid. It also helps prevent touching or picking at the area, which for me is a big plus and probably helps with faster healing. I also like how discreet they are. Once applied, they stay put through the night and don’t slide off, even if you move around in your sleep. The patches are thin enough that I’ve even worn one during the day at home without it being too noticeable. The reason this lands at four stars instead of five is that results can vary depending on the type of breakout. Deeper, under-the-skin pimples don’t respond as well and often need more than one patch or a different treatment altogether. Additionally, the count goes quickly if you deal with frequent breakouts. Perfect Size and Value for money is a plus. Overall, Mighty Patch™ is a solid, effective solution for targeted acne care and a great tool to keep on hand for sudden flare-ups. reviews.title: Great when used properly!
reviews.body: As a physician, I see pimple patches used incorrectly all the time, so I wanted to share how to get the best results from them. These work best on true whiteheads, not deep cystic acne. Before applying, start with clean skin. If there is a visible whitehead at the surface, proper drainage is key for the patch to work effectively. After cleansing, I gently prep the area with alcohol. When appropriate, a sterile needle can be used to lightly open the whitehead (this should only be done if the head is clearly at the surface). This allows the hydrocolloid patch to actually absorb the contents instead of just sitting on top of the skin. Once prepped, place the pimple patch directly over the area and press gently to seal. Leave it on for several hours or overnight. You’ll know it’s working when the patch turns white as it absorbs fluid. In the morning, the inflammation is noticeably reduced and the area looks flatter and calmer. When used correctly, these patches help protect the spot, reduce redness, and prevent picking—which is huge for healing and minimizing scarring. This brand adheres well, stays in place overnight, and removes easily without irritation. Bottom line: excellent product, but technique matters. Used properly, pimple patches can significantly speed up healing and improve outcomes.

reviews.title: Doesn't replace good hygiene efforts
reviews.body: It's not a miracle patch if that's what people are buying it for. The patch, once skin has been cleansed and dried, adheres well and stays put overnight. The patch does pull out dead white cells but never has it been one patch and done. I am finding that another patch has to go on to the blemish as it's now open and very red. So depending on how big the blemish is, determins how many patches you will need to fully clear up. On a great note: It does help with preveting me from touching it or picking at it which does indeed help the blemish heal faster. I keep a box in my medicine cabinet for really big, very noticable , blemishes, but I don't find it very cost effective nor do I find that my blemishes are cleared up 100% with use. It's really more a matter of: stop touching your face with dirty hands, don't go to bed with makeup on, stop squeezing/popping white heads, invest in a good facial cleanser with proven ingredients. There is no miracle patch. It's an good product for sure, but not a miracle. It will not replace good hygeine and a good cleanser. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Life-Saver for Breakouts — Wish We Had These Years Ago!
reviews.body: My husband and I constantly say we wish Mighty Patch existed when we were in high school. These pimple patches are an absolute game changer. The second I feel a pimple starting to form, I put one on, and it genuinely helps stop it in its tracks. They stay on well overnight, are discreet enough to wear during the day if needed, and actually do what they promise. I travel frequently and do not leave the house without these in my bag. They’ve saved my skin (and my confidence) more times than I can count. Simple, effective, and totally worth it. Highly recommend! They are on subscription delivery in this house.

reviews.title: Stick it on. Sleep it off. Wake up clearer.
reviews.body: I had a stubborn breakout on my right cheek and under my eye that hung around for over a week. I had tried everything! I finally picked up the Mighty Patch™ Original from Hero Cosmetics, and I’m honestly impressed. As soon as it arrived, I slapped a couple patches on before bed. By morning, the breakouts were gone—just a touch of redness left, which was easy to cover with makeup. I also have very sensitive skin and psoriasis, and I had zero irritation or issues using these patches—even under my eye. That alone makes them a win for me. I know people say you can save money by buying a roll of hydrocolloid and cutting it yourself, but let’s be real…I’m lazy 😅 These patches are convenient, easy, and they work. For me, that’s worth it. These also come in invisible/clear patches too! For me, my skin matched these patches, barely could tell I was wearing them. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Pretty impressive!
reviews.body: I had an under-tthe-skin pimple that I foolishly picked at, leaving a pretty ugly scab on my face. I put one of these patches on before going to bed - when I woke and peeled it off, the scab was soft and peeled off of my face without reopening the wound - neat! There was still clearly some pimply goo left that hadn't erupted yet - I was SOOO tempted to pop it, but put another patch on and left it on for about 3 hours. I checked in the mirror and there was a lot of "white" under the patch - pulled that one off, and most of the goo was gone (side note: it was fascinating seeing how it stuck to the used patch - reminded me of those old pore strips)! Just put a 3rd one on and will leave for a few more hours, but I can't get over how much improved it is in just 12 hours. Definitely will keep these in my toolbox! One person found this helpful

reviews.title: 19 April, 2023Verified purchase
reviews.body: Worked with some time I’m 29 with sensitive combination skin and thankfully it’s usually pretty clear, but I had a stubborn patch of acne on my cheek that wasn’t clearing up with just my usual cleansing and moisturizing, so I decided to give these patches a try. I was disappointed the first morning when the spot actually looked worse, but I decided to give it a little longer and after 9 days (wore patches every night except 2), it’s finally almost gone. I don’t think this is a coincidence, since I didn’t really change anything else in my routine. The area was clearly very infected and needed a lot of time to get all the gunk out. So if it seems like the patches aren’t working at first, maybe give them a few days. The only other downside is that they’re smaller than I pictured - I thought they’d be dime-sized but they were even smaller. I ended up needing 2-3 patches/night for my pimple crop. But they didn’t irritate my skin and did the trick eventually! I also appreciated the relatively low price. Picture shows the night before I started using patches and the morning 9 days later. reviews.title: 17 March, 2022Verified purchase
reviews.body: HOLY GRAIL PRODUCT sorry i’m opting out of adding a picture because i find it weird to take a pic of my pimple patch and post it for everyone to see.. haha anyone else? Anyways: If you’re debating on getting these & don’t know if you should try them or not considering the few negative reviews… DO IT. try them out and see the results!!! these are amazing and i don’t know how i was living without them before. also buy multiple boxes if you normally have more than just a pimple here and there. you will not regret this purchase!! you must leave them on for AT LEAST 7-8 hours to see results. and make sure when you stick them on, you press them onto your skin so they stick well and can do their job!! also, these are specifically for white heads, not necessarily acne that is buried under the skin and just looks like a giant bump or black heads or anything else. i’ve used them on my white heads because i started breaking out a lot recently, from giant white heads to a few smaller ones. i went through the whole box in a week and i am obsessed. i’ve worn them during the day and at night (7+ hours each time). as soon as i take them off, i notice my pimples have shrunk immensely. sometimes it takes some white out and the pimple gets even bigger and the white head looks like it’s ready to burst. but i promise you DONT TOUCH IT, use another patch on it and it WILL get it all out. i love these because they don’t ruin your skin, which normally happens when i pick at my acne. i won’t be having any new scars because of these. they really do heal the skin too, when i put them on if my pimple feels “bruised” and is painful to the touch, by the time i take it off it doesn’t hurt at all. it’s also a plus that they get the gunk out of your pores in the area all around the pimple that the patch covers. i’m sorry this review is so long but i truly can’t say enough good things about this product. it is now my holy grail and i have tried all sorts of pimple creams and acne products. reviews.title: 20 March, 2021Verified purchase
reviews.body: Not great for sensitive skin at allll I have mixed feelings about these patches. I do have sensitive skin on my face so that may be part of what caused my issue. I bought them to try to shrink blemishes on my face and help reduce scarring by doing it this way. First, these do work well at diminishing the size of pimples. Larger ones will probably need more than one treatment. However I noticed that when I took the patch off, the skin from the head of the pimple would be stuck to the patch, leaving me with an actual sore on my face instead. At first I thought it was user error, but after it happened several times, I realized this was going to be a consistent problem with these. I definitely was not achieving my goal of reducing acne scarring and this was actually making it much worse. So...if you have sensitive skin, use with caution! If you don’t have any skin issues, these might work totally fine for you. YalleyfromthevalleY quality quality: 5 out of 5 effectiveness effectiveness: 5 out of 5 value

reviews.title: 18 July, 2022Verified purchase
reviews.body: THIS IS LONG BUT TRUST ME: I’ve used these for years so if you want the whole lowdown & an experienced user’s input, read on, my friend! You can watch the results as it works if you look in the mirror every couple of hours, so you will see it really working. It’s fascinating to see the end result / gunk on the sticker when I look in the mirror when I wake up but when I remove the patch there’s no mushy grossness. But! Wash your face of course after using it, whether it’s between reapplying another one, or just if your need has been met and you’re good to go, bc the sticky hydrocolloid residue mark IS there (like when you take a barcode label of a new item or sometimes a bandaid) but it’s NOT HARD to get it off and clean, just wash your face. I use miscellaneous water or witch hazel sometimes but foaming face wash or w/e too. Fyi, They’re about the size of a dime or my finger tip. That said, PRO TIP: I cut them in half on the plastic sheet they come on (and even in quarters if my bump is reallyyyy small tbh) bc often I don’t need as much coverage in one area as the patch but on the occasion I need more, I’ll use a whole one and put a half of one next to it. It depends on how my face depends to make me suffer when I start pms-ing. You get more bang for your buck that way :) *Do note this is only for white heads/open surfaces like AFTER unclogging a pore or even a bit of annoying chin or nose milia - not blackheads or bumps like blocked pores (it won’t do anything if it can’t get to the gunk, so I’m not advocating for popping things on your face since I know the ‘rules’ of face care but…that clog/ingrown hair/etc needs to have an opening before this guy can work it’s magic. TLDR: yes buy it, the claims on the box are true Tyongf value value: 4 out of 5 effectiveness effectiveness: 4 out of 5 quality

reviews.title: 29 August, 2023
reviews.body: Favorite patches--and I've tried a bunch When used correctly, these significantly accelerate healing. It’s a misconception that hydrocolloidal patches suck out gunk. Covering a spot with a patch really just promotes an anaerobic healing environment, keeping the area moist, clean, and covered which in itself promotes better healing and lessens likelihood of bad scarring. The white that appears on the patch is not necessarily “gunk” that’s been sucked out, it’s mostly moisture that the spot has secreted while undergoing the healing process. I typically use these on freshly popped/picked pimples as I’d use a BandAid on a wound, but they also work on whiteheads. For whiteheads, they shrink the breakout and reduce swelling and redness (but again, they don’t really extract gunk). I have used them on deeper cystic acne (doesn’t have extractable gunk) as well to, again, promote a healing environment. The patches are still white in the morning (from moisture, not gunk) and the spot is less red and flatter. Blackheads and closed comedones won’t look much different in the morning and the patch won’t look white when you remove it. The white signifies that the healing process has happened so if you don’t see white, the patch wasn’t used on the right type of spot. I use these on specific spots at night after double cleansing (cleansing balm then gel cleanser), applying hyaluronic acid serum, and applying rx clindamycin gel and rx Tretinoin/niacinamide combo cream to my entire face. I let products dry down and them apply the patches and moisturize around them. I’ve tried a bunch of brands of patches (COSRX, Rael, Starface, Truly, PanOxyl, Peace Out) and these are the thickest and stickiest (they will last over 24hrs). When you remove them, the spot looks less angry and moisturized (they help keep things from getting really scabby/crusty). The longer you can leave them on, the better and more healed the problem spot will look when you remove them. For larger areas of the face (picked areas, cystic areas) I really recommend the large size BandAid brand Hydro Seal bandages. They are just jumbo hydrocolloidal patches that work the exact same way. A popped pimple is a wound and covering wounds (not letting them “air out” like your mom told you to do when you were a kid) is the best, fastest way to let your body heal. jess quality quality: 5 out of 5 effectiveness effectiveness: 5 out of 5 value

reviews.title: 19 August, 2020
reviews.body: Hey bullseye reviewer They work! I was a little skeptical before I tried this, but I was really impressed with these. First of all, I’m pregnant, and these are pregnancy safe. The kind of have the color where they might do this for most skin tones. These patches are translucent with just a hint of color. They seem like they can blend into most skin tones. They blend in very well with my fair skin tone. They are maybe a little more noticeable on the edge of my chin than if it were somewhere else but really just because of the angle. They adhere to the skin very well. They recommend you wear them for at least 4 hours. I wore them overnight. I thought I might find them on my pillow in the morning, but they didn’t budge! The pimples were about significantly reduced the next morning. You’ll have a big white spot though where the pimple material was pulled out so the patches do become more noticeable as they’re working. I’ve used these on pimples where you don’t see the whitehead yet, and it pulled out a significant amount of material. It, also, took out some adjacent blackheads. I’ll be buying more of these when I run out!

reviews.title: 12 August, 2020
reviews.body: Hey bullseye reviewer Works Like a Charm I was so excited to try the Mighty Patch because I’d heard great things about it from some friends. It was super easy to figure out, and very comfortable. I’ve used it at night and during the day when I wouldn’t be seeing anyone and it didn’t bug or irritate me at all to have it on. The best part is that when I took the patch off, there was a noticeable difference in my zit. Both times I’ve tried it it has made the zit smaller than before! I would recommend this product to others trying to reduce the appearance of a zit, with the warning that it does not make the zit disappear completely and only worked well on zits that already had heads.

reviews.title: 9 August, 2020
reviews.body: Hey bullseye reviewer Works great I received the Hero Cosmetics Mighty Patch Original Acne Patches which come in a small box of 24. I have never used acne patches before so I thought these were really interesting. The box comes with two piece of film in which you peel off the patch. They are really small, probably about the size of a pinky nail. You then clean the skin and apply the patch to the affected area (white head). You leave the patch on until it turns white which is about 4-6 hours. The patch contains hydrocolloid which helps draw out the pus and nasty stuff. I applied this at night before bed and woke up to my blemish being much less noticeable. It wasn’t red or raised anymore, which was awesome! The other thing I like about these are, they’re not very noticeable when you wear them. You could easily wear these out in public if you need to cover up a blemish. Overall, I am very happy I found these. I would recommend them to anyone who has issues with annoying whiteheads or breakouts.
