reviews.title: Count: 24
reviews.body: Works perfectly, wish there was some more instructions when it came to how often you should use but I guess it's up to your discretion. The area of effect is large so for the small pimples I cut them in half and even forths.

reviews.title: Perfect water bottle for my 6-year-old!
reviews.body: I got this for my 6-year-old and it’s been amazing so far. It’s very easy for him to use, and I like that he can sip or use the straw. The bottle keeps his water cold for hours, which is perfect for school and outings. The lid closes securely with a click and hasn’t leaked in his backpack, which I really appreciate. It also opens easily for him without help. It’s sturdy, the size is just right for kids, and the design is really nice. Overall, great quality and definitely worth it!

reviews.title: Leak-Proof and Super Convenient — One of the Best Water Bottles I’ve Used
reviews.body: I’ve been using the Owala FreeSip 24 oz Water Bottle, and it’s honestly one of the best water bottles I’ve owned. The biggest thing for me is that it’s completely leak-proof. I’ve tossed it in bags, carried it around all day, and never had an issue with spills. That alone makes it worth it. The FreeSip design is also really convenient—you can sip through the built-in straw or tilt it back to drink normally, which I didn’t think I’d care about at first but now really appreciate. It keeps water cold for a long time, and the size is perfect for daily use, travel, or the gym. The build quality feels solid, and the Shy Marshmallow color looks great. Overall, this is a well-designed, reliable water bottle that just works. If you want something durable, leak-proof, and easy to use, this is a great choice. Highly recommended.

reviews.title: Product does exactly as they advertise! My bottle kept my water cold for 120 hours!
reviews.body: We were traveling on Thursday, April 9 and flew out of Charlotte, NC. I filled my Owala with cold tap water at home and drove 2.5 hours to Charlotte Airport. I chose to leave my Owala in the car, couldn’t take the chance of TSA keeping my Owala. We returned on Monday, April 13. We got in the car and it was very hot inside. I took a sip from my Owala and it was still cold! Not as cold as when we left but cold! Your ad says “stays cold or hot for 25 hours!” You can change that to 120 hours! I love the features of the Owala. The colors are so fun, the straw feature to sip from is awesome or the spout is convenient as well. I love the carry handle and the push button release is excellent! I bought two. One for me and one for myself. We LOVE them and would highly recommend the Owala for anyone who has the desire and an awesome water bottle! Thank you Owala for taking such care to create a wonderful product! Lisa Gorum

reviews.title: A Great water bottle that simply does it all.
reviews.body: I was looking for a new water bottle and saw this product mentioned on a travel blogger video. I have been very impressed with it. Start out with the fact that its just an attractive product. It's shape allows for comfortable carrying. I love that it supports both straw sipping and tip drinking. There are times (like on an airplane) where straw drinking is preferred and times (like at the gym) where I prefer to tip the bottle and drink that way. This bottle seamlessly supports both. And it keeps water cold. The lid doesn't leak, so you could carry it upside down without making a mess. I would highly recommend it.

reviews.title: Great Bottle
reviews.body: Best water bottle!! Love the shape - fits around my hand and design makes for a comfortable grip. I like the choice between the straw and pour spout. Lid stays closed and has easy push button to open. Keeps water cold and ice takes awhile to melt. Plus the choice in colors!!! Highly recommend

reviews.title: Great Everyday Bottle with Smart Design and All-Day Insulation
reviews.body: This bottle is the perfect size for everyday use—not too bulky but still holds plenty of water to keep me hydrated throughout the day. One of my favorite features is the sip vs. swig option. It’s so convenient to be able to drink through the straw or take a bigger gulp depending on what I need. It also fits easily in standard cup holders, which makes it great for commuting or traveling. And the insulation is seriously impressive—my drinks stay cold for well over 8 hours, often much longer. Overall, this is a super practical, well-designed bottle that I find myself reaching for every day!

reviews.title: Very nice color and bottle
reviews.body: The owala is truly wonderful and useful. Contrary to popular belief the Stanley still ties this with 1st place in the “water bottle race”. Seriously I think the Stanley is good for people who are in office jobs or who are staying in one place a lot and the owala is for people who move around at their job and need a water bottle with a top that is secure. An athlete might want an owala to throw in their bag and run with so that they can have the peace of mind knowing that it’s not spilling in their bag. A secretary at a school might want the Stanley because it looks very modern and sharp when sitting on an office desk, and also it has a straw that you can drink out of at work when your thirsty. I personally have both the Stanley and the owala, and I use the both interchangeably whenever I need each one. If I’m taking a trip I take the owala, if I’m sitting at home or in an office I use the Stanley.

reviews.title: Best water bottle on the market
reviews.body: Owala water bottles are my favorite. I've had mine for 2 years now. Just bought this one for my 8 year old daughter. Love the dual option for sipping with straw or gulping. I've dropped mine and banged it up. I do have a few dents in the bottom of it but the paint has stayed in tact. It is very durable and easy to carry around with the handle. Some handles break quick but this one has no issues. I have the 24 oz which is a perfect size for me, fits in the car cup holder. I don't drink my water with ice but my daughter does and the ice will hold for several hours before melting.

reviews.title: Total Convert — Perfect Size, No Leaks, and Incredible Value
reviews.body: I bought this after using 40 oz Stanley cups for a long time, and I’m officially a convert. I now have several of these and use them daily — one for water, one for coffee, one for tea, and even a few for gifting. The size is one of the best features. The 24 oz feels much more practical for everyday use, and it actually fits securely in my car’s cup holder without tipping — which is a huge plus. The quality is excellent. The stainless steel feels durable and well-made, and the design is very user-friendly. The FreeSip feature makes it easy to drink either through the straw or directly, depending on what you prefer. There’s no funky taste at all, which is something I’m very particular about with bottles like this. There was a slight packaging smell when it first arrived, but it disappeared immediately after a quick wash. It’s completely leakproof in my experience, which makes it easy to carry in a bag without worrying. The texture has a nice, smooth finish, and the colors are honestly beautiful — they make it fun to collect more than one. Everything arrived quickly and in perfect condition. For the price, the value here is outstanding — especially compared to other brands I’ve used. This has easily become my go-to water bottle. The handle and lid opening are just phenomenal 🥰 #AmazonFinds #Chihuahuamom #Dobermanmom #Owala #WaterBottle #Hydration #EverydayEssentials #OnTheGo #ReusableBottle #HealthyHabits #NoSpill #TravelEssentials #Drinkware #AmazonFine

reviews.title: Game Changing Built-In Straw
reviews.body: It's all about the clever built-in straw! I no longer have to tip my water bottle upside down while swigging for the last drop. I spend a lot of time outdoors for work and to stay hydrated I've tried all kinds of water bottles and coolers. I can't believe how a built-in straw is such a simple solution to sipping small amounts of cool water continuously rather than in large amounts at once. Now sure yet if it will keep my water cold up to 24 hours since it's not quite summer, but I will still be 5-star happy if it will keep my water cool for an 8-9 hour work day. So far, I love my new, brightly colorful ("Summer Sweetness" color) Owala water bottle with the game-changing built-in straw that allows me to sip my cool water! Steady sipping small amounts throughout the day is better for your health than chugging only when thirsty ... it improves hydration efficiency, prevents overwhelming the kidneys, and supports better digestion. The 24 oz is slightly bigger than my Contigo coffee travel mug, but still fits my SUV cup holder. And when filled it's still easy to carry around with the loop. It appears worth the price.

reviews.title: Best Water Bottle I’ve Bought — And It Fits My iColor
reviews.body: I’ve bought a lot of water bottles over the years, and while most of them were just “okay,” this one is by far the best. The Owala FreeSip is easy to carry, keeps my drinks hot or cold, and its design makes it simple to drink however I want — with a straw or by tilting. Now I can fill it with hot coffee at night before bed and when I wake up in the morning, my coffee is still hot and on my nightstand! The biggest win for me is that it actually fits in my iColor, which comes in very handy when I don't want to carry my purse! That alone makes it a favorite. The quality feels solid, the color is beautiful, and it’s become the one I reach for every day. Highly recommend this one.

reviews.title: Thoughtful design, easy to drink from
reviews.body: This water bottle is really well designed and makes staying hydrated easy. The FreeSip feature is super convenient, letting you sip through the straw or tilt to drink, depending on what you prefer. The size feels just right for daily use, not too bulky but still holds a good amount. It keeps water tasting fresh with no weird odor or metallic aftertaste, which is a big plus. The seal is secure and truly leak proof, so it travels well in bags without worry. It feels stable when set down and doesn’t tip easily, and the overall build quality feels durable. It’s easy to use, easy to clean, and has become one of those everyday essentials you end up reaching for without thinking.

reviews.title: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Game changer for busy moms & active kids
reviews.body: I didn’t expect to love a kids water bottle this much, but here we are 🙌 The Owala Kids FreeSip Insulated Stainless Steel Water Bottle 16 oz Celestial Sphere is hands down the BEST water bottle we’ve tried. My kid is obsessed—and honestly, so am I. ✔️ FreeSip design = genius They can sip through the straw OR tilt and chug like a regular bottle (no spills, no drama). Perfect for school, sports, and car rides. ✔️ Actually leak-proof I’ve thrown this in bags, left it rolling around the car, and NOT ONE leak. That alone deserves 5 stars. ✔️ Keeps drinks cold forever We’re talking ice still in there hours later—even after a full day out. ✔️ Easy for kids to use The button pop-open lid is smooth but secure, and the built-in carry loop is perfect for little hands. ✔️ Durable + cute The Celestial Sphere color is adorable and it holds up to real life (aka drops, chaos, and everything in between). As a mom constantly on the go, I need things that just work—and this is one of them. Already planning to buy more so we always have one clean and ready. 10/10 recommend. Worth every penny. 💧✨

reviews.title: Sturdy daily driver for the office
reviews.body: I’ve been taking this to work every day for a while now, and it’s held up perfectly. It’s built like a tank and doesn’t feel like it’s going to dent or scratch just from being moved around. The size is exactly what I was looking for—plenty of capacity so I’m not constantly heading to the breakroom to refill it, but it still fits where it needs to. The "FreeSip" design is actually pretty clever in practice; being able to choose between the straw or just swigging from it depending on the situation is a nice touch. It keeps water cold throughout the entire shift, and the lid mechanism feels solid, not like the cheap plastic hinges that usually snap off after a month. If you need a reliable, high-capacity bottle for daily use that can take a bit of a beating, this one is an easy choice. It’s simple, functional, and does exactly what it's supposed to do.

reviews.title: Near-Perfect Water Bottle
reviews.body: This Owala water bottle is about as close to perfect as it gets! The stainless steel design feels durable and keeps drinks at the right temperature, and best of all, it doesn’t leak (a must-have, especially for kids’ backpacks). I love the one-handed push-button lid. It makes it super easy to open and close on the go. The carrying handle is also really convenient for travel or everyday use. One of my favorite features is that the straw is removable, which is great if you’re like me and don’t enjoy the extra effort of cleaning straws. The lid being dishwasher safe is another big win and makes cleanup simple. The only downside is that the bottle itself is recommended for hand wash only, which isn’t quite as convenient. Honestly, it’s a small trade-off for such a well-designed product. I loved it so much I bought one for each of my kids’ Easter baskets. Highly recommend!

reviews.title: Perfect Bigger Size for Active Kids – Leak-Proof & Ice-Cold All Day in Texas Heat!
reviews.body: Picked up this Owala Kids FreeSip in 24 oz (insulated stainless steel) for my kid because the smaller 16 oz ones ran out too fast during school days and our hot Texas adventures. Needed something that holds more water without sacrificing the awesome FreeSip features we love—no spills, easy sipping, and great insulation. What stands out (Pros): Huge capacity with great insulation: 24 oz means fewer refills, and it keeps drinks ice-cold for 12–24 hours easy (ice still there after a full day outside in 90+°F Texas heat—no sweat on the bottle either). Leak-proof magic: The push-button lid with lock/carry loop is foolproof—tossed in backpacks, car seats, or dropped, zero leaks. The lock is a game-changer for preventing accidental opens. FreeSip spout is kid-approved: Upright straw sipping for low-effort drinks (great for little ones or on the go), or tilt-to-chug for bigger gulps. Smooth flow, no hard sucking, and the wide mouth makes adding ice or cleaning straightforward. Durable build: The stainless steel feels tough—handles drops and rough play. BPA-free, cup-holder friendly base fits in car holders too. Minor downsides: Heavier and taller than the 16 oz version—fine for school-age kids or older, but might be a bit much for tiny hands all day. Higher price point than basic bottles, but the no-leak reliability and long-lasting cold make it worth every penny—no more soggy backpacks or wasted water. We've been using it daily for a few weeks, through school, playground runs, and road trips. It's exceeded what we hoped for—reliable hydration without the mess. If your kid needs more volume than the standard kids' 16 oz but still wants the kid-friendly FreeSip design, this 24 oz is spot-on. Highly recommend—easy 5/5 stars!

reviews.title: The lid broke after falling off a table (it took a few months to a year but still)
reviews.body: I love this water bottle so much that I'm a little biased, so it's the only reason it's getting four stars and not three for this one issue. In a two year time span, I have now had to buy two replacement Owalas because the lid broke simply from falling off a table. It is very very durable, but I assume it can only take so many falls or it sometimes falls the wrong way? Who knows. Without this problem I'd rate this five stars any day; my family has pointed out that I sounded like a walking advertisement when I first explained just how good it is (but it worked on a couple of them!).

reviews.title: The only water bottles for my family!
reviews.body: I love these water bottles! Between the functionality and ease of use (especially one handed), thoughtful design, effortless washability, and beautiful colorways. This is my new favorite water bottle brand! I have the 24oz in a couple colorways and a few of the kid sized bottles (I think 16oz). They're all great! I appreciate that the lids come apart and are easy to clean. We do hand wash the bottles though. I also really appreciate the shape of the bottles and the finishes. Both make it comfortable to hold with my small hands and even the smooth finish has something about it that keeps it from being too slick to hold. The ergonomic mouthpiece is a welcome comfort and my kiddo and I both love that you can drink two different ways; one wide-mouth, one straw. I will say to be gentle with the handles, as they seem to be a weak point and I have experienced the occasional weird condensation on the lid. So much so that one time, I thought the bottle was leaking. Turns out, my water was just extremely cold. I've had many water bottles over the years and I have found that not only does this brand keep water cold, it seems to make it colder if you have ice in there. I've never had that happen. I honestly can't say enough about these water bottles. Just get yourself one and see for yourself!

reviews.title: So much more than a water bottle – truly a lifesaver for me
reviews.body: I originally bought this Owala bottle during my pregnancy after being diagnosed with a hematoma. I wasn’t supposed to make much effort, and even something as simple as tilting my head back to drink from a regular bottle felt like too much. This bottle made a huge difference. The built-in straw meant I could just sip without any effort at all, and that alone made staying hydrated so much easier during a really difficult time. Fast forward to now—over 2 years later—and I’m still using the same one. It has held up incredibly well and still works perfectly. I loved it so much that I had to buy one for my husband too because he kept using mine… and now even my toddler wants to drink from it all the time 😂 It’s durable, super convenient, and honestly one of the best purchases I’ve made. Highly recommend!

reviews.title: My most favorite water bottle ever! Best "straw" design!
reviews.body: It's hard to get a five-star review from me, but Owala FreeSip has gone above and beyond expectations! I LOVE this water bottle. I'm not a fan of some color combinations, but the dark black one is exactly what I need for my concerts. I love the easy sip part so much, I had to get a second one for my every day use. (I ordered Black Cherry, for me, which I also love, and Denim, for my husband which I we both love. I wish I could build my own color combo!) No regrets here. I'll be ordering more as gifts! **I use off-brand silicone boots on them for protection and grip.**

reviews.title: Great Bottle for Everyday Use
reviews.body: I really like this water bottle. It keeps my drink cold, feels sturdy, and is easy to carry with me throughout the day. The FreeSip lid is convenient because I can either sip through the straw or tilt it back like a regular bottle. It is also easy to use while driving, at work, or on the go. The size is practical, the design looks nice, and it encourages me to drink more water. Highly recommend if you want a good insulated bottle that is simple, durable, and convenient.

reviews.title: I Hate to Admit I Love It!
reviews.body: I usually try to resist what’s trendy, but I decided to give these water bottles a try after getting frustrated with my old go-to water bottle leaking. These are honestly super great quality and I love that you have the option to drink through the reusable straw, or tip it back like a regular water bottle. I haven’t had issues with leaking and it’s holding up great being washed in the dishwasher. This one is too big to fit in my car cup holder or backpack, so this is my at home water bottle. I bought a slimmer Owala for work/travel, as well one of their to-go coffee cups. I love them all! Kind of expensive, but the quality has made it worth the purchase in my opinion.

reviews.title: Perfect insulated bottle no matter the drink preference.
reviews.body: I don't believe in the cleanliness of straws for any of the bottles or cups out there. I tend to drink out of bottles without the straws. It was sometimes tedious before I found this brand. While it does have a straw which I'll keep (in a drawer)it also has a lid that's both easy to drink from and easy to lock and unlock with one hand. I've noticed the finish on these bottles is also very durable. I have a cabinet full of Stanley bottles, a few are fine, but nothing like the Owala bottles. I have two, so far, but I tell everyone I know that this "FreeSip" lid is definitely my favorite. Hopefully I'll get some more. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: A must have!
reviews.body: Owala bottles have been the best bottle purchase for me on the market. They are super durable and keep your liquids cool or hot for long periods of time. The 32oz bottles are my favorite because they hold enough water for any activity and hardly any spillage outside of my own flows. It is heavy duty steel but light weight for carrying over long journeys. However, the 24oz bottles are better for hiking bags if you choose to purchase for hikes. I also love the different colors they come in. This had a very smooth surface and works well with every day life.

reviews.title: Damaged
reviews.body: I have multiple Owala bottles and love them. The product is wonderful but the bottle came damaged with a dent on the bottom. Edit- I returned the Owala and received a new one. They packaged in a box with the proper padding. It came in perfect! I changed my review from one star to 5, since it was a packaging and delivery issue, and not a product issue.

reviews.title: Best bottle for an Amazing Price
reviews.body: My favorite water bottle I’ve had yet, no leaks no problems other than the handling breaking which was easily replaceable. My 40oz bottle helps me push atleast 120fl oz of water in a day. They offer a great bottle at an affordable price, in addition to this they also sell replacement lids if yours ever breaks. Thanks Owalla Product Team for creating a great bottle!

reviews.title: Legit and perfect!
reviews.body: The 24oz is perfect for me, that being said it does not fit in most cupholders. Easy to drink out of and the seal is tight! The ice I put in my water bottle usually lasts half the day or the entire day depending on the amount of ice I put in and how much direct sunlight or heat the bottle receives. It is not super heavy even when filled with water. Definitely love my Owala!

reviews.title: Long lasting cold drinks
reviews.body: It's pretty well known for a reason! This size is so perfect to just have next to you in your desk. Definitely not a size I would choose to carry around, but I suppose it depends on what you need it for. I've read that you can keep ice in this for pretty long, and though I've never tried that, it certainly does keep drinks cold enough through the night. It's pretty sturdy too, I've dropped it a bunch of times and obviously it does dent, but it doesn't seem to change the stability at all. Great purchase!

reviews.title: Very Good Quality
reviews.body: I bought myself one of these locally in the white then my husband wanted one but in another color. Thank you Amazon! He chose the charcoal one and we both love them! The spill-proof feature was probably the biggest selling point for us AND the fact that you can hang them from a hook or whatever in your car. It holds 32 ounces - perfect! And the straw feature!!!! LOVE IT! You have the option of holding it straight and drinking out of the straw OR tipping & sipping for a larger gulp, your choice. CON's: (1) I don't believe it keeps the liquids cold as long as it says, and (2) It is not for hot liquids or "carbonated" liquids which is what my husband mostly drinks. Our way around that is by not locking down the lid so it doesn't build up pressure. They're extremely easy to clean with their wide mouths and we're very happy with our bottles.

reviews.title: Great design + actually keeps water cold all day
reviews.body: Everyone in our family now has their own Owala! We've tried a lot of water bottles over the years, and this one stands out for a few reasons. - Comes in lots of color combos, so each family member has a different Owala and we know which one to grab on our way out the door! - Sturdy, won't break when dropped. -Lid seals tight, so your beverage won't spill out into your bag. -Fits in our car cup holders which is a major bonus. The BEST feature is the FreeSip lid—it gives you the option to either sip through the built-in straw or tilt it back and drink from the spout. It simple, and it’s super convenient depending on what you’re doing (walking, driving, working out, etc.).

reviews.title: Best water bottle!
reviews.body: I wasn’t sure I wanted to pay $30 for a water bottle. I finally decided to just do it and am glad I did! The color on this bottle is beautiful! It’s the best water bottle I’ve ever had. I love the free sip feature, along with it being leak proof. It definitely keeps liquid cold. I filled it with water and 4 ice cubes at 9am. 4 hours later there is still a cube floating around. Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Please buy!
reviews.body: Owala’s are probably one of the best water bottles out there, and I totally understand why they’re so popular. It keeps your water cold, and very scratch resistant (I drop things like crazy and my owala is one of the victims lol). For a $30 water bottle, I would definitely get one of theses.

reviews.title: No Metal Taste and Stays Cold For Hours
reviews.body: This bottle is amazing, it keeps water cold for many, many hours. I'm very picky about water bottles because I don't like when I can taste anything like plastic or metal but there is no taste at all. I'm also picky about drinking cold water, if it becomes room-temp I don't like it anymore so I don't drink as much water as I should but this one keeps it cold for so long that I find myself drinking water much more consistently than before. It's a little heavy once it's filled with water but it's worth it.

reviews.title: The perfect water bottle!
reviews.body: Owalas are elite water bottles! If you’re on the fence, definitely do it. They have the straw option or the open option to drink if you want to chug. I got the 24oz and it’s the perfect size for cup holders. I also love the gray color! It’s really subdued compared to a lot of the other colors, but I like it. I usually put about 1/3 full of ice and then fill the rest with water before bed and in the morning there’s still ice in it! Easy to clean and no spillage. Love it!!

reviews.title: Good brand, good bootle
reviews.body: I used different brands before until someone recommended this one to me. I gave it a try and after a couple of weeks using it - I like. Keeps it cool and it doesn't leek. That's the most important thing. Some weird color options but the plain white one is nice.

reviews.title: Absolutely love these!!!
reviews.body: Multiple purchases of this product. Love it. Sturdy. Keeps my water cool all day. Multiple sizes available. You can sip through a straw or you can use it to gulp. Love it! Normally use CamelBak as they used to always have parts to trade out if anything broke. Switched over to these and love them! Still have my CamelBaks but ...

reviews.title: Love it! The color is bomb! 🔥🔥
reviews.body: I love it! It's a really pretty color! The size is perfect to make sure i'm getting enough water intake. Its durable and I like that there is a lock over the lid. So convenient that there is a straw. I'm glad that I finally invested in one. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: obsessed
reviews.body: The whole thing about being able to choose whether you use the straw or tilt it to chug is awesome and all, but mainly I was scouring the internet for a bottle that had some kind of straw mechanism that wasn't (1) on a spring-loaded flippy thing that would eventually wear out and stop working, (2) wasn't open to the air where it could collect other peoples' geeeeerms when I took it around everywhere with me, and (3) didn't have some kind of screw-top or other cap that I wouldn't be able to open one-handed. DO YOU KNOW HOW HARD THAT IS TO FIND? I do. Literally, Owala was the only brand/bottle I found that checked all three of those boxes. THE ONLY ONE. So even though it was a little more than I would normally pay for a water bottle and I hated that I couldn't customize my colors, I decided to give it a try. And lo, I found the light and the heavens blessed me with a (near) perfect water bottle. I've used my bottle pretty much all day every day since purchasing it several months ago and it works as well now as it did when I first bought it. I do worry about the spring-loaded lid release eventually wearing out, but it hasn't yet with constant use (and more than occasional extra use for funsies). The only sign of wear over time is that there are a few small (aesthetic/surface) scratches on the outside from a couple times when I dropped my bottle on concrete and asphalt. I wish they weren't there but it doesn't affect anything except my delicate eyeballs. My only qualm with the bottle's near perfection, which seems entirely unavoidable from a design perspective, is that ice cubes inside of the bottle make a lot of noise when sloshing around and bumping up against the metal interior. When you work a job that sometimes requires near-complete silence, that noise can be an issue. For most, it probably wouldn't matter in the slightest (when I'm at home, I don't even think about it). I don't blame Owala for this. But if they solved it, then it would be the holy grail of sheer perfection designed to hold ambrosia for the gods. Until then, I feel honored to partake. The handle is convenient for carrying. The shape of the bottle (flat on the sides) is a clever touch that makes it easier to grip for twisting off the top and such. It fits in average cup holders. It's leakproof when the lid is secured well and the cap is clicked into place. The button to open the cap is strong (works well) and is easy to press. If the handle is down (it gently clicks into place if you fold it down), it covers the button and prevents any accidental presses. That said, while it could theoretically be pressed accidentally if you leave the handle up/open all the time (like I do), it has never happened to me. The inside is metal, which keeps the insides cold for ages (with ice). The straw is plastic, which stops the water from tasting like gross metal water bottle. The outside is... not-metal, which means the sun can be directly on the bottle and warm the outside while the inside is still ice cold and, more importantly, that there's no condensation on the outside getting everything around it wet (bane of my college existence :| ). Vinyl stickers adhere well to whatever the outside material is. It disassembles into straw, lid, and bottle for cleaning, and the the shape of the lid makes it a lot easier (and way more sanitary) to clean than any other straw-based bottle I've had/used. I run the lid and straw through the dishwasher occasionally. I think the bottle recommends hand washing but I forget; in addition to hand washing, I occasionally do a polydent cleaning to eliminate any odors and ensure all bacteria are dead af. It works like a charm. This is a 100% honest review and I generally try not to fangirl or gush excessively: this bottle is /that/ good. I legit recommend it for anyone looking for a water bottle (unless they prefer the big mouth/screw-top kind) and am very seriously considering buying one for everyone in my family.

reviews.title: Keeps drinks cold even on super hot days
reviews.body: This is my go to water bottle for everyday use. It keeps my water cold all day even when it’s in my hot car in the summer. It’s slim enough to fit in my cup holder which is even more convenient. This is a leakproof water bottle so when I throw it in my bag I don’t have to worry about it leaking. I got the white one and I love how simple and classy it this color is. It has held up well through drops and is easy to carry with the pop up handle it has on the lid.

reviews.title: YES YES YES
reviews.body: ⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ I honestly don’t think I’ll ever use another water bottle again after getting my Owala. The ergonomic mouthpiece makes it so comfortable and easy to drink from, and I’ve noticed I actually drink way more water throughout the day because of it. I actually left my Owala at church for a few days and had to use a different water bottle, and I genuinely hated it. I didn’t even want to drink water out of it because it just wasn’t the same. It also keeps water cold for up to 24 hours. If you put ice in it, the ice stays frozen for a really long time. I’ve never actually had water sit in it that long because I drink it all, but every time I add ice it stays cold for hours and hours. The design, insulation, and drinking spout just make it an amazing bottle. After using it, every other water bottle feels like a downgrade. 💧

reviews.title: Hiding that lipstick in style!
reviews.body: I freaking love this thing. I'm not a trend person, I go for function. And when my STB SIL got my fiancé a shiney new Stanley I decided it was time to replace my beat up Rtic with the mismatch lid and straw. I may not follow trends, but I didn't want my fiancé's cup to put mine to absolute shame! First off, can we all agree that the marshmallow is gorgeous? THIS cup is spectacular. Strange to say that about a water bottle, I know. But since I never jumped on that train, I didn't know what I had been missing out on. Let me tell you what! The most important things to me is taking care of my service dog. She keeps me safe so her care is paramount to me. This enables me to pour my water into her bowl on the fly, without having to open my bottle past what I would do to drink from it. Quick and easy. That right there was the selling point for me. But as it turns out, there was even more benefit than that. I don't chug my water but if I did, the chug hole does works fine. The straw portion is ready to sip from, and it doesn't stick up tall like other water bottle straws. It's JUST tall enough to get the job done. No worry of toppling over because the straw got knocked over by the cat. (Because that happens.) The "handle", which is generally the locking feature unless you're using it as a handle, is well placed and comfortable for my sized hands. Not to small and not clunky and large. STB SIL likes to talk about how great the Stanley handle is, which I don't really get. I like that this doesn't have a handle. I have one to carry it if I need it, and not if I don't. The seal is completely water tight. I occasionally put a drink mix in it and shake vigorously. No leaks whatsoever. In fact... It fits in a car cup holder upside down. That's how I transport it in the car. It doesn't fit butt down, so I put it top down. I've been known to do this with various bottles, so I tried it with this one and it fits ok. It's not ideal but it's better than nothing. Try it in yours! Since it's completely leak proof there's are no worries about spilling. (Just be careful when you remove it that it doesn't unlatch.) Another bonus of the covered straw is that germs don't get in the straw. Perfect because people in public often don't cover their sneezes. Ugh. And the coolest part? Lipstick messes are concealed with a flip of the lid! Easy peasy! I have noticed that the inside of the straw stains a little, which was disappointing. I don't like that I look at it and it looks dirty when it isn't. But I guess I shouldn't have used drink enhancers then. Also, my top color is very light. Other colors might not show that. Another thing that want fun was that when it fell, it dented! Oh no! So beware of that. It keeps my water super cold and I don't go anywhere without it. I love it so much that I'm thinking of getting another so I don't have to limit myself by style! Definitely worth the price since I use it constantly, every day. This one is so chill and Zen... The next one shall be colorful and crazy! One for every mood! I simply can't say enough about this darn water bottle... Does that mean I'm on trend now? Who knows! I just love my Owalla! I'm definitely a fan!

reviews.title: Perfect for Kids and Adults — My 12-Year-Old Loves Her Owala FreeSip!
reviews.body: I bought the Owala FreeSip Insulated Stainless Steel Water Bottle (24 oz) for my 12-year-old daughter, and it has been a total win. She absolutely loves it, and I do too—for a lot of good reasons. If you’re looking for a high-quality water bottle that actually makes hydration easy and fun (yes, that’s possible), this is the one. Thoughtful Design & Amazing Sip Options The feature that really makes this bottle stand out is the FreeSip lid. It has both a built-in straw and a wide-mouth opening, so you can drink two different ways. My daughter loves using the straw when she’s on the go, and when she’s really thirsty or refilling at home, she just tilts it back and chugs like a regular bottle. It’s a small thing, but it makes a big difference in how often she actually uses the bottle. She even mentioned it feels “cooler” to drink from than her old one. Perfect Size for School and Sports The 24 oz size is ideal for a kid. It holds enough water to get through most of the school day or a sports practice without being bulky or too heavy. It fits easily into backpack pockets, cup holders, and even the side pouch of her dance bag. She’s taken it to school, dance class, and weekend outings, and it’s always the first thing she grabs when heading out the door. Keeps Water Cold All Day This bottle really performs when it comes to insulation. It keeps water cold for hours—literally from morning until late afternoon, even on warm days. My daughter actually gets excited to take a sip and feel how cold the water still is. That’s a big improvement from some of the cheaper plastic bottles we’ve used in the past. Durable and Kid-Proof Kids drop things. A lot. This bottle has held up incredibly well. The stainless steel body is sturdy and the powder-coated finish hasn’t chipped, even after being tossed into lockers and dropped a few times. It still looks nearly brand new. The lid closes securely and hasn’t leaked once, even when it’s rolling around in a backpack. Cute Colors That Kids Love Owala really nailed it with their color choices. We got the “Shy Marshmallow” (pastel blue and peach), and it’s adorable without being too childish. The color combo is one of my daughter’s favorite things about the bottle—it feels stylish and fun, which means she actually wants to carry it around. Easy to Clean The lid design might look complex at first, but it’s surprisingly easy to take apart and clean. We usually rinse it by hand, but the bottle is dishwasher safe on the top rack, which is a huge bonus. There’s no weird plastic smell or funky taste either, which has been an issue with some other bottles we’ve tried. ⸻ Pros: • Dual-function FreeSip lid (straw + wide-mouth) is super convenient • Excellent insulation keeps water cold for hours • Kid-friendly design that’s durable and fits in backpacks/cup holders • Leak-proof lid means no spills in school bags • Fun, stylish color combos kids actually like to use • Dishwasher safe and easy to clean • Comfortable size and weight for younger users Cons: • Lid handle is a bit small for adult hands (not an issue for kids) • Straw can take a little getting used to at first (minor learning curve) ⸻ Final Thoughts The Owala FreeSip Water Bottle is one of the best purchases I’ve made for my daughter. She drinks more water without reminders, loves showing it off to friends, and it’s held up to daily use like a champ. Whether it’s for school, sports, or weekend outings, this bottle goes everywhere with her. If you’re looking for something functional, fun, and built to last, this is absolutely worth it. I might even grab one for myself next!

reviews.title: 10 October, 2023Verified purchase
reviews.body: Good purchase I bought the Owala and Tekaya on my same trip to target. The Owala cost was a bit less than the Tekaya. I’m a fan to ice water. I filled both water bottles with 6 ice balls. After 24 hours bottles remained in the same room together. The Owala had 2 ice balls still floating around while the Tekaya had none. Also to note the water bottles are the same OZ capacity. I would buy the Owala as the ice lasts longer and it’s $4 cheaper. Hope this review helps the next buyer. Karm quality quality: 5 out of 5 value

reviews.title: 4 December, 2024Verified purchase
reviews.body: Owala’s products and warranty fall short of expectations I have purchased two Owala water bottles from Target over the past year, and both have had issues with the lids. The pin came out on one bottle, and the lid wouldn’t close. On the other, the spring in the button broke, making it impossible for the lid to stay closed. I attempted to get replacement lids under their "lifetime warranty," unfortunately, they refused, claiming these defects were due to "non-standard use." The water bottle has been used exclusively in a standard manner—my college-age child uses it for classes—and has not been subjected to impact damage or nonstandard use. The product’s durability should reasonably exceed four months under such conditions. I am disappointed in the quality of this product. Since we've experienced similar issues with two separate bottles, it seems this is a common problem. I would not recommend these water bottles, and I would not expect much from this company’s “limited lifetime warranty” on their products. 1 reply from Trove Brands team - 1 year ago We are sorry to hear bout your disappointment with your bottles and our warranty process. reviews.title: 4 July, 2022
reviews.body: Dishwasher safe? yea NO! Life time warranty? uh NO. Wow, wow, wow. I am so confused about this product. First off I bought it from the retailer that I work for and I brought it home and read the label where it said dishwasher safe and what do you think I did? I put it in the dishwasher where after the dish washing sequence was completed I pulled the bottle out of the washer which the bottle so far seems to be ok however the button that clips the spout lid closed popped off the lid and at first I thought it was done before ever even using the bottle but it turned out that the heat of the water just softened the clips and weakened it so it popped off but did not break so I was able to get it to go back into place but it clearly does not have the same stout durable feel to it anymore. It is clearly weakened (TAKE NOTE PEOPLE) DO NOT PUT IN DISHWASHER and DO NOT USE WITH HOT LIQUIDS! Now with that said I went back and read more of the bottle and it says not for use with (HOT), nor carbonated, nor perishable liquids. (QUESTION) if you can't put hot liquid in it then how the heck is it dishwater safe? I have read many of the reviews and found that there are many who had the same failure in the clip of the lid or the spring behind it. Thankfully I didn't spend much more than a large cup of starbucks on this thing since I bought it during a blow out start of summer sale. I also went to this sight and read the (Limited) Lifetime warranty and it's details and sorry OWALA but your warranty is a joke. The details of your warranty spell out that the product is covered for replacement if it is defective out of the box so to speak. Once it is used the warranty is pretty much void at that point. I will from now on stick with Stanley/Aladdin Thermoses who really do believe in their product and truly stand behind their true life time warranty. I have attached a photo of the bottle where it clearly states (DISHWASHER SAFE)

reviews.title: 26 March, 2026
reviews.body: Price goes up, Quality goes down i dented my old Owala Freesipe so i ordered a new 1. i have had about 5-6 of these over the last 2 years and have always loved the quality, design, and ice retention of these. When i received my new water bottle i immediately noticed alot of differences in quality and feel. The new bottle is lighter which means they are using less meterial, now it will dent and break more easily. There is a visible diffrence in thickness of lid and bottle. The new straw is now shorter, and a cheap glorified version of a Mcdonalds straw that is going to bend and break, if it doesnt melt in the dishwasher first. i used to only fill my bottle up with ice every 2-3 days, not by the end of the day the ice is almost all melted. Everything about this new water bottle feels off. The clicking sound of lid now sounds like a cheap hollow plastic-y click, when you set it on the counter it now has a higher pitch ping instead of the lower thud noise it used to make. I have contacted Owala directly and they have verified that they have made "some changes" to the design which confirms all of my observations. I am sick of these companies making a good product to get us to like, support, & promote them and then once they are popular, they cut their manufacturing prices by cutting corners and using less cheaper material so that they can increase their profits. This is where i draw the line and refuse to support a compantly that has completely changed their identity and roots. It is no longer the water bottle i once loved and promoted, but now it is just some cheap sad water bottle that is no diffrent than one you can get on temu. 1 reply from Trove Brands team - 28 days ago We greatly appreciate your insight and feedback. While we have made adjustments to design elements and expanded our product offerings, we have not made any changes to material or manufacturing processes. We understand where you are coming from though, and would love to take a closer look to ensure we can resolve this for you! Please contact our team directly at customerservice@owalalife.com to connect with a representative directly. Robin quality quality: 1 out of 5 value

reviews.title: 29 November, 2023Verified purchase
reviews.body: Excellent, would recommend I love this water bottle. I swear it was designed by an occupational therapist. 1. The sides are flat so it is natural to hold. 2. The top is textured so it is easy to twist off. 3. It locks, and the button to open it is easy to push, but won't get pushed accidentally. 4. The "straw" part of the mouth of the water bottle fits your lips - it is not round but rather flat, and the bottom of the water bottle mouth is curved to fit the bottom of your mouth. I have a swallowing disorder, and this eliminates my drinking problems. I can only fill my mouth with what the straw allows, and no extra air gets in. It's also not as expensive as other name brand water bottles, and all of the colors remind me of different birds. Roxanne C quality quality: 5 out of 5 value

reviews.title: 18 April, 2024Verified purchase
reviews.body: Return/Replacement/Refund I purchased the Owala Flask from Target on 11/24/23, and it quickly became an indispensable part of my daily routine. Whether I'm commuting, at work, or traveling, this flask has been a reliable companion, always ensuring my beverages stay at the perfect temperature. One of the standout features of this flask is its user-friendly design. It's incredibly easy to use and carry around, making it a convenient choice for anyone on the go. In fact, I was so impressed with its performance that I decided to gift one to my younger sister. However, my positive experience hit a snag when the top covering the opening and closing mechanism unexpectedly broke on 4/17/24. I was understandably devastated, but my faith in Owala and Target was quickly restored when I reached out to Target's customer service team. Their professionalism and efficiency in handling my issue were exemplary. Despite the purchase being made several months ago, they promptly assisted me and facilitated a replacement without any hassle. This level of customer service truly exceeded my expectations and reaffirmed my loyalty to Target. I encountered a setback with the Owala Flask, the outstanding support provided by Target's customer service team turned it into a positive experience. I'm grateful for their assistance and look forward to continuing my patronage with both Owala and Target. Thank you! Stephanie value value: 5 out of 5 quality

reviews.body: Good water bottle but lid loop lock is plastic and can break off My kid recently replaced their Simple Modern water bottle flip spout and fixed handle with this Owala 24 oz. Pros: kid like option of sucking through straw or chugging through spout. Kid also like that it doesn’t leek compared to previous simple modern water bottle that eventually leaked through flip spout. Cons: kid carries water bottle using the loop lid lock but kid swings the water bottle that puts a lot of tension on the tiny plastic nubs that connects to the rest of the lid- it broke after day 2 so not as sturdy “handle”compared to previous water bottle. I do foresee several lid replacement purchases. Barely fits into cup holder in back seat arm rest where kid usually sits (it can fit in one of the front seat cup holders and back seat side door pockets) Overall, good water bottle- I cannot attest to longevity of the container itself yet. reviews.title: 18 September, 2024Verified purchase
reviews.body: The best bottle for everyday use and especially for new moms! I love the Owala Free Sip stainless steel bottle. I own 4 of them (they have so many colorful combinations), my son has 2, my daughter has one as well. I’m a nurse and have recommended it to all of my coworkers and new moms. Most of my coworkers have bought one because of how highly I speak of this water bottle. I also gift it to all my expecting mom friends. My #1 most favorite thing about it is that you can carry it, open, drink and close it with one hand. Just flip the handle lock, press the button to open the top, sip, and close all with one hand. Definitely useful when you’re breastfeeding, holding a baby, or busy at work. You don’t even have to lift the bottle, you can set it on the table and sip it while you’re typing away since there is a straw. You’re choice lift it up and tilt or set it down and sip, your choice. The last thing I love is that the drink spout is not exposed, you don’t need an additional straw top to cover the straw/spout it’s protected and safe when you close it. It’s also super easy to clean, my whole hand fits in the tumbler so I don’t need a bottle brush (but I do have one for when I want a real super soapy clean. You have to try this bottle, I have a 24oz, 32oz, and 40oz bottle. The 40oz is my favorite since it really helps me stick to my water goal of 80oz per day. Myraaaaa value value: 5 out of 5 quality

I understand water bottles get dented when you drop them, but it looks like this bottle was thrown from the roof of a building, not fell out of my 4 foot tall son’s hand while walking (aka fell no more than 2 feet). reviews.title: 6 July, 2025
reviews.body: The Ultimate Insulated Tumbler; Owala Delivers! I recently purchased the Owala insulated tumbler, and I couldn’t be happier with my decision! This tumbler has exceeded my expectations in every way. First off, the design is sleek and modern, making it a stylish accessory to take anywhere. The wide mouth opening is incredibly convenient for both filling and cleaning, and I love the included straw that enables easy sipping without spills. Functionally, the Owala tumbler performs impressively. It keeps my drinks icy cold for hours, even on the hottest days, thanks to its double wall vacuum insulation. I’ve tested it with ice water and iced coffee, and both stayed cold long into the afternoon. No more lukewarm drinks for me! Additionally, the leak proof lid gives me peace of mind while commuting, as I can toss it in my bag without worrying about drips or spills. The bottom fits perfectly in cup holders, adding to the convenience for travel. Overall, the Owala insulated tumbler is a fantastic investment. It combines functionality, style, and durability, making it a must have for anyone looking to enjoy their favorite drinks at the perfect temperature. Highly recommended! hannah16 quality quality: 5 out of 5 value

reviews.body: (no review title) There are m many things I like about this water bottle. The top is very unique and made so that you can sip out of it, but not directly out of the straw, rather drinking from the spout, but without having to tip it. However, if you prefer to drink it like a regular cup, you can also tip it back. The top locks into place so it doesn’t leak but also has a piece that pops up to create a handle to carry it. The color I got is called Kickflip and is a fun and bright pink color. It does fit into my cupholders in my truck, but it is a bit snug. It may not fit all car a cupholders if yours is on the smaller side. I’ve only been using it for about a week, but it does not seem to keep my drinks as cold for as long as other brands I have used in the past such as Yeti and Hydrojug. reviews.body: My favorite bottle!! I always had an issue with finding a water bottle I liked. I came across Owala while browsing around the store and liked what I saw. I didn't notice the price on the shelf, so when I rang it up at checkout, I was a bit taken aback by how much it cost. I was going to remove it from my cart but I'm so glad I didn't! I love that you can either sip through the straw or tip it back. It's easy to clean and can take a beating! The lid lock is definitely handy. Best of all, it keeps my water cold for DAYS (I'm working on my water intake!). I left it in the car for nearly 4 hours in 94 degree summer heat (which would be much hotter in the car) and I still had plenty of ice left! My colleague's plastic water bottle was hot and the water tasted like the plastic. This bottle has paid for itself and I will absolutely get another when the need arises! reviews.title: Bottle saved my life
reviews.body: I was walking through these wet wood steps at the Wakiwa state park towards the springs. I slipped like some kinda looney toon character and woulda landed straight on the stone steps below, directly on my spine, folding me in half. The water bottle was full, and in my backpack. The water bottle broke my fall perfectly. If it wasn’t for the material of this bottle, I would spend the rest of my vacation in a Florida hospital. It’s the next day now and I have absolutely no bruises or pain! Thanks Owala.

reviews.title: Multipack quantity: 1
reviews.body: love it does not leak just don't put soda in it. love this water bottle keeps my water cold all day. the only thing I would say do not put soda in it because of the carbonation. when you open the bottle it opens really fast and it also will spray soda but other than that I just be careful not shake up the bottle. love taking this bottle to casinos and to the beach and outdoor stuff because the sand and dirt will not get into my drink. the lid protects it perfectly in the lid does not leak at all definitely Best buy. can't wait to get another one in a different color reply from Trove Brands Thank you for your review. We discourage the use of hot, carbonated, or perishable liquids. These and other liquids may build up pressure within the bottle and unexpectedly leak or spray out. Please reach out to us at owalalife.com with any further questions or concerns.

reviews.title: Great Design and Easy to Clean
reviews.body: Perfect for older children or adults. Keeps water cold for hours.

reviews.title: My one and only favorite water bottle
reviews.body: Since I bought my I have dramatically increased my water intake and I actually love the taste of my ice cold water!! It stays ice cold for hours and hours. I fill it with 1/4 of ice then fill it up with my tap water and it tastes exactly like bottled water and i find that sipping through the straw helps me to drink more too. I absolutely hands down love my and I wouldn't trade it for anything else on the market!! Review from Owala

reviews.title: Love my Free Sip!
reviews.body: The only water bottle you need! I love that I was able to get rid of all my water bottles after buying this. Thought I had a serious water bottle addiction, turns out I was just looking for "the one" this whole time. I love this water bottle so much, I bought one for each of my kids, so they can have nice cold water for their sports and practices. Now everyone stays hydrated every day. I love that I don't have to touch the spout/straw in order to open and drink from it, as with other water bottles, this is a plus when you're a nurse, or when you have children who don't alway practice good hand hygiene. Best investment ever! Review from owalalife.com

reviews.title: Love this bottle!
reviews.body: As a nurse, I needed a bottle that would close completely, but still offer me a straw, as I drink more when there is one, and I need all the hydration I can get. It keeps my water cold all day, and I like if I m extra thirsty, I can take a big swig. Love that it won t spill if I knock it over in my vehicle and that it has a built in carrying loop. Thanks for my new favorite water bottle! Review from Owala

reviews.title: MY FAVORITE WATER BOTTLE EVER!
reviews.body: I m somewhat of a water bottle obsessor, I ve had the camelbacks and the hydroflasks, but this bottle makes drinking water fun. It s keeps the water cold, it s easy to grip and is so cute! I ve told all of my friends to get one! My only suggestion is to widen the color range! Review from Owala

reviews.body: Great bottle design, light weight and keep the water cold for a long time, like 12 hrs. The straw design is cool. However, I never drink water from the wide mouth, it s too wide. I have found the straw mouth is hard to wipe, it will end up make your water dirty. Review from Owala

reviews.title: Amazing!
reviews.body: I ABSOLUTELY LOVE THIS BOTTLE! In the par two weeks I have recommended it to my friends, family, and random coworkers that I just met. My favorite feature about this bottle is the hidden straw. I have another insulated bottle with a flip straw cover and I would have to disinfect it or wipe it multiple times during the day while at work. Now I don t have to do that because of the completely covered straw! I bought both the 24oz and 32oz but have only used the 24oz so far. The 24oz fits perfectly in my car cup holders. -A very satisfied customer Review from Owala

reviews.title: Really great water bottle!
reviews.body: I really can't say enough good things about this water bottle. Never wrote a water bottle review before, but here we go: I bought 2, one for me and one for my son hoping it would help us drink more water and - it has. We are carrying these with us everywhere we go, I love the heavy duty handles and the click tight leak proof lid- Mom win right there! We love the straw feature and the pour feature is great for getting some water out quickly to the dog on the hike. Keeps water cold without issue. No weird smells or tastes (I know you know what I mean!) Overall - Great water bottle ~ I'm really trying to be selective about the stickers I add to it cuz its gonna be around for a while. aœŒ Review from Owala

reviews.title: So good!
reviews.body: Best water bottle ever! Love this for work! Thanks Owala for giving me a discount so I could try your fabulous water bottle! I m a RN and this keeps my water cold for 2 days! It will not be my last! Review from Owala

reviews.title: Awesome water bottle

reviews.title: Whitening HEAVEN!
reviews.body: This stuff works really well. I used it on some older white shirts and towels that were starting to look dull, and the stain removal was impressive—they came out noticeably brighter after one wash. It also cleans well and boosted my regular detergent. The powder dissolves easily and didn’t leave any residue or stickiness on the clothes or in the washer. Everything smelled clean with a light freshness, not an overpowering scent. The container also arrived with no leakage, which I appreciated. Overall, it’s a great product if you want to bring your whites back to life. I’ll definitely keep using It!

reviews.title: Way better than Bleach, and no bleach spot risk
reviews.body: Works better than bleach — without ruining your clothes ✔️ Removes deodorant buildup ✔️ Brightens whites without damage ✔️ Works on older stains ✔️ Easy soak method ✔️ Better results than bleach I used this on white undershirts with deodorant buildup and saw noticeable improvement after soaking. It didn’t make them brand new, but it worked far better than bleach ever has. I’ve used bleach my whole life because that’s what I grew up with. I like the smell of freshly bleached whites, but I’ve always hated the results, shirts slowly turn cream-colored, deodorant areas darken, and body oils go from invisible to grey and hazy. Instead of throwing more shirts away, I tried OxiClean White Revive on a recommendation. I soaked a mix of newer and older white t-shirts in a storage tote with warm water and about half a scoop for roughly 8 hours, keeping everything fully submerged. After soaking, I washed them on a whites cycle with a Tide pod and air-dried overnight. The results were obvious. Newer shirts looked clean again, and the older ones showed a major reduction in deodorant buildup. Bleach has never improved those stains for me — this actually did. I won’t soak every load, but when whites start to discolor, this is what I use now. Bleach is staying in the cleaning closet.

reviews.title: Brilliant Whites, Happy Laundry Days
reviews.body: OxiClean White Revive Laundry Whitener and Stain Remover Powder is like a burst of sunshine in a little scoop—bright, cheerful, and full of magic for your clothes. Each wash feels like a tiny adventure, as the powder lifts stubborn stains and restores whites to their sparkling best. Even the dullest, most tired fabrics seem to perk up and smile, ready for another day of fun and play. I was amazed at how easily it works, leaving no residue behind, just brilliantly clean, soft, and fresh-smelling laundry. It makes a chore feel less like work and more like a little moment of joy. For anyone wanting bright, happy whites without fuss or disappointment, this powder delivers consistently and delightfully. Five stars for a product that truly brings life—and light—back into every garment.

reviews.title: Laundry magic!
reviews.body: This stuff is my secret weapon with my whites. My husband always wears white shirts but he works hard so they get very dirty. My two young children also play hard and their clothes get dirty. We also have hard water so our whites always end up yellow and dingy looking after a while. I came across this product a year ago and have never looked back. Between my husband and children I use a lot of this product so I always get the 5lb tub. I wish there was an even larger sized tub - I'd buy it for sure! This product is not only great on whites! I safely use it on all colors to release stains (this is noted as permissible on the packaging). For tougher stains or when I'm washing whites I let them soak in this overnight before washing. For lighter soiled items that still need something more than just detergent I throw a scoop of this in the wash load and they come out ultra clean. This product works extremely well, in my opinion, and releases dirt like magic! I'm also shocked by how dirty the water gets when I use this product - it pulls the dirt right out! It does have a very strong scent that I don't particularly love but I am also sensitive to and picky about smells. I feel like the scent of this product wouldn't overwhelm or bother you if you're used to using any type of traditional laundry detergent. It does not bother my husband or children. Speaking of laundry detergent, you still need to use it! This is a laundry BOOSTER and not a laundry detergent. Overall, I highly recommend this product if you are struggling to get your laundry as clean as you'd like.

reviews.title: Very easy to use.
reviews.body: This is a great product to use in your laundry. It is powerful and it works well. You can see the difference in the clothes when you use. The smell is not overwhelming and it does not burn your eyes. This product is easy to use.

reviews.title: Brightens Whites Well, But Takes Some Effort
reviews.body: This works noticeably better than regular detergent when it comes to reviving white clothes. I’ve used it on white shirts and towels that were starting to look dull, and after soaking and washing with this powder, they definitely looked brighter. It’s a good alternative to bleach if you don’t want the harsh smell or risk of fabric damage. It also helps with certain stains, especially light discoloration from sweat or general wear. The powder dissolves well in warm water and doesn’t leave residue when used correctly. That said, it’s not a miracle product. For heavily set-in stains, you’ll still need to pre-treat or soak longer. It also works best with warm or hot water, so it’s not as effective in cold cycles. And like most whitening products, consistency matters — one wash won’t completely restore very old fabrics. Overall, it’s a solid option if you want to maintain white clothes without using chlorine bleach. It takes a little effort, but the results are worth it. 4 stars. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Total Satisfaction with Quality, and Performance.
reviews.body: Excellent alternative to Bleach. Most of the newer fabrics, including cotton, don't recommend liquid bleach or powdered bleaches containing chlorine. This product works as advertised on whites and light-colored sheets.

reviews.title: Does the trick!
reviews.body: Excellent for odor removal. I use washable puppy pads and they were still smelling rather ripe after being washed. I now use OxiClean in every wash and they come out smelling fresh. My whites are also looking much brighter.

reviews.title: Good to have to boost cleaning power of laundry soap. Subscribe and Save is great.
reviews.body: I like using this as a laundry booster in every load. I think it really helps cut down on residual odors and it seems to make stain removal much better. I also use it to pre-soak some particularly dirty things like rags and shop cloths. Works well. Good at stain removoal. Doesn't seem to bleach out colors. A little goes a long way so the container lasts about 3 months. I would get this from a real store, but the fact that it is offered as a Subscribe and Save is why I get it from Amazon. The cost is almost the same, but now it just shows up when I am getting low without having to think about it. The only downside of this is related to the Subscribe and Save. Amazon will randomly tape the container lid on with some sort of indestructible tape with an adhesive that will not come off at all. I guess they do this to hopefully keep the powder from busting out of the container, but it doesn't really help with that. Delivery drivers are brutal to packages and several times this has arrived damaged. Sometimes taped, sometimes not. I don't think the manufacturer designed the container for shipping and that is the problem.

reviews.title: Keeps Whites Bright without Damaging Fabric
reviews.body: I started using OxiClean White Revive as a replacement for bleach, mainly for my white dress shirts and it’s been a noticeable upgrade. In my experience, bleach tends to yellow shirts over time and weaken the fabric, especially with frequent washing. Dress shirts are expensive so you want to protect them, this stuff brightens whites without that harsh effect. It works well for keeping shirts looking fresh rather than overly stripped or worn. One thing to note is that you need to follow the instructions it should be added to the washer before the laundry for best results! Overall, it’s a much gentler and more fabric friendly alternative to harsh bleach for maintaining white clothing

Taking away one star because it does leave a soap chemical smell on your clothing. It’s not perfume , so I’ll settle since scented perfume detergents are disgusting!!! But it’s definately there and I have to air out the laundry room and leave fans on for a couple days after I use it. Still recommended though if you can tolerate it. I wish they’d make a completely fragrance free option.

reviews.title: Very effective
reviews.body: In my ongoing quest to keep my laundry spotless and bright, I recently stumbled upon a laundry whitener and stain removing powder that truly stands out from the rest. After several uses, I am thoroughly impressed by its effectiveness and feel compelled to share my experience with others who might be looking for a reliable solution to common laundry dilemmas. First and foremost, the performance of this laundry whitener is exceptional. It has brought life back to my whites, which had begun to dull after repeated washing. The brilliance and radiance it adds to fabrics are noticeable after just one wash, transforming my once lackluster white clothes and linens into vibrant, like-new pieces. The difference is not just noticeable; it’s striking. Moreover, the stain removing capability of this powder is nothing short of amazing. From tough-to-treat spills like wine and coffee to more stubborn spots like grass and mud, this product handles it all with ease. What truly sets it apart is its ability to tackle both old stains that have set into fabrics and fresh spills that can often be a hassle. The powder works efficiently without the need for pre-soaking or multiple washes, saving both time and energy. One of the aspects I appreciate most about this product is its formula. It is powerful yet seems gentle on fabrics, preserving the integrity and texture of the clothes despite its strong whitening and stain-removing properties. Even after continuous use, my clothes show no signs of wear or damage, which is a concern with many harsh chemical cleaners. Usage is straightforward and convenient—just add a scoop of the powder with your regular detergent, and it works its magic effortlessly. The packaging is robust and practical, with clear instructions and helpful tips for optimal results. In terms of eco-friendliness and skin sensitivity, I found this product to be reasonably mild compared to other harsh bleaches and whiteners. It didn’t cause any irritation, which is a necessity for families with sensitive skin or those preferring more environmentally benign products. In conclusion, this laundry whitener and stain remover powder is a top-performing must-have for anyone looking to revitalize their whites and eliminate tough stains. Its effectiveness, ease of use, and fabric safety make it a standout product that I highly recommend. Say goodbye to dingy whites and stubborn stains; this product will revolutionize your laundry routine

I haven’t tried it on anything that was already dingy looking so idk if it brightens on its own; but it has def kept my white sheets and towels very white even tho I wash them too often. I’ve also used it to pretreat stains that I thought would be hopeless but it cleaned them really well even before they went in the washer. It smells great but it’s not too strong.

reviews.title: Transformed My Whites — A Must-Have Laundry Booster
reviews.body: OxiClean is the best! It has become an indispensable part of my laundry routine. Unlike traditional bleach, this oxygen-based powder is safe for colors and delivers impressive results without the harsh chemicals. I use it regularly to brighten whites, and it consistently revives dingy items like vintage t-shirts and yellowed sheets. The powder dissolves well in water, and I appreciate that it works effectively in both standard and HE washers. For best results, I recommend using it as a presoak or adding it directly to the wash drum. While some users have reported mixed results, I find that following the instructions carefully yields the best outcomes. It's important to note that this product is designed for whites and colorfast fabrics, so it's not suitable for delicate materials like wool or silk. Overall, OxiClean White Revive is a reliable and affordable alternative to chlorine bleach, effectively restoring the brightness of my laundry. I highly recommend it for anyone looking to maintain their whites without the risks associated with traditional bleach. No issues with the scent or skin irritation. The container is sturdy as well, so less risk of making a huge, unwanted mess!

reviews.title: 20 September, 2023
reviews.body: It really works! After reading Answer Angel in my Sunday paper (9/18/23), I went out and bought some Oxi-Clean White Revive. My husband has several "white" t-shirts with very unattractive stains in the underarm area. I have tried Borax, baking soda and vinegar paste, Oxi-Clean, all without success. Still beige and yellow. I followed Oxi-Clean White Revive directions and soaked several t-shirts for 6 hours. I cannot believe how well it worked!! Now, I am not a laundry nerd, but I was so excited about how WHITE they were I rushed to show my husband. EVEN the unattractive yellow WAS 95% gone!!! Thank you for making my life a wee bit easier, and brighter!

reviews.title: 23 May, 2023
reviews.body: Wish I had known OxiClean sooner… After watching how people used OxiClean as cleaning hacks, I’ve decided to give it a try. reviews.title: 20 September, 2023
reviews.body: Holy whiteness, Batman I've tried so many products and methods to get old discolored whites whiter. They all failed. Some of these are from as early as the 1980s and have never been "treated" with anything. White Revive worked on bringing them mostly back to their former glory. It also worked on white pillow cases, white denim, dress shirts and old towels. Six hour soak in hot water with dissolved product, swooshing out the air every 90 minutes (so three times). More product in washer, just like it says, and WHITE WHITES! Measurements aren't clear and it seems some may have tried full scoops! I used 2 TBS for each gallon of water and ~9 shirts at a time. Then two more scoops in the washer. Magical. Some seem to think it smells, but they must have used a ton of it! the light odor is barely noticeable.

reviews.title: 25 March, 2014
reviews.body: The only thing we use at work! [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I was thrilled to get the chance to use the new Oxi Clean White Revive. I work in the laundry department in a condominium and we have been using Oxi products for months. We rave about it to our friends and customers, and are thrilled with how white the linen comes out. It may seems funny, but we have danced around our laundry department, because we are so thrilled with how white and bright the towels and sheets are, after using this product. We have had a huge difference in the number of towels that have to be thrown out since we have been purchasing Oxi Clean products for our daily use. I didn't notice much difference in the white revive over the Oxi Clean versatile powder. Both work so well. Usually I use the soak cycle, because our socks get pretty dirty at home. I was pleased with the brightness of my whites after using this new product. I have, and will continue to recommend this product to my friends. Thank you Oxi Clean, for making my laundry work easier and more productive.

reviews.title: 16 August, 2024
reviews.body: Better packaging needed Just bought this item and is baffled by the packaging of the product. The product design team constructed this poorly. A lid that is sold in a tub that’s not properly sealed is automatically defective. It’s easily tampered with while on shelves. Even ice cream tubs are now sealed more securely after it was proven ease of access can lead to easy contamination.

reviews.title: 6 February, 2020
reviews.body: Saved the day - and my towels! If this doesn’t convince you that OxyClean White Revive works, then nothing will! After soaking my towels overnight they were resplendently white. I then put them through a hot water wash only to discover that the water heater in our new rental spewed out sediment into the washer - the towels came out with rusty brown stains all over them. I immediately tried another soaking, making sure the hot water was clear and added four scoops of OxyClean White Revive to a small water-level setting. Then I waited six hours and ran them through a cold water wash and two rinse cycles. They came out as if nothing happened. I use this product only to soak and restore my white towels and I prefer the powder form - I feel it’s more effective than the liquid. Along with the hottest water and lots of patience, you will be rewarded with like-new towels for following the soaking directions.

reviews.body: Excellent part of my routine Do you know that straight up lemon juice will rid your whites of almost any fresh stain? My kiddo left a Hershey kiss in one of her pockets and it went thru the DRYER and ruined every single piece of clothing - especially the white ones. Soak 1/2 part lemon juice and 1/2 part water for about 12 hours (overnight). Check it and stains will be GONE. This is a review for Oxiclean - so wash in Oxiclean after the stains come out, and you will be most pleased! ;)

reviews.title: 11 June, 2017
reviews.body: Love this product! Except the scent I consider this to be the best whitener I have every used. I am sort of a local stain expert and use a variety of products to remove all kinds of stains. The majority of products I use for this are all by oxyclean. Friends bring me their stuff with stains they have had no luck with and I get them out. I have used White Revive on a number of stubborn stains as well as dingy whites, including lace curtains that hadn't been washed in at least 10 years. I make a strong solution in hot water and make sure the powder is completely dissolved. I then completely submerge the item and let it sit for up to 24 hours (or even longer if it still looks dingy when you check it). Watch the water turn dramatically gray. On a couple of occasions I have washed an item and had to soak it a second time. In such cases I do not dry it first. You don't have to worry that it will weaken fabric like bleach does. I got washed and dried in lipstick out of a friend's quick dry shirt that had lipstick all over it. It looked like new. She was absolutely amazed. You can also dab the liquid version on small stains and let it sit for a while before washing and your stain should be gone. Its cleaning and whitening power are over the top, a ++5. However it does not get a five because it absolutely STINKS, REEKS of perfume which I hate. I might use it more if it did not have this one awful flaw. The perfume is so strong you can smell it throughout the house and it takes several washings to get rid of it. I would like to recommend to all laundry product companies that, if you are going to make only one version of a product to make it odor free and hypoallergenic. That way the most customers can use it. It is also pretty expensive so I use it only when needed. Oxyclean's other stain products are great too and I use them on a more routine basis reserving white revive for the toughest jobs or for when the others are not enough.
