reviews.title: A good product but with reservations
reviews.body: I used this on some pretty yellowed & grubby white lace curtains and they came out very nice and clean (revived, as the name indicates). I saw the ad on TV about a week ago and bought this product specifically for this project because I liked the idea of non-chlorine product that would not hurt the somewhat older, delicate fabric. So, yes, the whitening aspect works as promised and it did not harm the fibers as bleach sometimes does. However, the perfume smell was so strong that I kind of wished I'd taken the chance with bleach. Bleach smell dissipates after a day or so, but Oxy's perfume is actually kind of obnoxious even after several days. I am one of those people who is mildly sensitive to too much fragrance. I feel this would be a perfect product if the perfume was lightened up considerably, or if there was a fragrance-free version. I have now used the Oxy Clean-White Revive in two wash loads, both of which are airing outside. I will have to give the rest of the product away to someone who is less fragrance sensitive. If a fragrance-free version comes out, I'll definitely buy. Review from oxiclean.com

I just threw in my son's white sports socks, t shirts, and underwear with the additive. I was amazedwith the outcome! The whites were snowy white, but the product didn't fade the color trim on the socks, or the screen printing on the t shirts. In fact, the colors seemed more vivid also! I also washed my other son's white down comforter with it. Our family dog likes to sneak into his room on the comforter and I usually have to pretreat it also. I just washed it once, and it came out looking brand new. I love this product! Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: great stain remover
reviews.body: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] i used the OxiClean White Revive Laundry Stain Remover with my laundry and i put my washer on presoak and i took the clothes out and they smell so good and they looked whiter and briter and it got all the stains out but you do have to let it soak for awhile to get deeper stains out i love this product! Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: All I could ask for
reviews.body: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] Best thing I have used on whites - ever. Does the same job as bleach only won't rip everything to shreds or make them smell like a swimming pool. Haven't tried it on colors yet but I have no doubt it will dominate on those as well. Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: It did the job!
reviews.body: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] After my husband colored his hair, he used a white towel to dry off. His hair looked great but the towel was a mess! Fortunately, OxiClean was up to the challenge. After a good soak, the towel is as snow-white as it was before. Fabulous! Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: I'm Sorry I Bought This
reviews.body: I've used a lot of Oxi product and liked them which is why I bought White Revive. Also I don't like to use bleach. White Revive is so over loaded with fragrance that I am going to have to rewash these clothes many times to get rid of the horrible smell. We only use dye and perfume free products because of allergies so this is a bad situation for my family. This product be labeled that it contains heavy fragrance. I don't know if it brightened the clothes or not and it doesn't matter because they can't be worn smelling like this. I wouldn't have said anything if the smell washed out but they are now going through a 3rd washing with 2 rinses. The first 2, and that's 6 water changes, didn't reduce the smell at all. Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: whitest
reviews.body: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I started using this product a day after the man came to pump my septic. He told me I had been using too much bleach and that I had to cut way back. In trying your product not only was I able to cut down on the bleach, I am finding that I really don't need it all. Our clothes are coming out wonderfuly clean and fresh smelling. I thought they had to smell like bleach to be clean but come to find out they don't Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: great product
reviews.body: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] This product performed very well, I tried on set in stains as well as ground in dirt stains. I was impressed with product, I did pre soak these items for several hours. Also used this product in the wash with colored laundry and they did appear brighter. Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: This product was made for my daughter...
reviews.body: My 17 year old daughter Shannon has every shoe ever made yet refuses to wear any of them. Rather, she prefers something white, and easily stainable. Her high school requires white tights as part of the uniform, as soon as she walks through the door at home, shoes off for the rest of the day! She's a cheerleader, uniform includes white knee socks with green and yellow stripes along the top... after a game, shoes off! I've even caught her practicing at school on the football field without shoes! You can imagine those stains! She's also in dance and rehearses in her regular white knee socks for hours. So as you can see, she tends to have her stocking feet laundry pile up quick, so I needed something specifically for whites without the damaging effect of bleach, and this was a godsend. The stains are gone.. even her tights which get the worst damage, they look like she's walked on coals for a day sometimes, and not to mention the times she's stained them AT school (meatball grease and red sauce spill anyone?) it gets it all out the first time. Best product on the market for getting extremely tough stains out of whites!!! Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: Dingy socks looking whiter
reviews.body: I had received a mission from Smiley360 to try this product for free and give my own opinions about it. They did not pay me and the review are my own thoughts and experience. I got this product today and had soaked my husband's and son's dingy socks for about 6 hours. And I have to say I'm sticking with this product. It definitely does get the stains out and made an impact on the whites. Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: Oxiclean tops all other products
reviews.body: I'm a 71 year old great-grandmother and have washed thousands of loads of laundry. unfortunately I have destroyed more clothes and linen than I care to remember until I tried this awesome product called OxiClean. my clothes can't get any whiter.. I wash everyday. Please send me some coupons. I recommend this product to literally everyone who will listen. IT'S GREAT!!!!!! Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: horrible strong perfumed smell!
reviews.body: I recently purchased this to whiten men's white underwear. why would a cleaning product for garments worn next to skin be so heavily perfumed? even when using a very small amount, the scent permeates the entire house. it is gross! Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: Works great!
reviews.body: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I used this to remove old food stains from an old tablecloth. It removed all the stains without fading the printed fabric. I couldn't be happier with the results. Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: Great Product
reviews.body: I have two boys and there clothes, especially now that the weather is great needs extra cleaning. Lets just say this product works wonders. Removes stains and leaves clothes looking semi- new. Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: Like magic
reviews.body: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I soaked my husbands white t-shirts in this to get the yellow pit stains out and it worked like magic! Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: Strong scent
reviews.body: Does the trick as far as removing stains and whitening but the scent is so over powering I cannot use it unless I wash things a second time with baking soda to remove the scent. Make it unscented please! Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: Brought a dingy white duvet cover back to life!
reviews.body: [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I decided to try the White Revive Oxiclean product on a very expensive, older white duvet cover. The duvet cover is 1000 count cotton sateen and has been a beautiful addition to a bed that is used often in my home. Unfortunately, over the years the cover has become a dingy, somewhat grayer, white. I've washed in warm water with Tide before and it was clean but not bight white. One washing with the white revive oxiclean significantly improved the whiteness. I am very pleased! Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: Works great!!
reviews.body: My husband is hard on his clothes and super stinky at times, This product worked great at brightening his whites and getting all odors out. I was really impressed how well the stains were removed. He worked out in the garden for hours, came in with dirt and grass stains.. all gone! Review from oxiclean.com

reviews.title: Good product
reviews.body: I have used this product for over six months and it works great! Easy to charge. Cleans teeth well.

reviews.title: Not as weak as they say
reviews.body: This is my 1st electric toothbrush. I ordered it over the oral b smart 1500 because it's cheaper and I found more people who swear by it than oral b. Later on, I read comments where people who have this 4100 for 10+ years say that the newer 2021 version has only half the power of the old version. Someone on reddit showed images of the inside of the new and old versions and the new version has a small plastic toy-like motor, while the old version has a big copper-wrapped motor and an old version user said "now I understand why the new version is lighter and less powerful". I was disappointed on the new version and tried to cancel the order but I couldn't. Once it came, I decided to try it and to my surprise, it brushes my teeth way better than my manual soft-bristled and hard-bristled toothbrushes(I use both manual brushes daily compared to my daily usage of the electric toothbrush 4100 C2 head of medium bristles. If you have gum sensitive issue, buy the S sensitive or G2 optimal brush head. If you want hard-bristled, buy W diamondclean brush head). I don't know how good the old version was but this newer version definitely brushes better than any manual toothbrush unfortunately it doesn't not come with a travel case. Update 5/16/2024: I bought the Oral B smart 1500 because it was on sale and I wanted to see how better it is compared to the sonicare. The oral B package looks nicer and bigger than the Sonicare but upon opening the smart 1500 package, I saw hair all over the toothbrush. I did read many customers saying on reddit that their Oral b toothbrush were repackaged, but I can't believe I'm a victim of this nastiness. It also took 2 weeks to reach me from Hawaii and since it says "made in China", I think the seller is located in China. I'll return this and I'll advise you to buy the Sonicare instead of the oral b if you don't want to be cheated receiving a used-repackaged toothbrush. Update 05/22/2024: On 05/17/24, I bought the Oral b smart clean 360 which has exact power as the smart 1500 that I returned but the smart 360 has 5 modes and a button to change the mode while the smart 1500 has 3 modes and no button to switch between the modes. The advantage of the oral b smart clean 360 is that the brush heads cost is cheaper than the Sonicare 4100 and I find it better at whitening my teeth than the Sonicare. That's all the advantages there. The things that I don't like with it are many, so I'm just going to name the most annoying ones: 1) The brush heads are too big compared to the Sonicare(see picture): Putting Oral b brush heads in my mouth is like putting a golf ball in my mouth(my head and mouth are wider than the average US male). 2) The brush heads have holes(see pictures) that trap saliva+toothpaste and creates more noise when the brush handle needle rotates in the presence of the liquid mixture, and moisture will be also trapped easily in the holes(most customers complain about Oral b brush heads design. Oral b corrected the needle issue with the IO version but the IO introduced a shallow hole which now traps moisture in the brush handle). When I'm using the Oral b with no choice but to have my mouth wide-open, it forces my mouth to produce more saliva which gets mixed with the toothpaste and enters the brush head and creates more noise. 3) Rattling noise: The Oral b smart clean 360 isn't as noisy as people say. It's the quality of the noise the problem. It's a very unpleasant rattling noise as if you're hearing an old car engine with broken parts in it. That noise gets worse when liquid enters the brush head holes. 4) The charger is not round like a perfect circle: You must put the Oral b brush handle in a specific position to charge it and if you place it wrong, it'll fall and might break. 5) The Daily Clean mode is rough and combined with the big brush head the experience is bad, but the smart clean 360 has more mode which make it smoother and better than the smart 1500 but not as good as the Sonicare 4100. 6)It has no auto-stop mode: You keep brushing without knowing how long you have been brushing your teeth. To summarize, the oral b non-IO brush heads are cheap but very badly designed for a pleasant toothbrushing. I won't recommend it if you're shorter than 6 foot tall or have a small mouth.

reviews.title: Great functionality, low price.
reviews.body: The model 4100 does everything I need for a much lower price than the Diamondclean model. Been using the 4100 for about 6 months and no regrets. I previously had the Diamondclean model which finally died after a few years of use so I needed a replacement. I didn’t need the 5 setting options or the WiFi connectivity of Diamondclean so I decided to try the 4100 model. The 4100 has two settings - I still only use one. It’s also lighter, uses the same brush heads, and has a smaller and lighter charger that should travel well. It feels the same when I’m brushing and the best part is that if it dies, I could buy about four of them for the same price as one Diamondclean. I’m extremely happy I gave it a try and saved so much money. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Good for the money
reviews.body: We bought three of these electric toothbrushes on sale during Christmas time. Two of the three still hold a great charge but one seems to not hold a charge more than a few days. When they work, they are great brushes and the heads are nice sized to fit in any position around your mouth. Only difficult to reach spot is very back tooth on top and bottom. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: Great toothbrush with a UBS charger
reviews.body: Great toothbrush. This one is a replacement for a Sonicare I bought at least 20 years ago. It made many international and national trips, so it did get a little battered up, but that makes it a pretty reasonable price. I will say that the brushers are expensive but I asked my dentist where he purchased the ones he gives away and ordered from that company, PlaquePro.com, in bulk. Be aware that you have to order about a bazillion of them, so get a big drawer ready for them. Note that if you travel, while many larger hotels do have a UBS charge station, some hotels will not, so you may want to take a UBS hub that plugs into the wall socket.

reviews.title: minor accidents and there goes your 3 digit electric tooth brush
reviews.body: Sonicare loses cleaning power after minor drops I’ve now gone through three Sonicare toothbrushes in about two years. In my experience, even a minor drop can make the vibration much weaker. The brush still turns on and vibrates, but the power drops so much that it no longer feels like an effective electric toothbrush, even with a new brush head. That is the most frustrating part: it does not completely fail, so at first glance it seems fine, but the cleaning performance is clearly reduced. At that point, the main advantage of using an electric toothbrush is basically caput.. This has happened to me twice, and it makes the product feel far less durable than it should be for normal real-world household use. For a premium toothbrush, I expected better resistance to minor drops and better long-term reliability. I still think Sonicare is a great product, but after this repeated experience I would strongly advise buyers to be very careful with handling and not assume it can tolerate everyday bumps or falls. Planned obscolecence? Sad if it is. I am sure it should not take more than a minor cushioning, internal or external to make the product more rugged.

reviews.title: My Teeth Feel Like They’ve Been Polished by a Tiny, Enthusiastic Pit Crew
reviews.body: I used to brush my teeth like a common laborer, moving my hand back and forth like I was trying to start a fire with two sticks. Then I bought the Philips Sonicare 4100. I realized within thirty seconds that I had been doing it wrong for thirty years. My mouth doesn't just feel clean; it feels like it’s been through a high-end car wash and then buffed with a microfiber cloth. Why Your Mouth Needs This Sonic Engine: The Vibrations of Victory: 62,000 brush movements per minute. If I tried to do that manually, my arm would fly off and hit the mirror. This thing creates a "microbubble" situation that reaches into crevices I didn't even know I possessed. The "Stop Pressing So Hard" Sensor: Apparently, I used to brush my teeth with the same intensity I use to scrub a cast-iron skillet. The 4100 has a pressure sensor that pulses when I’m being too aggressive. It’s like a gentle life coach for my gums. The Two-Minute Timer: I used to think two minutes was a long time. It turns out, my "manual" two minutes was actually about forty seconds. This brush keeps me honest. It’s the longest two minutes of my life, but my dentist’s inevitable praise will be worth it. Battery Life for Days: I charged this thing once, and I’m pretty sure it’s going to outlast my current lease. It’s the Energizer Bunny of dental hygiene. The Life Impact I combined this with my 1,000 RESHLIX Floss Picks, and I’m fairly certain my mouth is now the cleanest environment on the North American continent. I went to the dentist last week and he asked me if I’d recently had a professional cleaning. I just smiled—a blinding, sonic-powered smile—and walked out. Pros: My teeth are so smooth I can’t stop sliding my tongue over them. It’s slim, it’s sleek, and it makes my bathroom look like a futuristic spa. Cons: The vibration is so powerful that if you turn it on before it’s in your mouth, you will decorate your bathroom mirror with toothpaste art. You’ve been warned.

reviews.title: New 4100
reviews.body: I've had my first Sonicare for 9 1/2 years, and while it still works, the battery light never reaches full any more, and this is the first time I've seen them on sale, so I went for this one. I paid around $30 instead of $50 on Black Friday sale. They say they don't give you a wall plug for sustainability, which is clearly a falsehood. If they cared about sustainability, I'd be able to use my perfectly good previous wall charger. Do they honestly think we want to charge our toothbrushes from our laptops? It's ludicrous. I found a link on their website where you can request a wall charger brick. In the mean time, I'm using one I already had. Who knows if it's appropriate. I should have just stuck with my old charger. One star off for this. The toothbrush itself is fine. I picked this one because I don't think they need more than one button. It has all the features I need: timer and high or low intensity. Also the pressure sensor is a nice touch. It's thinner to hold and makes a lot less noise. I like the change for that reason alone. All my variety of Sonicare and non-Sonicare brush heads work. So far so good. We'll see if this one lasts 9 1/2 years, though.

reviews.title: Strange sensation at first, but well worth it!
reviews.body: I had always heard about the Sonicare brand of tooth brushing systems, but had never really picked one up until recently. Sonicare uses vibrating motions to move the brush over the surface of your teeth. The sensation was a little strange at first, as the brush motion can produce a "tickling" sensation on your gums, but after a few uses, you will get used to the sensation. I found it's streamlined brush systems to be very good at reaching many hard to reach areas in your mouth, yet it was still gentle enough to not damage your gums. This particular system has a 2-minute timer, which is the minimum recommended time you should spend brushing your teeth, however you can just use it for however long you need to clean your teeth. The system comes with a charging base which lasts up to 14 hours in between charges, and also a protective plastic cover for in between brushings. Overall, I am very satisfied with this system, and my teeth have never felt better. I would recommend this for any adult that is curious about Sonicare.

reviews.title: Help!
reviews.body: I bought this model of the Sonicare based on other people's reports, and I'm confused - but I think it's because I'm biased. My previous Sonicare just died after at least 10 years - maybe more, and I have to confess, when this new skinny thing with a teeny brush fit for a three yeat old arrived, I was horrified. I guess I'm just out of date! I'm used to a brush the size of a regular tooth brush, and something I can grip firmly. Also, it comes with a complete book of information and instructions - hilarious! Will I adapt? I guess i'll try. The four stars as I'm trying not to be too mean!

reviews.title: Swapped my 7 year old model - WOW
reviews.body: I'm not a big review person, but I'm hoping this will be a blessing to someone interested in the 4100. I've had a Philips Sonicare (don't remember the model name) for probably 7 years or more. I wear a mouth guard at night and the dental hygienist said saliva was pooling causing plaque in a few areas. I was having issues with them using the sonic scraper thing when cleaning my teeth and was wondering if perhaps a newer model w/newer tech would help. I checked with GROK and it recommended the 4100. As sure as I'm typing this, I used it and afterwards it made my teeth, all around (still have my wisdom teeth and I'm in m 60s) feel like I just got back from a cleaning. This thing keeps the plaque away and was absolutely what I was hoping for. I'm hoping my next cleaning will be a snap now that I'm using the new Sonicare. Could not be happier.

reviews.title: great e-brush and well priced, BUT expensive replacement heads.
reviews.body: I’ve been using this toothbrush for a few months now and i really feel a cleaner, fresher mouth, and the pressure sensor is a great feature that helps protect my gums. I also like the built-in timer, which makes it easy to stick to the recommended brushing time. The battery lasts a loooong time, and the design is simple and comfortable to use. Overall, a great upgrade for everyday oral care. but 5 stars i couldnt give because the replacment heads are really expensive (im buying off brand heads).

reviews.title: Third one we've owned but not their best effort
reviews.body: This version is very similar to ones we've owned in the past. Works well. Battery lasts about a week with 2 users brushing 2x per day. My only real complaint is that this has a low speed and high speed brushing setting. You have to push the power button twice every time you start brushing to get to the higher speed. Not sure why this isn't a setting like all the other features on this device?!

reviews.title: does not include an ac adaptor
reviews.body: what a great item. we updated out 15 plus year old sonicare electric tooth brushes with 2 of these are are very pleased with the new ones. much smaller, lighter and more efficient than previous models. we are extremely pleased with the purchase. BE AWARE that this model ( and perhaps others) does not come with an ac adaptor which needs to acquired elsewhere. it's cord terminates with a USB plug. not a problem if your wall outlets contain USB ports in addition to electric plug-ins. all in all a great product and big improvement over older units.

reviews.title: Planned obsolescence
reviews.body: Without fail this toothbrush breaks every year, leading to my purchase of a replacement. Still at the price point and promise of working 700 percent better than the average toothbrushe it never feels insurmountable. Brilliant work by the folks at Phillips Sonicare to so perfectly thread the needle of price, quality and duration so myself as the consumer still feel ok with my purchase, year after year. See- Planned obsolescence is a business strategy where products are deliberately designed with an artificially limited lifespan or functional utility to ensure they become outdated or non-functional after a set period. This practice compels consumers to purchase replacements or upgrades more frequently, thereby increasing a manufacturer's long-term sales volume.

reviews.title: The only way to brush from now on.
reviews.body: Even better than the last sonic care toothbrush I had. With this brush you get more brush Strokes per second, so you clean your teeth a lot better with less damage to the gums from a regular toothbrush. It has two speeds on it and a sensor for when pushing down too hard. Change the brush every 3 months and you're good to go. I got this because of a recommendation from my dentist.

reviews.title: Best Value for the $$
reviews.body: This is my 3rd Sonicare (22yrs a Believer). Great quality and this one's got some cool features like a sensor that tells you if you're applying too much pressure (!). I know this product has saved me from Periodontal Disease (the main reason my dentist strongly recommended it all those years ago). I come from a loving family that just happens to have terrible teeth- many many w/dentures @young ages (18 for Mom... !!) It took a few weeks to get used to way back, but I have never regretted starting w/Sonicare. Customer for Life. If I ever win the lotto, I'm getting a custom gold-plated one just kidding hahahaha.

reviews.title: Sonicare Toothbrush
reviews.body: This is a pretty good electric toothbrush. I wish that it had a few more settings, a gentle setting would be ideal. This works on 1 charge a week, that was the selling point for me!

reviews.title: Excellent electric brush 5000 over not needed
reviews.body: This is my 3rd electric brush I owned. I had oral-b and also had a sonicare from the older 5000 series. It not as powerful as 5000 series but it’s not necessary works perfect as is. It smaller compared to 5000 fits my mouth way better. Only has one button has multiple options. It also lighter than the old one.

reviews.title: Sonic with USB
reviews.body: I have always used Sonic. I tried Oral B and wasn't impressed so I went back to Sonic. The only problem is that the charger has a USB and now I have to buy an adapter to plug it into my bathroom WOW

reviews.title: good economical version if you dont need 10 different settings
reviews.body: Good, sealed, seems to have enough power, but a little less than my old one and somewhat smaller. might be great for travel.

reviews.title: Good product
reviews.body: Good toothbrush and kind. Let you know when you need to change your brush hold. It’s a good charge too.

reviews.title: Works well, is quiet and relatively gentle
reviews.body: I have used the Oral B electric toothbrush for years but decided I would try the Sonicare. They both clean well but the Sonicare is quieter and more gentle. The way you brush is a bit different and on the first try I wasn't sure I liked it, but after a few times to get used to it, I have to say I actually prefer the Sonicare to the OralB, though both clean really well. I do have my first dental cleaning after using the Sonicare for a few months so I will be curious to see if my dentist notices any difference in my teeth or gums.

reviews.title: Better than a regular toothbrush
reviews.body: I love Philips Sonicare toothbrushes. My teeth always feel cleaner with my electric toothbrush. My battery lasts 2+ weeks. The toothbrushes are easy to clean when you replace the heads.

reviews.title: Works very well!
reviews.body: I bought this for my husband as it was recommended by his dentist. It prevents him from brushing too hard. It is a good quality toothbrush. The charge lasts a long time. He is able to bring it on vacation on a single charge. His dentist has noticed the difference. It is durable.

reviews.title: Very Nice Electric Toothbrush
reviews.body: I’ve had one Sonicare for over 10 years & it finally started to have problems. The part that the brush sits on came loose, so I purchased a new 4100. The new brush is quieter & brushes very smoothly—for lack of a better word. Only one small issue is the handle is very smooth & it can slide out of your hand if you don’t keep a good grip especially when it’s wet. I think it could use some ridges/ribs on the handle to allow me a better grip. Other than that, I foresee us having a long term relationship. It’s a really good electric toothbrush.

reviews.title: USB Charger is a mistake and feels not as robust as past iterations
reviews.body: First off the charger being switched from outlet plug to USB is the most ridiculous thing I've ever seen. Who is charging their toothbrush via their computer or needing an adapter in the bathroom? If it ain't broke don't fix it - we just need a regular outlet plug, please! Thankfully I kept my old Philips charger after my last toothbrush broke, otherwise I'd be screwed. Secondly, this is noticeably lighter and smaller than my older Philips Sonicare. It doesn't seem to vibrate as powerfully at first but when you click the power button a second time you switch to the stronger mode which does seem to match closer to the old Sonicare toothbrushes, so this might be better if you have sensitive gums so you can use the softer mode. I've heard about comments that it's lighter than the older ones due to using a cheaper and more plastic filled motor, so we shall see what durability comes of this one after some usage. My last Sonicare survived years and years of twice-daily use. If Philips is trending towards "ensh*ttification" of such a great product then I will have to go with another brand. Bring back the stronger (and heavier) internals and make the charger work with outlet plugs instead of USB and this could be a 5-star product. For now we are officially in the Amazon Prime/Temu mid tier.

reviews.title: Wish it had more battery life
reviews.body: Just replacing my older Phillips Sonicare. 4 stars because I don't feel like it's a strong as the other one I had and the battery doesn't last as long. Just spend the extra money and get the model with the longer battery life.

reviews.title: Great toothbrush
reviews.body: This really makes brushing your teeth super easy. The battery lasts a long while and they give everything you need to get started right away. Would highly recommend.

reviews.title: Great Value
reviews.body: Great value for a Philips electric toothbrush. Quieter than my previous Phillips and holds a charge well. The slim handle fits nicely in the hand for easy brushing. My 4100 meets my needs without the extra bells and whistles. Will buy again when needed.

reviews.title: Good product
reviews.body: Good product

reviews.title: Dead after few weeks used, didn’t take charge! *New one was shipped.
reviews.body: *Updated: I contacted to Philips customer service for warranty. They verified purchase and shipped me another one. So yes, 1 star was changed to 4 stars. Hopefully this one winter have any issues. I bought it for Christmas gift to my sister. The first week used after full charge, it’s very good. Then unfortunately, after using few weeks it keeps alerting to charge. Despite charging 2 days, 3 days, even a week the battery still didn’t take any charge. Now it’s completely dead !! Such a disappointing thing. I have been using another version of Philips Sonicare for almost 10 years, now the battery starts going bad but the last 10 years it’s amazing. That’s why I bought this simple version for my sister as I trusted Philips. Turning out this one is dead after few weeks, I don’t know it’s still reliable like before. So so sad.

reviews.title: Good product. Durable
reviews.body: It works perfectly. I find it a bit too noisy but nothing much. Battery is durable. I charge it once a month. Very satisfied with the purchase

reviews.title: Phillips Sonicare 4100
reviews.body: Excellent electric tooth brush - The best in the market place - Much improved from previous models

reviews.title: A Good Toothbrush with a Design Flaw
reviews.body: I’m happy with this toothbrush. My dentist recommended this brand and recommended the sensitive brush heads. I’m always brushing my teeth when tired, often really tired. Early in the morning or last thing before bed. I do a much better job caring for my teeth with an electric toothbrush. It's a fraction of the effort. It took a little while to get the feel for it. I wanted to continue to actually brush with this toothbrush which you’re not supposed to do. Once I got the hang of it, it was all muscle memory! My one issue is that this toothbrush is the power button which collapses over time (about a year to a year and a half). As it collapses, it gets harder and harder to turn on the brush, to the point where I've had to use the tip of my finger to get at the button. And the cover on the button disintegrates. You can see in the picture the difference between the power button on a new toothbrush and one that’s been well used. I contacted Phillips and received a replacement. The same thing happened to the replacement, shown in the picture. It’s a waste because the toothbrush is otherwise functional.

reviews.title: Quality electric tooth and tongue brush
reviews.body: I like this toothbrush. It is quality from Philips Sonicare, easy and versatile to use. Great at getting in hard-to-reach places.

reviews.title: Great Toothbrush

reviews.title: Philips Sonicare 4100: Your Smile's New Best Friend – A Must-Have for Superior Clean!
reviews.body: As someone deeply invested in maintaining optimal oral health, I'm always on the lookout for products that genuinely deliver. After experiencing the Philips Sonicare 4100 Rechargeable Electric Toothbrush (Model HX3681/23), I can confidently say this isn't just another gadget; it's a game-changer for your daily brushing routine. With an impressive 4.4 out of 5 stars from over 46,000 reviews and being an Amazon's Choice with 50K+ units bought last month, it's clear I'm not alone in my enthusiasm! From the moment you unbox this sleek white device, you appreciate its ergonomic design and lightweight feel. But the real magic happens when you start brushing. Here's why the Philips Sonicare 4100 stands out and why it's a smart investment for your oral health: Unmatched Plaque Removal: Forget your manual toothbrush! This sonic powerhouse is clinically proven to remove up to 7 times more plaque. The 31,000 brush strokes per minute gently yet effectively break up and sweep away plaque, leaving your teeth feeling incredibly clean and smooth – a noticeable difference from day one. This directly addresses the core need for effective cleaning. Gentle on Gums, Smart on Pressure: One of my biggest concerns with electric toothbrushes has always been applying too much pressure. The Sonicare 4100 brilliantly solves this with its built-in pressure sensor. If you press too hard, it automatically warns you and reduces vibrations, protecting your gums from damage. This feature alone provides immense peace of mind and makes it ideal for sensitive gums. Master Your Brushing Technique: The SmarTimer and QuadPacer are invaluable. The SmarTimer ensures you brush for the dentist-recommended two minutes, while the QuadPacer guides you to brush each quadrant of your mouth equally. This takes the guesswork out of brushing and helps you develop consistently good habits. Long-Lasting Convenience: With a 14-day battery life on a single charge, this toothbrush is perfect for travel or for those who simply don't want to constantly think about recharging. The included USB charger makes it easy to power up wherever you are. Aesthetic Appeal: Beyond its performance, the sleek white design is a welcome addition to any bathroom countertop, blending seamlessly with modern aesthetics. The Verdict: The Philips Sonicare 4100 isn't just an upgrade; it's an essential tool for anyone serious about elevating their oral care routine. It combines advanced technology with user-friendly features, delivering a superior clean while protecting your gums. Considering its current 20% savings, bringing the price down to just $39.96 from $49.96, it offers exceptional value for such a high-performing device. If you're ready to experience a truly effective, gentle, and smart brushing experience, the Philips Sonicare 4100 is highly recommended. It's more than just a toothbrush; it's an investment in a healthier, brighter smile.

reviews.title: Good all round rechargeable toothbrush
reviews.body: Hard to get excited about a toothbrush… First question, Braun or Philips. They work differently but all reviews are evenly split so buy the brand you want. Stuff like this, I tend to buy the best basic version. I don’t need an app that tells me where I’ve missed some teeth. Likes 1. light and easy to hold, 2. battery condition light ( useful so it doesn’t die halfway through a cleaning), 3. a distinct beep after every 30 second quartile, 4. brush replacement warning light (there’s a chip in the head that counts. Maybe that’s why the heads cost more than Braun) Dislikes 1. charges with USB B. ( why not USB C - the new everywhere standard), 2. A charging stand. Why not plug a cord directly into the toothbrush ( that way I can throw it in my travel bag and use an existing cord). However I recognize that if you’re not not traveling a stand is maybe preferable 3. Expensive replacements heads ( but see likes) Finally - it works well, but it’s a toothbrush so that should be a given.

reviews.title: Much weaker action than 5xxx/6xxx/7xxx series
reviews.body: Given that sonicare toothbrushes never seem to last more than a year anymore, when my 5100 failed yet again, I figured I'd save some money and try the 4100. The 4100 is kind of a dud. It seems to have 2 speeds, low and high. The low speed barely vibrates the troothbrush head and is pretty much useless. The higher speed is a little bit better, but still no where near the action of the 5xxx/6xxx/7xxx toothbrushes. Going back to the 5xxx series as that seems to be a better value for a toothbrush that has the required cleaning power. Side note: I've been using sonicare brushes for at least the past 20 years, and while they work fantastic, the reliability has gone drastically downhill. I still love they way the clean, but really wish they lasted longer. Replacing them every 12-18 months is getting pretty ridiculous.

reviews.title: Affordable, Effective, and Gentle on Gums
reviews.body: The Philips Sonicare 4100 offers an excellent balance of performance and simplicity. It delivers up to 7x better plaque removal compared to manual brushing, thanks to its sonic technology and 31,000 brush strokes per minute. The built-in pressure sensor alerts you if you're brushing too hard, helping protect your gums. Amazon With two intensity settings and an EasyStart mode that gradually increases power over 14 uses, it's user-friendly for both newcomers and seasoned electric toothbrush users. Electric Teeth The SmarTimer and QuadPacer ensure you brush for the recommended two minutes, promoting consistent oral care habits. GearLab While it lacks extras like a travel case or charging dock, the 14-day battery life and straightforward design make it a solid choice for those seeking effective dental hygiene without unnecessary frills. GearLab Overall, the Philips Sonicare 4100 is a reliable, budget-friendly option that delivers quality brushing performance. One person found this helpful

reviews.title: I've run a battery of tests...
reviews.body: I've run a battery of tests, having had, for years, a 2x AA battery, MUCH larger, cousin. My objective was to see how well this did in terms of battery longevity, as replacing batteries in my previous (wonderful, mind you) set proved tiresome, particularly as they inevitably quit in the middle of a session. I'm not your typical user. Most run through one cycle (it - and my prior - have a timer which cuts off after their recommended interval. The presumption is that you'll run your brush over all your teeth, repetitively, and then quit. Instead, I do each surface (top and bottom, inside and out), as I still, at 80, have all my teeth, and want to be sure I keep them - and minimize the scraping needed in my regular dentist visits! So, I do a full cycle on each surface, and then, for good measure, as that's where the largest accumulations wind up, AND it's where I have enamel flaking off the two bottom center incisors, I do a second round on the inside lower surface. As to batteries, while they don't last as long as the pair of AAs, they DO last through many of the marathons I put the motor portion through, each night. Just for comfort, I place my unit in its recharger once each week for an overnight refresher. I've yet to have it shut down on me. At a fraction of the size, it's easy to hold and to store in my sink-side drawer. The only downside I can mention is that it's so small that occasionally I find that I don't have the brush properly aligned due to not having a large flat surface as a reference point as I did in my prior Phillips Sonicare. But for those who use theirs in the typical fashion, likely switching hands in the process (you don't have to be very ambidextrous to make this work in both hands!), likely it's of no issue to most. Very well done, and I very much like it even better than my previous dentist-recommended Sonicare!

reviews.body: Mad and Disappointed - Shrinkflation much?? I bought this same model toothbrush in 2020 and loved it. It’s about 30% smaller in size, feels like a lightweight plastic piece of junk, and 1/10th the power and weight of the older version! It doesn’t even feel like it’s doing anything, you have to brush as aggressively as you would with a manual/non-electric toothbrush to get any cleaning done. It’s sooo soft and powerless - it actually feels like the battery is dying. At first I thought it was lol And what is the USB charger for? Who has a USB port in their bathroom? Cheapskates. Suppose the vibration level of your toothbrush has suddenly reduced. In that case, it may be a sign that EasyStart has been activated. reviews.title: 14 April, 2023Verified purchase
reviews.body: Not very powerful I had to replace my Sonicare toothbrush that I had for three years after it broke and bought this model, which replaced my previous model. I thought that I would be getting the same quality that I had before with my previous model, but it has not been the case. The motor in this model is not as powerful as it was in the previous model I had before. I am also noticing that I have to charge this model a lot more frequently. This model also doesn't come with the plug outlet that goes into the socket, but if you reach out to them directly, they will provide you with one. This is so they can be more environmentally conscientious. I get that, so it wasn't a big deal for me, but something to be aware of in case this is a deal breaker for you. The only pro of this model from the other model is that it has two speed settings. The previous model only had one. However, even on the higher speed it still does not exude the same performance as the other model they had. My previous model made me feel like I came out of the dentist with each use while being gentle at the same time. Many times this model makes me feel like it's not cleaning like it should. I don't know why they changed the quality of the product with this model. Although I know this model is the more economic model of their brands, it's not worth the price you pay when you have to continue to buy the brush heads for a model that doesn't clean as it should. I do not recommend it. 1 reply from Philips team - 3 years ago Hello There. We’re sorry to hear that you're not happy with the performance of your Philips Sonicare 4100 Series toothbrush. If your toothbrush is vibrating less powerfully than before, the troubleshooting tips below may help you find a solution. A toothbrush without a fully charged battery may need more power to vibrate at its original strength. We recommend charging it overnight for at least 24 hours. Your toothbrush has the EasyStart feature activated by default. This feature causes your toothbrush to slowly increase its vibration level after each brushing session. The increase in vibration will occur over the first fourteen sessions. If you want your toothbrush to vibrate normally, we recommend turning off the EasyStart feature. It could also mean that your brush head is worn. We recommend replacing your brush head every three months. The intensity setting selected can impact the vibration level of your toothbrush. You can choose between different intensity levels. Your toothbrush will automatically select the intensity level based on the brush head you attach. We hope this helps. If you still need assistance, we’re available 24/7 on Facebook (@Philips) and Twitter. (@PhilipsCare) Alternatively, you can reach out to us via the contact page on our website. One of our colleagues will then try their best to help you further. We hope to speak to you soon. Kind regards, Philips Consumer Care Tearglass quality quality: 1 out of 5 value value: 2 out of 5 ease of use

reviews.body: We love it! I got this brush for both me and my 6 year old. I originally got her the kids oral-b brush but returned that after seeing the mold issues with that brush, because ew. I did some research to make sure it would be ok for her and it is. The brush has a sensitive mode that helps with the tickle feeling and it’s small enough for her and for me to clean around my “out of bite” wisdom teeth. The brush that comes with it is soft enough for both of us. It hasn’t caused either of us any irritation and leaves our teeth feeling very clean. I’d been using a regular manual brush before this and my mouth feels so much cleaner in the morning since switching to this. I like that there are different brush heads for different needs, that it’s rechargeable and the battery life is great, and I like the quadrant timer. Overall I’m impressed with this brush and recommend it. Oh, there is a variation in the color. reviews.title: 29 March, 2025
reviews.body: This is an awesome brush for people who have sensory issues and hate loud noises, cause this thing is quiet I didn’t realize how off my old toothbrush felt until I used the Sonicare 4100. This one’s on a completely different level. You can feel it working without it being overwhelming. The vibrations are strong but controlled, and the brushing motion doesn’t trigger any sensory irritation. My teeth feel smooth, almost polished, even behind the back molars where most brushes usually miss. The grip feels secure without being bulky, and the texture of the handle doesn’t slip when wet. I also like that I can use the same brush head for my tongue. It’s efficient and doesn’t have any weird textures or need for switching. Battery life is excellent, and the charger doesn’t take up space or flash aggressively. No pointless features. Just clean, simple, and surprisingly satisfying to use. Definitely a big step up from what I was using before. Noisyonion ease of use ease of use: 5 out of 5 quality quality: 5 out of 5 value

reviews.body: USB Charger ONLY - Very disappointed I bought a new one as it was time and was very disappointed that you did not include a new wall plug, instead it's USB to charge. I don't keep a USB block in my bathroom, why would you do this??? 1 reply from Philips Consumer Care team - 1 year ago Hi there, Thank you for writing your review. We are sorry to hear that you are not satisfied with our decision to not provide a charger with your Philips Sonicare 4100 Series Toothbrush. At Philips, we are committed to reducing plastic waste. As part of our environmental efforts, some Philips products no longer come with a USB power adapter. While this may seem inconvenient, we assure you that it is because most consumers already own one or more USB wall adapters. We will continue supplying USB charging cables with the product that can be used with a USB power adapter. If you already own a USB power adapter, please ensure that it is certified and meets safety standards, ideally from a trustworthy brand. The safety standards for safe charging are as follows: Input voltage: 100-240V; Output voltage: 5V; Power output: 1A or higher. If you want to use or charge your product in a moist environment, such as in the bathroom, always use a splash-proof (IPX4) rated adapter. If this does not help or if you need any further assistance, we kindly ask you to reach out to us via social media. We are available on Facebook (@Philips) and X (@PhilipsCare). We are very sorry to hear about the issues you are experiencing with our toothbrush, and would like to see how we can help. We were wondering if you have already disabled the easy-start feature? The easy-start feature enables you to increase the vibrations over time to get used to electric brushing. We kindly ask you to disable the easy-start feature to increase the vibrations. Easy start is disabled by holding the mode/intensity button and by pressing the power button once while on the charger. The handle will beep once to confirm it is disabled. reviews.body: Annoying to charge I bought this on 5/23/2023 and it’s only 9/2/2023 and I’m already having issues getting it to charge. Also I don’t understand why these new models don’t come with a regular plug. I have to use my own cell phone charger brick to charge this. It will say it’s charging but I plug it in for well over 7 hours and it already needs to charge again a few days later. I laughed when I pulled this page up to check the warranty and saw it say it’s supposed to last 2 weeks on a single charge. The toothbrush itself works fine so maybe mine’s defective. I never had problems charging my old Philips toothbrush. 1 reply from Philips team - 3 years ago Hi Alicia, Thank you sharing your feedback with us. We are sorry to hear that your Sonicare is not charging. If your toothbrush is not charging, the troubleshooting tips below may help you find a solution. Does your toothbrush not charge when it is plugged in? Your outlet might not work. Try using a different outlet to charge it. Try checking if your charging adapter or cord is damaged. If you notice any damage to your adapter or cord, stop using it immediately. If you use a charging stand, pad, or glass, ensure that your toothbrush is placed centrally on the charger. The toothbrush will release two short beeps, or the battery light indicator will light up, confirming that the toothbrush is placed on the charger correctly. Ensure the charger is not placed on a metal surface or near other chargers, as this may cause interference while charging. If you have any further questions, please feel free to get in touch via Social Media. We're available 24/7 on Facebook and Twitter. reviews.title: 24 February, 2025Verified purchase
reviews.body: Weak, warranty sucks & no free brush head with registration I am in the dental field & until 2023 always purchased the "dental staff brush & extended warranty." The same thing always happens, it sounds like something is broken inside the brush handle & it makes lots of noise. After one year I returned the brush under warranty for the same issue. Less than one year later the replacement brush had the same problem & I attempted to return it since it was under a "two year warranty." I was told they wouldn't honor the warranty "because then we'd just be giving away brushes all day long." I asked why the brushes always seem to have the same problem & maybe if they were able to address the issue they wouldn't have returns. I said I would no longer recommend the sonicare brush to my patients & if I did I would warn them about the shoddy warranty. I bought a basement level sonicare from Target in 2023 & in 2025 it started that loud noise so I purchased another from Target. I expect this one to last about a year at which time I will purchase another from Target. No matter how much spent on a Sonicare brush, expect the brush to last less than two years. Sonicare will honor the warranty the first time (& take their sweet time getting the brush to you) but not on the replacement brush. I guarantee the same issue will be the loud rattling in the handle rendering the brush useless. Karla Mojezati ease of use ease of use: 5 out of 5 quality quality: 1 out of 5 value

reviews.title: 24 April, 2023
reviews.body: Not sustainable Not sustainable. The only way to access the battery to replace it is to smash it with a hammer as per their own instructions. Don't put some sort of green washing sticker on the front about energy use when your own user manual says to just smash it and toss in the landfill when the battery goes. 1 reply from Philips team - 3 years ago Hi Abonnema, Thank you for contacting us. We're to hear about your experience with your Sonicare. We would like to inform you that you cannot replace the integrated battery of your toothbrush. If you want to discard your toothbrush, we recommend removing and recycling the battery. Do not throw away the product with the normal household waste at the end of its life, but hand it in at an official collection point for recycling. By doing this, you help to preserve the environment. Sonicare contains a built-in rechargeable battery which shall not be disposed of with normal household waste. Please take your product to an official collection point or a Philips service center to have a professional remove the rechargeable battery. We hope this helps. If you have any further questions, please feel free to get in touch via social media. We're available 24/7 on Facebook and Twitter. reviews.title: 14 September, 2023
reviews.body: Unfortunately great until a year later Made the switch from a normal toothbrush and loved it. Really easy to use, feels squeaky clean after the full 2 minute timer. Unfortunately it’s now been a little over a year and it seems the inside mechanism is falling apart/leaking brown and black residue that stains my sink ceramic. It looks like rusty water or black oily sediment comes out of it when I turn it upside down or lay it sideways after brushing. I really wanted to like it and use it for a long time but it seems like my unit wasn’t really made too well. The problem started when I noticed a louder noise from the previously quiet motor; the metal tip started wiggling a little and you could feel the inside pieces wiggle with the tip, producing a ridiculously loud rattle during usage. Overall, I would be weary of spending on another sonicare considering it really only properly laster 14 months:( 1 reply from Philips Consumer Care team - 3 years ago Hi there, Thank you for your feedback. We're sorry to hear about the issue that you are facing with your Philips Sonicare 4100 Series toothbrush. In this case, we would request you to reach out to us via social media. This feature causes your Toothbrush to increase its vibration level slowly after each brushing session. The increase in vibration will occur over the first fourteen sessions. Turn off your Toothbrush and press the mode/intensity button to choose the preferred mode. We hope this will improve your experience. reviews.body: does not have intensity , [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] seems like just making noise in the mouth not cleaning teeth, not enough power , I had different model before it was good of course it was expensive. 1 reply from Philips Consumer Care team - 4 years ago Hi, Thanks for your review regarding our Sonicare 4100 Series Toothbrush. We are sorry to hear that your new toothbrush is not as powerful as expected, and we hope we can improve your next brushing session! Kindly make sure that the handle has been fully charged for 24 hours with no interruptions. Check that the charger is not placed on a metal surface, or close to other chargers as this can interfere with the charging process. If the issue persists, check if the EasyStart feature is still active. The EasyStart feature gently increases the power over the first 14 brushings to help you get used to the brushing with the Philips Sonicare. You can follow these steps to deactivate the feature: 1: Put the handle on the plugged-in charger. 2: Press and hold the power on/off button while the handle remains on the charger. 3: Keep the power on/off button pressed until you hear a single short beep (after 3 seconds). 4: Release the power on/off button- - Triple tone of low-med-high means the EasyStart feature has been activated. The battery level indicator light will also blink green 2 times to confirm activation. - Triple tone of high-med-low means the EasyStart feature has been deactivated. The battery level indicator light will blink amber 1 time to confirm deactivation. If you have any further questions, please feel free to get in touch via social media. We’re available 24/7 on Facebook and Twitter. Alternatively, you can reach out to us via the contact page on our website www.philips.com/support. reviews.title: 25 January, 2024Verified purchase
reviews.body: New and Not Improved. I’ve been using this series of toothbrush for a long time. I’ve had two already that both lasted me for several years, which is the best you can ask for with the cheaper end electric toothbrush. However, I have purchased two of these new editions (returning the first assuming it was a fluke) and they are generally terrible. Within a few uses, they have inconsistent vibrations that make crazy sounds and are much louder than the old versions. I am very familiar with the different vibration levels and the pressure sensitivity sound and the sounds my brush is making has nothing to do with them. It’s also an issue that seems to stem from the connection on the brush head and only presents itself when in use. reviews.title: 18 April, 2023Verified purchase
reviews.body: For The Thorough Review Readers! I read so many of these reviews before I committed to a new electric toothbrush! I had an Sonicare Essence from about 5-6 years ago and this was replacing that. Very relieved that I LOVE this toothbrush. The toothbrush head is a bit smaller than the older Sonicares so it fits in more little mouth spots. It also blips every 30 seconds of tooth brushing so you know when to move to another area of your mouth. SO NICE. My teeth truly feel dentist-y clean. I can't speak to the battery yet truly, I've had it for about a week but so far so good. The intensity is also definitely different than the Essence but not weaker by any means. Maybe we're just used to rocking our teeth and these newer brushes don't need as much pressure/vibrations. All in all, I'm glad I got this and for the price it's worth it. These bad reviews are being very specific. =) Melly quality quality: 5 out of 5 value value: 5 out of 5 ease of use

reviews.title: 1 March, 2026
reviews.body: Good brush, misleading expired promo [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I recently purchased the Sonic electric toothbrush, and overall, it’s a decent electric toothbrush. It feels well made, has good cleaning power, quiet, and leaves my teeth feeling noticeably cleaner compared to a manual brush. However, I was disappointed with the promotional offer included inside the packaging. There was a leaflet advertising a free replacement brush head if you register the product. That sounded like a nice added bonus and definitely influenced my impression of the purchase. Unfortunately, when I went online to register, I was told that the offer was no longer available. It’s frustrating to see promotional material inside a brand-new product box, only to find out the offer has already expired. If the promotion has ended, the packaging should be updated accordingly. It feels misleading and left a sour note on what would otherwise have been a straightforward purchase. In summary, the toothbrush itself performs well and does what it’s supposed to do, but the outdated promotional offer was disappointing. Because of that experience, I’m giving it

reviews.title: 13 December, 2024
reviews.body: A Budget-Friendly Option with Basic Functionality [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] The Philips Sonic 4100 Series HX3681 is a straightforward electric toothbrush that offers a gentle cleaning experience. It's a good choice for those with sensitive gums or dental work, as the two-speed setting allows for a customized clean. Pros: * Affordable: It's a budget-friendly option that provides basic sonic cleaning. * Gentle: The low-speed setting is particularly gentle, making it suitable for those with sensitive gums or dental work. * Long Battery Life: The battery life is impressive, lasting around 3 weeks on a single charge. Cons: * Limited Cleaning Power: The brush and speed settings may struggle to remove stubborn stains, such as tea stains. * Slow Charging: Charging the toothbrush takes many hours, which can be inconvenient. Overall: The Philips Sonic 4100 Series HX3681 is a decent option for those who prioritize a gentle cleaning experience and a long-lasting battery. However, if you're looking for a toothbrush that can tackle stubborn stains and has a faster charging time, you may want to consider a higher-end model.

Some models also have an EasyStart feature that slowly increases the vibration level. During the first 14 brushing sessions, this feature gradually increases the vibration. reviews.title: 5 August, 2023
reviews.body: It’s great until it’s not!!! [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I’ve had my toothbrush for several months and even though I’ve swapped the head for it a few times it still turns off randomly in the middle of me brushing. Is there a reason for that? And I don’t mean the 2 minute timer that’s built into it either. 1 reply from Philips Consumer Care team - 3 years ago Hi there, Thank you for your feedback. We're sorry to hear that your Philips Sonicare 4100 Series toothbrush stops randomly. If your toothbrush turns off by itself, the troubleshooting tips below may help you find a solution. The selected brushing mode defines the brushing time. Your toothbrush will turn off automatically when the recommended time is up. We recommend changing the brushing mode. Brushing mode times can range from 20 seconds to 3.5 minutes. The QuadPacer is an interval timer that has a short beep and pause to remind you to brush the four sections of your mouth evenly and thoroughly. This Philips Sonicare toothbrush comes with the QuadPacer feature activated. The SmarTimer indicates that your brushing cycle is complete by automatically turning off. Your toothbrush measures the pressure you apply while brushing. If you press too hard, it briefly stops, indicating that you are using too much pressure. If these steps do not help or if you have any additional questions, please get in touch via Social Media. We’re available on Facebook (@Philips) and Twitter (@PhilipsCare). Don’t worry, if you don’t use Social Media, you can also reach out to us via the contact page: www.Philips.com/contact. Hope to speak to you soon. Kind regards, Philips Consumer Care

We will continue supplying USB charging cables with the product that can be used with a USB power adapter. If you already own a USB power adapter, make sure that the power adapter is certified and meets safety standards, ideally from a trustworthy brand. See the safety standards for safe charging below: Input voltage: 100-240V. Output voltage: 5V. Power output: 1A or higher. If you want to use or charge your product in a moist environment, for example, in the bathroom, always use a splash-proof (IPX4) rated adapter. If you do not own one than we recommend a splash-proof (IPX4) adapter. You can get this adapter in the Philips online store or any other retailer. Look for "IPX4" text or symbols on the adapter. reviews.title: 2 April, 2025
reviews.body: A few years in and a screw came loose I had this for 2 or 3 years before it stopped vibrating with any intensity. Kind of happened over night. I looked up this issue (knowing that I was well past my warranty) and found that an internal screw (there's literally just one) has a common tendency to come loose. It seems like this should be a very easy fix - open the shell of the toothbrush and tighten said screw. Well, it appears that Sonicare designed their toothbrush to not be user-friendly, in the repair field. First you have to have a vice grip or locking pliers to squeeze the base of the toothbrush until you have a small gap to pry the bottom off. This process feels like you're going to break everything. Then you have to forcefully jam the toothbrush end against something sturdy until it pops outout of the casing. This also feels like you will break a lot of things. I thought I could get inside part out with some small pliers - instead I broke the copper coil holder (a very brittle plastic) that is necessary for the device to have any charging function. I fixed the small issue with the screw - but this toothbrush is officially on its last charge - less I want to do some delicate soldering work. This would be a 5-star product. However it is clear to me that it is designed to fail and when it fails it is designed to not be easy to repair - even though the most common fail-point is simply one screw - and that one screw is something that doesn't even need to be a screw. The screw could have been replaced with something more secure that wouldn't eventually wiggle loose. The screw could have been glued in place so that it couldn't come loose... many things could have fixed this design flaw. However, I am convinced that this company wants their products to break and not be repaired. Agent031693 ease of use ease of use: 4 out of 5 quality quality: 1 out of 5 value

Just awful, do not buy!! 1 reply from Philips team - 3 years ago Hi Lolo, thanks for your review. We are sorry to hear that your Sonicare 4100 is not providing you with enough power to clean your teeth effectively and hope we can help! Firstly, make sure that the handle has been fully charged for 24 hours with no interruptions. If the issue persists after the full charge, you can check if the Easy Start feature is still active. The EasyStart feature gently increases the power over the first 14 brushings to help you get used to the brushing with the Philips Sonicare. To deactivate the feature: 1: Put the handle on the plugged-in charger. 2: Press and hold the power on/off button while the handle remains on the charger. 3: Keep the power on/off button pressed until you hear a single short beep (after 3 seconds). 4: Release the power on/off button. - Triple tone of low-med-high means the EasyStart feature has been activated. The battery level indicator light will also blink green 2 times to confirm activation and a triple tone of high-med-low means the EasyStart feature has been deactivated. The battery level indicator light will blink amber 1 time to confirm deactivation. We hope these suggestions help! If you have any further questions, please feel free to get in touch via social media. We’re available 24/7 on Facebook and Twitter. Alternatively, you can reach out to us via the contact page on our website www.philips.com/support. One of our colleagues will then try their best to help you further. Hope to speak to you soon! reviews.title: 30 December, 2021
reviews.body: Excellent but no power adapter included [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] Overall, this is an excellent electric toothbrush from an extremely reputable company. It gradually increases the vibration over the first 14 days, which is a great feature for those who are new to electric toothbrushes. The device is lightweight but feels solid in your hand. My dentist recommended an electric toothbrush for me years ago because I was brushing too hard, and it was causing damage to my gums. This brush makes it super easy to brush softly and let the brush do most of the work. It will even vibrate and beep as a warning if you happen to brush too hard. The 2-minute timer is perfect for me, as otherwise I tend to brush too quickly. The QuadPacer feature is actually very helpful and lets you know when it's time to move to the next section of your mouth. My only real complaint is that it does not come with a USB power adapter, so you have to buy one or find one laying around your house. It seems like a silly thing for Philips to be cheap on, especially since it's designed to just leave on the charger. A matching white adapter should be included. I also wish that it didn't automatically turn off after the 2-minutes are up. Sometimes I feel like I'm not quite done and would like to brush a little longer, but it shuts off automatically. It comes with one standard brush head in the package. Sonicare makes several types of brush heads, so I am looking forward to trying some of their other more specialized heads when I re-order more. Overall, this is a must have for good oral health and I wouldn't live without it. If you use this twice daily, along with flossing and a non-alcoholic mouthwash, you will be amazed at how good your oral health can be. I am no longer worried about visiting the dentist. I get easy check-ups every 6 months and it can save you a ton of money on dental bills in the long run!

reviews.title: 13 January, 2022
reviews.body: Amazing toothbrush [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] The most amazing toothbrush. It’s leaps and bounds above a manual toothbrush in every way. It removes the food and particles while massaging your gums and actually feels good while you brush. The smooth vibration is great for cleaning and really getting into the tiny places. I love that you can change out the brush heads so easily and the ease of charging as well. It’s also a slim toothbrush and charging station, which I love, as you can leave it out on the counter if you wish. Five stars; highly recommended.

reviews.body: Does not come with adapter This review isn’t so much for the toothbrush itself. It works, it cleans my teeth. I do feel like it vibrates a bit more than my previous Philips toothbrush, which I don’t love for my very sensitive teeth. It’s not terrible though. My issue is that this toothbrush doesn’t come with an adapter. There’s a card in the box saying go to the website and you can order one. Scoured the website, no link anywhere to order a charger. Spoke to customer service, which I found to be a hassle, felt like I had to provide my whole life history, and finally was told that they’ll send one. Now I got an email that they don’t have it in stock and will send a travel charger if I want it. So, another email, and they needed my address again- not sure where that info went. Way too much of a hassle just to be able to use a toothbrush. I respect the fact that Philips doesn’t provide adapters for environmental reasons, but there should be a simple process to get one.

reviews.title: 16 December, 2022Verified purchase
reviews.body: No adapter included. Can't charge! I’ve been using Sonicare for years and have been pleased. I paid $40 for a toothbrush (3000/4000 series) that I can’t charge because it comes with no adapter, just a USB cord. Sorry, but I am not interested in charging my toothbrush at my office desk. An insert in the box reads “If you require an adapter, please contact us.” Good luck with that. I couldn’t find any help in the support FAQs, and support hours are limited. The chat link wouldn’t work. The package insert claims the reason an adapter is not included is because “Philips is committed to sustainability” and that not including an adapter “prevents the equivalent of over 19 million plastic bottles reaching landfills.” Another company using the “sustainability” excuse to be cheap. Good luck sustaining your customer base. I’ll try Oral B based on the suggestions in their official web site reviews. DStan value

The Quadpacer is an interval timer that has a short beep and pause to remind you to brush the 4 sections of your mouth. Depending on the brushing mode you have selected, the Quadpacer beeps at different intervals during the brushing cycle. The Quadpacer works in all 3 modes and at all 3 intensity levels. To activate this feature, place the handle with a brush head attached on the plugged-in charger or sanitizer. Press and hold the mode button for 2 seconds., You hear 1 beep to indicate the Quadpacer has been deactivated.You hear 2 beeps to indicate the Quadpacer has been activated. We’re sorry to hear that your Philips Sonicare 4100 toothbrush is running slow. We request you to try the following troubleshooting steps to help resolve the issue: If the battery is not fully charged, the vibrating action of the toothbrush may require extra power. It should be charged for at least 24 hours. Your toothbrush has an EasyStart feature. This feature causes your toothbrush to increase its vibration level slowly after each brushing session. We recommend turning it off. It could also mean that your brush head is worn. We recommend replacing your brush head every three months. Your toothbrush gives you the option to choose between low and high intensity. While brushing, you can change the intensity by pressing the mode/intensity button. Adjusting the intensity is not possible when the handle is turned off. We hope this will help. If you still require assistance, please feel free to contact us via social media. reviews.body: Never going back to a manual brush... [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] Wow. I've always been told by my dentist to consider switching to electric toothbrush, and I am never going back. I think quality and price is second to none, and if you need to get the job done this is your toothbrush. reviews.title: 5 April, 2023
reviews.body: Upgrade Your Dental Health with the Phillips 4100! [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] As someone who has always been a bit skeptical about electric toothbrushes, I have to say that the Phillips Model 4100 has completely changed my mind. This toothbrush has truly revolutionized my dental hygiene routine and made me excited to brush my teeth twice a day. One of the things I love most about the Phillips Model 4100 is how easy it is to use. The brush itself is lightweight and comfortable to hold, and the brush head easily clicks on and off for easy replacement. Plus, the toothbrush comes with a rechargeable battery that lasts for weeks on a single charge, so you never have to worry about running out of power. But beyond the convenience factor, this toothbrush also delivers amazing results. The brush head uses advanced sonic technology to clean your teeth and gums, removing plaque and leaving your mouth feeling fresh. I've noticed a significant improvement in the overall cleanliness and health of my teeth since I started using this toothbrush. Another excellent feature of the Phillips Model 4100 is the built-in timer that helps ensure you are brushing for the recommended two minutes. Plus, the toothbrush has a pressure sensor that alerts you if you are brushing too hard, which is a helpful feature for those with sensitive teeth. Overall, I would highly recommend the Phillips 4100 to anyone looking to take their dental hygiene routine to the next level. This toothbrush is easy to use, delivers great results, and is sure to leave you with a brighter, healthier smile.

reviews.title: 8 October, 2022
reviews.body: More compact but short battery life [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] I've had many previous Sonicare toothbrushes in the past and have been thrilled about them. My last one was the older 4100 model (slightly bigger in size). I received this 4100 as a warranty replacement. Initially I was amazed at how small the body has become. It came with a USB charger instead of AC powered. The annoying part is that you have to provide your own brick. That is just an annoyance. The real negative part is that now it only lasts FOUR days instead of 2 weeks, like my old model 4100 and what the new 4100 manual says. My usage and charge pattern is IDENTICAL to how I used my old model 4100. As an Engineer I was fearing this as a smaller size battery usually means a smaller capacity. And it looks like my fear came true. However, it does the job in cleaning my teeth, as usual. 1 reply from Philips Consumer Care team - 4 years ago Hi, Thanks for your review regarding our Sonicare 4100 Toothbrush. We appreciate your feedback, and can understand that sometimes switching to a different system can take some time to get used to. By switching to a USB charger in our Philips Sonicare 1000-4000 Series alone, we can use up to 189,210 kg less plastic every 4 years. It's great to hear that your new Sonicare is cleaning your teeth as expected, what a shame that the battery is not lasting more than four days. This model should also provide you with 14 days of brushing time, kindly fully charge the battery for 24 hours with no interruptions and try again. If you have any further questions, please feel free to get in touch via social media. We’re available 24/7 on Facebook and Twitter. reviews.body: Doesn't vibrate enough [This review was collected as part of a promotion.] The toothbrush has two modes: slow and fast. the problem is the fast mode is also slow. i just use it as a plain tooth brush 1 reply from Philips Consumer Care team - 3 years ago Hi there, Thank you for your feedback. We're sorry to hear that your Philips Sonicare 4100 toothbrush is vibrating as expected. If your toothbrush is vibrating less powerfully than before, the troubleshooting tips below may help you find a solution. A toothbrush without a fully charged battery may need more power to vibrate at its original strength. We recommend charging it overnight for at least 24 hours. The chargers for all Sonicare toothbrushes are different and not compatible with other Sonicare models. We recommend charging the toothbrush using the original charger that came with it. Your toothbrush has the EasyStart feature. This feature causes your toothbrush to increase its vibration level slowly after each brushing session. The increase in vibration will occur over the first fourteen sessions. If you want your toothbrush to vibrate normally, we recommend turning off the EasyStart feature. It could also mean that your brush head is worn. We recommend replacing your brush head every three months. You can purchase a new brush head in our online store. If this does not help or if you have any further questions, please feel free to get in touch via social media. We’re available on Facebook (@Philips) and Twitter (@PhilipsCare).
